Exodus12:17 You must celebrate the Festival of Unleavened Bread because it was on this very day that I brought you out of Egypt in organized family groups. This is a permanent law for future generations: You must celebrate this day.
Exodus12:18 From the evening of the fourteenth day of the first month until the evening of the twenty-first day you must eat unleavened bread.
Exodus12:19 There should be no yeast in your houses for seven days. Whoever eats anything with yeast in it must be excluded from the community of Israel

Exodus22:20 Whoever sacrifices to any god except the Lord must be condemned and destroyed.
Exodus22:21 Never mistreat or oppress foreigners
Exodus22:22 Never take advantage of any widow or orphan.
Exodus22:23 If you do and they cry out to me
Exodus22:24 I will become angry and have you killed in combat. Then your wives and children will become widows and orphans.
Exodus22:25 If you lend money to my people—to any poor person among you—never act like a moneylender. Charge no interest.
Exodus22:26 If you take any of your neighbor's clothes as collateral
Exodus22:27 It may be the only clothes he has to cover his body. What else will he sleep in? When he cries out to me
Exodus22:28 Never show disrespect for God or curse a leader of your people.
Exodus22:29 Never withhold your best wine from me. You must give me your firstborn son.
Exodus22:30 You must do the same with your cattle and your sheep. They will stay with their mothers seven days
Exodus22:31 You must be my holy people. Never eat the meat of an animal that has been killed by wild animals out in the countryside. Throw it to the dogs.
Exodus23:1 {The Lord continued
Exodus23:2 Never follow a crowd in doing wrong. When you testify in court
Exodus23:3 Never give special favors to poor people in court.
Exodus23:4 Whenever you come across your enemy's ox or donkey wandering loose
Exodus23:5 Whenever you see that the donkey of someone who hates you has collapsed under its load
Exodus23:6 Never deny justice to poor people in court.
Exodus23:7 Avoid telling lies. Don't kill innocent or honest people
Exodus23:8 Never take a bribe
Exodus23:9 Never oppress foreigners. You know what it's like to be foreigners because you were foreigners living in Egypt.
Exodus23:10 For six years you may plant crops in your fields and harvest them
Exodus23:11 but in the seventh year you must leave the land unplowed and unused. In that way the poor among your people will have food to eat
Exodus23:12 For six days you will do your work
Exodus23:13 Be careful {to do} everything I told you. Never mention the names of other gods or let them be heard on your lips.
Exodus23:14 Three times a year you must celebrate a pilgrimage festival in my honor.
Exodus23:15 Celebrate the Festival of Unleavened Bread: For seven days you must eat unleavened bread
Exodus23:16 Celebrate the Festival of the Harvest with the first produce harvested from whatever you plant in your fields. Celebrate the Festival of the Final Harvest at the end of the year when you harvest your crops from the fields.
Exodus23:17 These are the three times each year that all your men must come into the presence of the Master
Exodus23:18 Never offer the blood of a sacrifice to me at the same time you offer anything containing yeast. The fat sacrificed at my festivals should never be left over in the morning.
Exodus23:19 You must bring the best of the first produce harvested from your soil to the house of the Lord your God. Never cook a young goat in its mother's milk.
Exodus23:20 I'm going to send a Messenger in front of you to protect you on your trip and bring you to the place I have prepared.
Exodus23:21 Pay attention to him
Exodus23:22 But if you will listen to him and do everything I say
Exodus23:23 My Messenger will go ahead of you and will bring you to {the land of} the Amorites
Exodus23:24 Never worship or serve their gods or follow their practices. Instead
Exodus23:25 You must serve the Lord your God
Exodus23:26 No woman in your land will miscarry or be unable to have children. I will let you live a normal life span.
Exodus23:27 I will send my terror ahead of you and throw any nation you meet into a panic. I will make all your enemies flee from you.
Exodus23:28 I will spread panic ahead of you to force the Hivites
Exodus23:29 I will not force them out of your way in one year. Otherwise
Exodus23:30 Little by little I will force them out of your way until you have increased enough in number to take possession of the land.
Exodus23:31 I will establish your borders from the Red Sea to the Mediterranean Sea and from the Sinai Desert to the Euphrates River. I will put the people living in the land under your control
Exodus23:32 Never make a treaty with them and their gods.
Exodus23:33 Never let them live in your land

Exodus29:45 Then I will live among the Israelites and be their God.
Exodus29:46 They will know that I am the Lord their God. I brought them out of Egypt so that I might live among them. I am the Lord their God.

Exodus34:16 Then your sons will end up marrying their daughters. When their daughters chase after their gods as though they were prostitutes
Exodus34:17 Never make an idol.
Exodus34:18 You must celebrate the Festival of Unleavened Bread. As I commanded you
Exodus34:19 Every first male offspring is mine
Exodus34:20 It will cost you a sheep or a goat to buy back the firstborn donkey. If you don't buy it back
Exodus34:21 You may work six days
Exodus34:22 You must celebrate the Festival of Weeks with the first grain from your wheat harvest
Exodus34:23 Three times a year all your men must come into the presence of the Master
Exodus34:24 I will force nations out of your way and will expand {your country's} borders. No one will want to take away your land while you're gone three times a year to the Lord's festivals.
Exodus34:25 Never offer the blood of a sacrifice to me at the same time you offer anything containing yeast. No part of the sacrifice at the Passover festival should be left over in the morning.
Exodus34:26 You must bring the first and best of the produce harvested from your soil to the house of the Lord your God. Never cook a young goat in its mother's milk.
Exodus34:27 Then the Lord said to Moses
Exodus34:28 Moses was there with the Lord 40 days and 40 nights without food or water. He wrote on the tablets the words of the promise
Exodus34:29 Moses came down from Mount Sinai
Exodus34:30 When Aaron and all the Israelites looked at Moses and saw his face shining
Exodus34:31 Moses called to them
Exodus34:32 After that
Exodus34:33 When Moses finished speaking to them
Exodus34:34 But whenever Moses went into the Lord's presence to speak with him
Exodus34:35 they would see that Moses' face was shining. Then Moses would put the veil back on until he went in again to speak with the Lord.
Exodus35:1 Moses assembled the whole Israelite community and said to them
Exodus35:2 You may work for six days
Exodus35:3 Never light a fire in any of your homes on this day of worship.
Exodus35:4 Then Moses said to the whole Israelite community
Exodus35:5 Choose something of your own to give as a special contribution to the Lord. Let everyone who is willing bring this kind of contribution to the Lord: gold
Exodus35:6 violet
Exodus35:7 rams' skins dyed red
Exodus35:8 olive oil for the lamps

Exodus35:22 All who were willing—men and women alike—came and brought all kinds of gold jewelry: pins
Exodus35:23 Those who had violet
Exodus35:24 Those who could give silver or bronze brought it as their contribution to the Lord. Those who had acacia wood that could be used in the construction brought it.

Exodus35:26 All the women who were willing and had the skill spun the goats' hair.

Exodus35:28 They also brought the spices and the olive oil for the lamps
Exodus35:29 Every Israelite man and woman who was willing brought all these items to the Lord as a freewill offering. They brought these items to be used to make everything the Lord had commanded through Moses.

Exodus35:31 The Lord has filled Bezalel with the Spirit of God
Exodus35:32 He's a master artist familiar with gold
Exodus35:33 He knows how to cut and set stones and how to work with wood. He's an expert in all trades.
Exodus35:34 Also
Exodus35:35 The Lord has made these men highly skilled in all trades. They can do the work of jewelers
Exodus36:1 {Moses continued
Exodus36:2 Moses called Bezalel and Oholiab and every other craftsman to whom the Lord had given these skills and who was willing to come and do the work.
Exodus36:3 Moses turned over to them all the contributions the Israelites had brought for the work of constructing the holy place. But the people still kept bringing him freewill offerings every morning.
Exodus36:4 Finally
Exodus36:5 They said
Exodus36:6 So Moses gave instructions to have the following message announced all over camp: No man or woman needs to make anything more to give as their special contribution to the holy place. Then the people stopped bringing gifts.
Exodus36:7 The material they had was more than enough to do the job.
Exodus36:8 All the skilled craftsmen among the workers made the inner tent with ten sheets made from fine linen yarn and violet
Exodus36:9 Each sheet was 42 feet long and 6 feet wide—all the same size.
Exodus36:10 Five of the sheets were sewn together
Exodus36:11 Then they made 50 violet loops along the edge of the end sheet in each set
Exodus36:12 placing the loops opposite each other.
Exodus36:13 They also made 50 gold fasteners. They used them to link the {two sets of} sheets together so that the inner tent was a single unit.
Exodus36:14 They made 11 sheets of goats' hair to form an outer tent over the inner tent.
Exodus36:15 Each of the 11 sheets was 45 feet long and 6 feet wide.
Exodus36:16 Five of the sheets were sewn together into one set
Exodus36:17 Then they made 50 loops along the edge of the end sheet in each set.
Exodus36:18 They also made 50 bronze fasteners to link the inner tent together as a single unit.
Exodus36:19 They made a cover out of rams' skins that had been dyed red for the outer tent
Exodus36:20 They made a framework out of acacia wood for the inner tent.
Exodus36:21 Each frame was 15 feet long and 27 inches wide
Exodus36:22 with two identical pegs. They made all the frames for the inner tent this same way.
Exodus36:23 They made 20 frames for the south side of the inner tent.
Exodus36:24 Then they made 40 silver sockets at the bottom of the 20 frames
Exodus36:25 For the north side of the inner tent {they made} 20 frames
Exodus36:26 and 40 silver sockets
Exodus36:27 They made six frames for the far end
Exodus36:28 They made two frames for {each of} the corners at the far end of the inner tent.
Exodus36:29 They were held together at the bottom and held tightly at the top by a single ring. Both corner frames were made this way.
Exodus36:30 There were eight frames with 16 silver sockets
Exodus36:31 They also made crossbars out of acacia wood. Five were for the frames on one side of the inner tent
Exodus36:32 five were for those on the other side
Exodus36:33 They made the middle crossbar so that it ran from one end to the other
Exodus36:34 They covered the frames with gold and made gold rings to hold the crossbars. They also covered the crossbars with gold.
Exodus36:35 They made the canopy out of violet
Exodus36:36 They made four posts of acacia wood for it and covered them with gold. They made gold hooks for the posts
Exodus36:37 They made a screen out of fine linen yarn for the entrance to the outer tent. It was embroidered with violet
Exodus36:38 They also made five posts with hooks for {hanging} the screen. They covered the tops of the posts and the bands with gold

Exodus37:4 Then he made poles out of acacia wood and covered them with gold.
Exodus37:5 He put them through the rings on the sides of the ark in order to carry it.

Exodus37:15 These poles were made out of acacia wood and were covered with gold.

Exodus39:1 From the violet
Exodus39:2 They made the ephod out of fine linen yarn and gold
Exodus39:3 They hammered the gold into thin sheets and cut them up. They twisted the gold into threads
Exodus39:4 They made two shoulder straps attached at the {top} corners so that the ephod could be fastened.
Exodus39:5 They made the belt that is attached to the ephod out of the same fabric. They followed the Lord's instructions to Moses.
Exodus39:6 They mounted the onyx stones in gold settings
Exodus39:7 Then they fastened them on the shoulder straps of the ephod as a reminder of who the Israelites are. They followed the Lord's instructions to Moses.
Exodus39:8 They made the breastplate as creatively as they made the ephod. It was made out of gold
Exodus39:9 It was folded in half and was 9 inches square.
Exodus39:10 They fastened four rows of precious stones on it. In the first row they put red quartz
Exodus39:11 In the second row they put turquoise
Exodus39:12 In the third row they put jacinth
Exodus39:13 In the fourth row they put beryl
Exodus39:14 They corresponded to the 12 sons of Israel
Exodus39:15 For the breastplate they made chains out of pure gold
Exodus39:16 They made two gold settings and two gold rings and attached the two rings to the {top} two corners of the breastplate.
Exodus39:17 They fastened the two gold ropes to the rings at the {top} corners of the breastplate.
Exodus39:18 They fastened the other ends of the ropes to the two settings on the shoulder straps of the ephod {so that the breastplate hung} in front of it.
Exodus39:19 They made two gold rings and fastened them to the other two corners of the breastplate on the inside edge next to the ephod.
Exodus39:20 They made two {more} gold rings and fastened them to the bottom of the shoulder straps on the front of the ephod. This was close to the seam just above the belt of the ephod.
Exodus39:21 Then they fastened the breastplate by its rings to the rings of the ephod with a violet cord. So the breastplate was attached just above the belt of the ephod and was held in place. They followed the Lord's instructions to Moses.
Exodus39:22 They made the robe that is worn with the ephod
Exodus39:23 The opening in the center of the robe had a finished edge (like a leather collar) all around it to keep it from tearing.
Exodus39:24 On the hem of the robe they made pomegranates of violet
Exodus39:25 They made bells out of pure gold and fastened them in between the pomegranates all around the hem of the robe.
Exodus39:26 A gold bell alternated with a pomegranate all around the hem of the robe that is worn by Aaron when he serves as priest. They followed the Lord's instructions to Moses.
Exodus39:27 They wove inner robes out of fine linen for Aaron and his sons.
Exodus39:28 They also made the chief priest's turban and the other beautiful turbans out of fine linen. They made the undergarments and belt out of fine linen yarn.
Exodus39:29 The belt was embroidered with violet
Exodus39:30 They made the flower-shaped medallion (the holy crown) out of pure gold and engraved on it (as on a signet ring): Holy to the Lord.
Exodus39:31 They fastened a violet cord to it and tied it on top of the turban. They followed the Lord's instructions to Moses.
Exodus39:32 So all the work on the inner tent (the tent of meeting) was now done. The Israelites followed all the Lord's instructions to Moses.
Exodus39:33 Then they brought everything to Moses—the inner tent
Exodus39:34 the cover made of rams' skins dyed red
Exodus39:35 the ark containing the words of God's promise with its poles and the throne of mercy
Exodus39:36 the table with all the dishes
Exodus39:37 the pure {gold} lamp stand with its lamps in a row and all its utensils
Exodus39:38 the gold altar
Exodus39:39 the bronze altar with its bronze grate
Exodus39:40 the curtains for the courtyard
Exodus39:41 the special clothes worn when serving as priests in the holy place—both the holy clothes for the priest Aaron and the clothes for his sons when serving as priests.
Exodus39:42 The Israelites had done all the work following the Lord's instructions to Moses.
Exodus39:43 Moses inspected all the work and saw that they had followed the Lord's instructions. So Moses blessed them.
Exodus40:1 Then the Lord said to Moses
Exodus40:2 Set up the tent (the tent of meeting) on the first day of the first month of the year.
Exodus40:3 Place the ark containing the words of my promise inside it
Exodus40:4 Bring in the table
Exodus40:5 Put the gold altar for incense in front of the ark. Put up the screen at the entrance to the tent.
Exodus40:6 Put the altar for burnt offerings in front of the entrance to the tent of meeting.
Exodus40:7 Put the basin between the tent of meeting and the altar
Exodus40:8 Set up the surrounding courtyard
Exodus40:9 Take the anointing oil
Exodus40:10 Anoint the altar for burnt offerings and all the utensils. In this way you will dedicate the altar
Exodus40:11 Anoint the basin and stand
Exodus40:12 Bring Aaron and his sons to the entrance of the tent of meeting
Exodus40:13 Then dress Aaron in the holy clothes
Exodus40:14 Have his sons come forward
Exodus40:15 Anoint them to serve me as priests
Exodus40:16 Moses did everything as the Lord commanded him.
Exodus40:17 So the tent was set up on the first day of the first month of the second year {after the Israelites had left Egypt}.
Exodus40:18 When Moses set up the tent
Exodus40:19 He spread the outer tent over the inner tent and put the cover on top. Moses followed the Lord's instructions.
Exodus40:20 He took the words of God's promise and put them in the ark. He put the poles on the ark and placed the throne of mercy on top of the ark.
Exodus40:21 Then he brought the ark into the tent and hung the canopy over it to mark off where the ark was. Moses followed the Lord's instructions.
Exodus40:22 Moses put the table in the tent of meeting on the north side of the tent outside the canopy.
Exodus40:23 He arranged the bread on the table in the Lord's presence
Exodus40:24 He placed the lamp stand in the tent of meeting opposite the table
Exodus40:25 He set up the lamps in the Lord's presence
Exodus40:26 Moses put the gold altar in the tent of meeting in front of the canopy.
Exodus40:27 He burned sweet-smelling incense on it
Exodus40:28 Then he put up the screen at the entrance to the tent.
Exodus40:29 He put the altar for burnt offerings at the entrance to the tent (the tent of meeting). He sacrificed burnt offerings and grain offerings on it. Moses followed the Lord's instructions.
Exodus40:30 He put the basin between the tent of meeting and the altar and put water in it for washing.
Exodus40:31 Moses
Exodus40:32 They would wash whenever they went into the tent of meeting or whenever they approached the altar. Moses followed the Lord's instructions.
Exodus40:33 He set up the courtyard around the tent and the altar and put up the screen at the entrance to the courtyard. Finally
Exodus40:34 Then the {column of} smoke covered the tent of meeting
Exodus40:35 Moses couldn't go into the tent of meeting
Exodus40:36 In all their travels
Exodus40:37 But if the column didn't move
Exodus40:38 So the Lord's column stayed over the tent during the day
Leviticus1:1 The Lord called Moses and spoke to him from the tent of meeting. He said
Leviticus1:2 Tell the Israelites: If any of you bring a sacrifice to the Lord
Leviticus1:3 If you bring a burnt offering from your cattle
Leviticus1:4 Place your hand on the animal's head. The burnt offering will be accepted to make peace with the Lord.
Leviticus1:5 Then slaughter the bull in the Lord's presence. Aaron's sons
Leviticus1:6 Skin the burnt offering
Leviticus1:7 Then the sons of the priest Aaron will start a fire on the altar and lay the wood on the fire.
Leviticus1:8 Aaron's sons
Leviticus1:9 Wash the internal organs and legs. Then the priest will burn all of it on the altar. It is a burnt offering
Leviticus1:10 If your offering is a sheep or goat
Leviticus1:11 Slaughter it in the Lord's presence on the north side of the altar. Aaron's sons
Leviticus1:12 Then cut it into pieces. The priest will lay the head and the fat on the wood burning on the altar.
Leviticus1:13 Wash the internal organs and legs. Then the priest will burn all of it on the altar. It is a burnt offering
Leviticus1:14 If your offering to the Lord is a bird
Leviticus1:15 The priest must bring it to the altar. He will break its neck and burn the bird on the altar. First
Leviticus1:16 Remove the gizzard with its filth and throw it on the east side of the altar on the place for the ashes.
Leviticus1:17 Then pull on the bird's wings to tear the bird open
Leviticus2:1 {The Lord continued
Leviticus2:2 Then bring it to Aaron's sons
Leviticus2:3 The rest of the grain offering will belong to Aaron and his sons. It is very holy
Leviticus2:4 If you bring a grain offering which has been baked in an oven
Leviticus2:5 If your grain offering is prepared in a frying pan
Leviticus2:6 Break it into pieces and pour olive oil over it. It is a grain offering.
Leviticus2:7 If your grain offering is prepared in a skillet
Leviticus2:8 Bring the Lord the grain offering prepared in any of these ways. Offer it to the priest who will bring it to the altar.
Leviticus2:9 The priest will remove part of the grain offering and burn it as a reminder on the altar. It is an offering by fire
Leviticus2:10 The rest of the grain offering belongs to Aaron and his sons. It is very holy
Leviticus2:11 Every grain offering that you bring to the Lord must be prepared without yeast. Never burn yeast or honey as an offering to the Lord.
Leviticus2:12 You may bring them to the Lord as offerings of your first products. But they must never be placed on the altar to make a soothing aroma.
Leviticus2:13 Also put salt on each of your grain offerings. The salt of God's promise must never be left out of your grain offerings. Put salt on all your offerings.
Leviticus2:14 If you bring a grain offering to the Lord from the first grain you harvest
Leviticus2:15 Put olive oil on it
Leviticus2:16 The priest will burn the flour
Leviticus3:1 {The Lord continued
Leviticus3:2 Place your hand on the animal's head. Then slaughter it at the entrance to the tent of meeting. Then Aaron's sons
Leviticus3:3 From your offering remove the fat that covers the internal organs
Leviticus3:4 and the two kidneys with the fat on them and offer them by fire to the Lord. Also cut off the lobe of the liver along with the kidneys.
Leviticus3:5 Then Aaron's sons will lay them on top of the burnt offering on the burning wood. It is an offering by fire
Leviticus3:6 If your sacrifice is a fellowship offering of sheep to the Lord
Leviticus3:7 If your offering is a lamb
Leviticus3:8 Place your hand on the animal's head. Slaughter it in front of the tent of meeting. Then Aaron's sons will throw the blood against the altar on all sides.
Leviticus3:9 Then take the fat from the fellowship offering and offer it by fire to the Lord. Remove all the fat from the tail and the fat that covers the internal organs.
Leviticus3:10 Also remove the two kidneys with the fat on them along with the lobe of the liver.
Leviticus3:11 Then the priest will burn the fellowship offering on the altar. It is food
Leviticus3:12 If your offering is a goat
Leviticus3:13 Place your hand on its head. Slaughter it in front of the tent of meeting. Then Aaron's sons will throw the blood against the altar on all sides.
Leviticus3:14 Then bring the fat that covers the internal organs
Leviticus3:15 and the two kidneys with the fat on them as an offering by fire to the Lord. Also remove the lobe of the liver along with the kidneys.
Leviticus3:16 Then the priest will burn them on the altar. It is food
Leviticus3:17 This is a permanent law for generations to come wherever you live: Never eat any fat or blood.
Leviticus4:1 The Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus4:2 Tell the Israelites: If a person unintentionally does something wrong—even one thing that is forbidden by any of the Lord's commands—this is what he must do.
Leviticus4:3 If the anointed priest does something wrong and brings guilt on the people
Leviticus4:4 He must bring the bull into the Lord's presence at the entrance to the tent of meeting. He will place his hand on the bull's head. He will then slaughter the bull in the Lord's presence.
Leviticus4:5 Then the anointed priest will take some of the bull's blood and bring it into the tent of meeting.
Leviticus4:6 The priest will dip his finger in it and sprinkle some of the blood seven times in the Lord's presence facing the canopy in the holy place.
Leviticus4:7 Then the priest will put some of the blood on the horns of the altar for sweet-smelling incense in the Lord's presence in the tent of meeting. He will pour the rest of the bull's blood at the bottom of the altar for burnt offerings at the entrance to the tent of meeting.
Leviticus4:8 He will remove all of the fat from the bull that is the offering for sin
Leviticus4:9 and the two kidneys with the fat on them. He will also remove the lobe of the liver and the kidneys
Leviticus4:10 the same way they were removed from the bull used for the fellowship offering. The priest will lay them on the altar for burnt offerings.
Leviticus4:11 Then he will take the entire bull (the skin
Leviticus4:12 to a clean place outside the camp where the ashes are dumped. He will burn it there on a wood fire.
Leviticus4:13 If the whole congregation of Israel unintentionally does something wrong
Leviticus4:14 When the wrong they have done becomes known
Leviticus4:15 The leaders of the congregation will place their hands on the bull's head in the Lord's presence. One of them will slaughter it in the Lord's presence.
Leviticus4:16 Then the anointed priest will bring some of the bull's blood into the tent of meeting.
Leviticus4:17 The priest will dip his finger in some of the blood and sprinkle it seven times in the Lord's presence facing the canopy.
Leviticus4:18 He will also put some blood on the horns of the altar in the Lord's presence in the tent of meeting. He will pour the rest of the blood at the bottom of the altar for burnt offerings at the entrance to the tent of meeting.
Leviticus4:19 He will remove all the fat and burn it on the altar.
Leviticus4:20 He will do the same thing with this bull that he did with the bull used as the offering for sin. So the priest will make peace with the Lord for the people
Leviticus4:21 Then he will take the bull outside the camp and will burn it the same way he burned the first bull. It is an offering for sin for the community.
Leviticus4:22 When a leader unintentionally does something wrong—even one thing that is forbidden by any of the commands of the Lord his God—he will be guilty.
Leviticus4:23 When he is told about what he has done wrong
Leviticus4:24 He will place his hand on the goat's head and slaughter it in the Lord's presence where he slaughters animals for burnt offerings. It is an offering for sin.
Leviticus4:25 Then the priest will take some of the blood of the offering for sin with his finger and put it on the horns of the altar for burnt offerings. He will pour the rest of the blood at the bottom of the altar for burnt offerings.
Leviticus4:26 He will burn all the fat on the altar the same way the fat of the fellowship offering is burned. So the priest will make peace with the Lord for what the leader did wrong
Leviticus4:27 If a common person unintentionally does something wrong—even one thing forbidden by the Lord's commands—he will be guilty.
Leviticus4:28 When he is told about what he has done wrong
Leviticus4:29 He will place his hand on the animal's head and slaughter it where animals for burnt offerings are slaughtered.
Leviticus4:30 The priest will take some of the blood with his finger and put it on the horns of the altar for burnt offerings. He will pour the rest of the blood at the bottom of the altar.
Leviticus4:31 He will remove all the fat the same way it is removed from the fellowship offering. The priest will burn it on the altar for a soothing aroma to the Lord. So the priest will make peace with the Lord for that person
Leviticus4:32 If someone brings a lamb as his offering for sin
Leviticus4:33 He will place his hand on the animal's head and slaughter it where he slaughters animals for burnt offerings.
Leviticus4:34 Then the priest will take some of the blood from the offering for sin with his finger and put it on the horns of the altar for burnt offerings. He will pour the rest of the blood at the bottom of the altar.
Leviticus4:35 He will remove all the fat the same way the fat of the lamb is removed from the fellowship offerings. Then the priest will burn it on the altar with the offering by fire to the Lord. So the priest will make peace with the Lord for what that person did wrong
Leviticus5:1 {The Lord continued
Leviticus5:2 If you touch anything unclean—the unclean dead body of a wild or tame animal or the body of an unclean
Leviticus5:3 If you become unclean by touching human uncleanness of any kind and then ignore it (although you know what you did)
Leviticus5:4 If you hastily take a vow about what you will or will not do (as some people do) and then ignore it (although you know what you said)
Leviticus5:5 So if you are guilty of any of these sins
Leviticus5:6 Bring your guilt offering to the Lord for the sin you committed. It must be a female sheep or goat as an offering for sin. Then the priest will make peace with the Lord for what you did wrong.
Leviticus5:7 Now
Leviticus5:8 Bring them to the priest
Leviticus5:9 He will sprinkle some of the blood from the offering for sin on the side of the altar
Leviticus5:10 Then
Leviticus5:11 But if you cannot afford two mourning doves or two pigeons
Leviticus5:12 Bring it to the priest. The priest will take a handful of it. He will burn it as a reminder on top of the offering by fire to the Lord on the altar. It is an offering for sin.
Leviticus5:13 So the priest will make peace with the Lord for what you did wrong
Leviticus5:14 The Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus5:15 If any of you fail to do your duty by unintentionally doing something wrong with any of the Lord's holy things
Leviticus5:16 Pay for whatever holy things you used plus one-fifth more. Give it to the priest. So the priest will use the ram sacrificed for the guilt offering to make peace with the Lord for what you did wrong
Leviticus5:17 If any of you do wrong—even one thing forbidden by any of the Lord's commands
Leviticus5:18 You must bring the priest a ram that has no defects from the flock or its value in money for a guilt offering. The priest will make peace with the Lord for the wrong you did unintentionally (although you didn't know what you did)
Leviticus5:19 It is a guilt offering because you are certainly guilty as far as the Lord is concerned.
Leviticus6:1 The Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus6:2 If any of you sin against the Lord by failing to do your duty
Leviticus6:3 If you find something that someone lost and lie about it under oath
Leviticus6:4 you are sinning and will be guilty. Return what you stole or seized
Leviticus6:5 or whatever it was that you swore falsely about. Pay it back in full plus one-fifth more. Give it back to its owner on the day you bring your guilt offering.
Leviticus6:6 Then bring the Lord your guilt offering
Leviticus6:7 So the priest will make peace with the Lord. Then you will be forgiven for whatever you did that made you guilty.
Leviticus6:8 The Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus6:9 Command Aaron and his sons: These are the instructions for the burnt offering that stays on the altar overnight while the altar fire is kept burning.
Leviticus6:10 The priest must put on his linen clothes
Leviticus6:11 Then he will take off these clothes and put on some others. He will take the ashes to a clean place outside the camp.
Leviticus6:12 The fire must always be burning on the altar. It must never go out. The priest will burn wood on it every morning. He will lay the burnt offering on the fire and burn the fat of the fellowship offering.
Leviticus6:13 The fire must always be burning on the altar. It must never go out.
Leviticus6:14 These are the instructions for the grain offering. Aaron's sons must bring it into the Lord's presence in front of the altar.
Leviticus6:15 One of them will remove a handful of flour from the grain offering
Leviticus6:16 Aaron and his sons will eat the rest of it. They will eat unleavened bread in a holy place
Leviticus6:17 Don't use yeast in baking the bread. I have given it to them as their share from the offerings by fire made to me. It is very holy like the offering for sin and the guilt offering.
Leviticus6:18 Every male descendant of Aaron may eat it. It is a permanent law for generations to come regarding the offering by fire to the Lord. Everyone who touches it will become holy.
Leviticus6:19 The Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus6:20 This is the offering that Aaron and his sons must bring to the Lord on the day he is anointed—eight cups of flour. They must do this every day. He must offer half of it in the morning and half in the evening.
Leviticus6:21 Prepare it in a frying pan with olive oil
Leviticus6:22 Aaron's son who is anointed to take his place as priest will prepare it. This is a permanent law of the Lord: It must be completely burned.
Leviticus6:23 Every grain offering made by a priest must be completely burned. It must not be eaten.
Leviticus6:24 The Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus6:25 Tell Aaron and his sons: These are the instructions for the offering for sin. The offering for sin must be slaughtered in the Lord's presence in the same place where the burnt offering is slaughtered. It is very holy.
Leviticus6:26 The priest who makes the offering for sin will eat it in a holy place
Leviticus6:27 Anything that touches its meat will be holy. If blood gets on someone's clothes
Leviticus6:28 Any piece of pottery in which the offering for sin is cooked must be broken into pieces. Any copper kettle in which the offering for sin is cooked must be scoured and rinsed with water.
Leviticus6:29 Any male among the priests may eat the offering for sin. It is very holy.
Leviticus6:30 Any offering for sin must not be eaten if some of the blood was brought into the holy place in the tent of meeting to make peace with the Lord. It must be burned.

Leviticus7:2 It must be slaughtered in the same place where the burnt offering is slaughtered. {A priest} will throw the blood against the altar on all sides.
Leviticus7:3 He will offer all the fat
Leviticus7:4 and the two kidneys with the fat on them. He will also remove the lobe of the liver along with the kidneys.
Leviticus7:5 The priest will burn them on the altar. It is a guilt offering by fire to the Lord.
Leviticus7:6 Any male among the priests may eat it. It will be eaten in a holy place. It is very holy.
Leviticus7:7 The same instructions apply to the offering for sin and the guilt offering. Both offerings belong to the priest to make peace with the Lord.
Leviticus7:8 The skin of the burnt offering belongs to the priest who sacrifices it.
Leviticus7:9 Every grain offering
Leviticus7:10 Every grain offering
Leviticus7:11 These are the instructions for the fellowship offering that you must bring to the Lord.
Leviticus7:12 If you offer it as a thank offering
Leviticus7:13 In addition to these rings of bread
Leviticus7:14 From every offering you must bring one loaf to the Lord as a {special} contribution. It will belong to the priest who throws the blood of the fellowship offering.
Leviticus7:15 The meat from your fellowship offering of thanksgiving must be eaten on the day it is offered. Never leave any of it until morning.
Leviticus7:16 If your sacrificial offering is something you vowed or a freewill offering
Leviticus7:17 However
Leviticus7:18 You will not be accepted if any meat from the fellowship offering is eaten on the third day. You will not receive credit for it. It is repulsive to God. The person who eats any of it must be punished.
Leviticus7:19 Meat that touches anything unclean must not be eaten. It must be burned. Anyone who is clean may eat from these sacrifices.
Leviticus7:20 Those who eat meat from the Lord's fellowship offering while unclean must be excluded from the people.
Leviticus7:21 Those who touch anything unclean
Leviticus7:22 The Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus7:23 Tell the Israelites: Never eat any fat from bulls
Leviticus7:24 The fat from an animal that dies naturally or is killed by wild animals you may use for any other purpose
Leviticus7:25 Those who eat the fat from an animal which they sacrificed by fire to the Lord must be excluded from the people.
Leviticus7:26 Never eat the blood of any bird or animal no matter where you live.
Leviticus7:27 Those who eat any blood must be excluded from the people.
Leviticus7:28 The Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus7:29 Tell the Israelites: Anyone who offers the Lord a fellowship offering must bring a part of that sacrifice as a gift to the Lord.
Leviticus7:30 Bring the sacrifices by fire made to the Lord yourself. Bring the fat with the breast. Take the breast and present it to the Lord.
Leviticus7:31 The priest will burn the fat on the altar. However
Leviticus7:32 You will also give the priest the right thigh as a contribution.
Leviticus7:33 When any of Aaron's sons offer the blood and fat of the fellowship offering
Leviticus7:34 From the fellowship offerings of the Israelites
Leviticus7:35 This is the share for Aaron and his sons from the sacrifices by fire made to the Lord. It was given to them on the day Moses ordained them to serve the Lord as priests.
Leviticus7:36 The Lord commanded the Israelites to give it to them on the day he anointed them. This is a permanent law for generations to come.
Leviticus7:37 These are the instructions for the burnt offering
Leviticus7:38 On Mount Sinai the Lord gave Moses commands about these offerings at the same time that he commanded the Israelites to bring their offerings to him in the Sinai Desert.
Leviticus8:1 The Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus8:2 Take Aaron and his sons
Leviticus8:3 Gather the whole congregation at the entrance to the tent of meeting.
Leviticus8:4 Moses did as the Lord commanded him
Leviticus8:5 Moses told the congregation
Leviticus8:6 Moses had Aaron and his sons come forward
Leviticus8:7 He put the linen robe on Aaron and fastened the belt around him. He also dressed him in the robe that is worn with the ephod. He fastened the ephod to it.
Leviticus8:8 Then he put the breastplate on him
Leviticus8:9 He put the turban on him and fastened the gold medallion (the holy crown) to the front of the turban as the Lord had commanded Moses.
Leviticus8:10 Moses took the anointing oil to anoint the tent and everything in it and dedicate them.
Leviticus8:11 He sprinkled some of the oil on the altar seven times and anointed the altar
Leviticus8:12 He also poured some of the anointing oil on Aaron's head and anointed him to set him apart for his holy duties.
Leviticus8:13 Moses had Aaron's sons come forward. He put linen robes on them
Leviticus8:14 He brought the bull that was the offering for sin. Aaron and his sons placed their hands on its head.
Leviticus8:15 When it was slaughtered
Leviticus8:16 Moses took all the fat that was on the internal organs
Leviticus8:17 He burned the rest of the bull
Leviticus8:18 He brought forward the ram for the burnt offering. Aaron and his sons placed their hands on the ram's head.
Leviticus8:19 Moses slaughtered it and threw the blood against the altar on all sides.
Leviticus8:20 When the ram was cut into pieces
Leviticus8:21 He washed the internal organs and the legs. Then Moses burned the whole ram on the altar as the Lord commanded him. It was a burnt offering
Leviticus8:22 He brought forward the second ram for the ordination offering. Aaron and his sons placed their hands on the ram's head.
Leviticus8:23 Moses slaughtered it
Leviticus8:24 Moses also brought Aaron's sons forward. He put some of the blood on their right ear lobes
Leviticus8:25 He took the fat
Leviticus8:26 He took a loaf of unleavened bread
Leviticus8:27 Then he placed all these things in the hands of Aaron and his sons. Moses presented all these things to the Lord as an offering.
Leviticus8:28 Then he took them from their hands and burned them on top of the burnt offering on the altar. These were ordination offerings
Leviticus8:29 Moses also took the breast from the ram of the ordination offering and presented it to the Lord. It was Moses' share
Leviticus8:30 Moses took some of the anointing oil and some of the blood that was on the altar
Leviticus8:31 Moses told Aaron and his sons: Cook the meat at the entrance to the tent of meeting. Take the meat and the bread in the basket of the ordination offering. Eat them there as I commanded when I said
Leviticus8:32 You must burn any meat or bread that is left over.
Leviticus8:33 You will not leave the entrance to the tent of meeting for seven days
Leviticus8:34 I did today what the Lord commanded me to make peace with the Lord for you.
Leviticus8:35 You will stay at the entrance to the tent of meeting day and night for seven days and serve as the Lord tells you. Then you will not die. This is what I was commanded.
Leviticus8:36 So Aaron and his sons did everything the Lord commanded through Moses.

Leviticus11:1 The Lord spoke to Moses and Aaron
Leviticus11:2 Tell the Israelites: Here are the kinds of land animals you may eat:
Leviticus11:3 all animals that have completely divided hoofs and that also chew their cud.
Leviticus11:4 However
Leviticus11:5 You must never eat rock badgers. (Rock badgers are unclean because they chew their cud but do not have divided hoofs.)
Leviticus11:6 You must never eat rabbits. (Rabbits are unclean because they chew their cud but do not have divided hoofs.)
Leviticus11:7 You must never eat pigs. (Because pigs have completely divided hoofs but do not chew their cud
Leviticus11:8 Never eat the meat of these animals or touch their dead bodies. They are unclean for you.
Leviticus11:9 Here are the kinds of creatures that live in the water which you may eat—anything in the seas and streams that has fins and scales.
Leviticus11:10 However
Leviticus11:11 They must remain disgusting to you. Never eat their meat. Consider their dead bodies disgusting.
Leviticus11:12 Every creature in the water without fins or scales is disgusting to you.
Leviticus11:13 Here are the kinds of birds you must consider disgusting and must not eat. They are eagles
Leviticus11:14 kites
Leviticus11:15 all types of crows
Leviticus11:16 ostriches
Leviticus11:17 little owls
Leviticus11:18 barn owls
Leviticus11:19 storks
Leviticus11:20 Every swarming
Leviticus11:21 However
Leviticus11:22 You may eat any kind of locust
Leviticus11:23 Every kind of winged insect that walks across the ground like a four-legged animal is disgusting to you.
Leviticus11:24 Regarding the creatures mentioned above
Leviticus11:25 Whoever carries any part of their dead bodies must wash his clothes. He will be unclean until evening.
Leviticus11:26 All animals whose hoofs are not completely divided or that don't chew their cud are unclean for you. Whoever touches them is unclean.
Leviticus11:27 All four-legged animals that walk on their paws are unclean for you. Whoever touches their dead bodies will be unclean until evening.
Leviticus11:28 Those who carry the dead body of any of these animals must wash their clothes and will be unclean until evening. These animals are unclean for you.
Leviticus11:29 The following swarming creatures that move on the ground are unclean for you—moles
Leviticus11:30 geckos
Leviticus11:31 Among all the swarming creatures that move on the ground
Leviticus11:32 When the dead body of one of these creatures falls on something
Leviticus11:33 If any of these creatures falls into a piece of pottery
Leviticus11:34 If water {from that pottery} touches any food
Leviticus11:35 Anything on which their dead bodies fall is unclean. If it is an oven or a stove
Leviticus11:36 However
Leviticus11:37 If their dead bodies fall on seed that is to be planted
Leviticus11:38 But if water is poured on the seed and their dead bodies fall on it
Leviticus11:39 When any animal that you are allowed to eat dies
Leviticus11:40 Those who eat any of its dead body must wash their clothes and will be unclean until evening. Those who carry its dead body away will wash their clothes and will be unclean until evening.
Leviticus11:41 Any creature that swarms on the ground is disgusting and must not be eaten.
Leviticus11:42 Don't eat any creature with many legs that goes on its belly or on the ground like a four-legged animal
Leviticus11:43 Don't become disgusting by eating anything that swarms on the ground. Never allow yourselves to become unclean because of them.
Leviticus11:44 Here is the reason: I am the Lord your God. You must live holy lives. Be holy because I am holy. Never become unclean by touching anything that swarms or crawls on the ground.
Leviticus11:45 Here is the reason {again}: I am the Lord. I brought you out of Egypt to be your God. Be holy because I am holy.
Leviticus11:46 These are the instructions about animals
Leviticus11:47 These instructions help you distinguish between clean and unclean
Leviticus12:1 The Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus12:2 Tell the Israelites: When a woman gives birth to a boy
Leviticus12:3 The boy must be circumcised when he is eight days old.
Leviticus12:4 Then she must stay at home for 33 days in order to be made clean from her bleeding. She must not touch anything holy or go into the holy place until the days needed to make her clean are over.
Leviticus12:5 When a woman gives birth to a girl
Leviticus12:6 When the days needed to make her clean are over
Leviticus12:7 The priest will offer them in the Lord's presence to make peace with the Lord for her. Then she will be clean from her flow of blood. These are the instructions for the woman who gives birth to a boy or a girl.
Leviticus12:8 If she cannot afford a lamb
Leviticus13:1 The Lord spoke to Moses and Aaron
Leviticus13:2 If anyone has a sore
Leviticus13:3 The priest will examine the disease. If the hair in the diseased area has turned white
Leviticus13:4 But if the irritated area is white and does not look deeper than the rest of the skin
Leviticus13:5 On the seventh day the priest will examine him again. If the disease looks the same and has not spread
Leviticus13:6 On the seventh day the priest will examine him again. If the diseased area has faded and not spread
Leviticus13:7 But if the rash has spread after he has shown himself to the priest to be declared clean
Leviticus13:8 The priest will examine him one more time
Leviticus13:9 If anyone has an infectious skin disease
Leviticus13:10 The priest will examine him. If there is a white sore that has turned the hair white
Leviticus13:11 he has a chronic skin disease. Without putting him in isolation
Leviticus13:12 If skin disease develops and covers the whole person from head to foot (so far as the priest can see)
Leviticus13:13 the priest will examine him. If the disease does cover his whole body
Leviticus13:14 But if raw flesh appears
Leviticus13:15 The priest will examine the raw flesh and declare him unclean. The raw flesh is unclean. It is an infectious skin disease.
Leviticus13:16 But if the raw flesh turns white again
Leviticus13:17 The priest will examine him again
Leviticus13:18 If a boil on the skin has healed
Leviticus13:19 and in its place there is a white sore or a pink area
Leviticus13:20 The priest will examine it. If it looks deeper than the rest of the skin and its hair has turned white
Leviticus13:21 But if the priest examines the affected area and the hair in it is not white or the affected area is not deeper than the rest of the skin but has faded
Leviticus13:22 If the area has spread
Leviticus13:23 But if the irritated area has not spread
Leviticus13:24 If anyone has a burn on his skin and the raw flesh of the burn turns into a pink or bright white area
Leviticus13:25 the priest will examine it. If the hair on the affected area has turned white and the affected area looks deeper than the rest of the skin
Leviticus13:26 But if the priest examines it and the hair in it is not white and the affected area is not deeper than the rest of the skin but has faded
Leviticus13:27 On the seventh day the priest will examine him again. If the area has spread
Leviticus13:28 If the irritated area does not spread but has faded
Leviticus13:29 If a man or a woman has some disease on the head or chin
Leviticus13:30 the priest will examine the disease. If it looks deeper than the rest of the skin and there is thin yellow hair on it
Leviticus13:31 But if the priest examines the scabby disease and it does not look deeper than the rest of the skin and there is no black hair in it
Leviticus13:32 On the seventh day the priest will examine the disease. If the scab has not spread
Leviticus13:33 the person will shave everything except the scab. The priest will put the person with the scab in isolation for another seven days.
Leviticus13:34 On the seventh day the priest will examine the scab again. If the scab has not spread on the skin and does not look deeper than the rest of the skin
Leviticus13:35 But if the scab spreads after the person has been declared clean
Leviticus13:36 the priest will make another examination. If the scab has spread on the skin
Leviticus13:37 But if he sees that the scab hasn't spread and black hair grows on it
Leviticus13:38 If a man or a woman has white irritated areas of skin
Leviticus13:39 the priest will make an examination. If the irritated areas on the skin are pale white
Leviticus13:40 If a man loses his hair
Leviticus13:41 If he loses the hair on the front of his head
Leviticus13:42 But if there is a pink patch on the bald places in back or in front
Leviticus13:43 The priest will examine him. If the sore from the disease in the bald places in back or in front is pink like a skin disease somewhere else on the body
Leviticus13:44 the man has come down with an infectious skin disease. He is unclean. The priest must declare him unclean because of the skin disease on his head.
Leviticus13:45 People who come down with a skin disease must wear torn clothes and leave their hair uncombed. They must cover their upper lips and call out
Leviticus13:46 As long as they have the skin disease
Leviticus13:47 Now about clothing—if there is a green or red area on a piece of clothing
Leviticus13:48 that is woven or knitted from linen or wool or on any leather article
Leviticus13:49 it is mildew. It must be shown to the priest.
Leviticus13:50 The priest will examine the mildew and will put the clothing in a separate place for seven days.
Leviticus13:51 On the seventh day he will examine the area again. If the spot is spreading
Leviticus13:52 He must burn the piece of clothing or the leather article because the mildew is growing.
Leviticus13:53 But if the priest sees that the area has not spread
Leviticus13:54 he must order the area to be washed and put the clothing in a separate place for seven more days.
Leviticus13:55 The priest will examine the area again after it is washed. If it doesn't look any different and the mildew has not spread
Leviticus13:56 If the priest sees that the area is pale after washing
Leviticus13:57 However
Leviticus13:58 But if the area disappears from the woven or knitted clothing or any leather article when it is washed
Leviticus13:59 These are the instructions for deciding whether mildew in clothing that is woven or knitted from linen or wool or in any leather article is clean or unclean.
Leviticus14:1 The Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus14:2 These are the instructions for making a person clean after a skin disease. He must be taken to the priest.
Leviticus14:3 The priest will go outside the camp and examine him. If the person is healed
Leviticus14:4 the priest will order someone to get two living
Leviticus14:5 Then the priest will order someone to kill one bird over a clay bowl containing fresh water.
Leviticus14:6 The priest will take the living bird
Leviticus14:7 He will sprinkle the blood seven times on the one to be cleansed and will declare that person clean. Then he will let the living bird fly away into the open country.
Leviticus14:8 The one to be cleansed must wash his clothes
Leviticus14:9 On the seventh day he must shave off all the hair on his head
Leviticus14:10 On the eighth day he must take two male lambs that have no defects and a one-year-old female lamb that has no defects. He must also take eight cups of flour mixed with olive oil for a grain offering along with a quart of olive oil.
Leviticus14:11 The priest who will declare him clean must bring the person and his offerings into the Lord's presence at the entrance to the tent of meeting.
Leviticus14:12 The priest will take one of the male lambs and the quart of olive oil and present them to the Lord as a guilt offering.
Leviticus14:13 He will slaughter the lamb in the holy place where he slaughters the offering for sin and the burnt offering. He will do this because the guilt offering
Leviticus14:14 Then the priest will take some of the blood from the guilt offering and put it on the right ear lobe
Leviticus14:15 The priest will also take some of the olive oil and pour it into his own left hand.
Leviticus14:16 He will dip his right finger in the oil in his left hand
Leviticus14:17 The priest will put some of the oil that is still in his hand on the right ear lobe
Leviticus14:18 The priest will put the rest of the oil in his hand on the head of the one to be cleansed. So he will make peace with the Lord for that person in the Lord's presence.
Leviticus14:19 The priest will also sacrifice the offering for sin to make peace with the Lord for the one who is being cleansed from his impurity. After that
Leviticus14:20 The priest will sacrifice the burnt offering and the grain offering on the altar. So the priest will make peace with the Lord for that person
Leviticus14:21 But if the one to be cleansed is poor and cannot afford that much
Leviticus14:22 and two mourning doves or two pigeons (whatever he can afford). The one will be an offering for sin and the other a burnt offering.
Leviticus14:23 On the eighth day he will take them to the priest for his cleansing at the entrance to the tent of meeting in the Lord's presence.
Leviticus14:24 The priest will take the lamb for the guilt offering and the quart of olive oil and present them to the Lord.
Leviticus14:25 He will slaughter the lamb as a guilt offering. Then the priest will take some of the blood of the guilt offering and put it on the right ear lobe
Leviticus14:26 The priest will pour some of the olive oil into his own left hand.
Leviticus14:27 With his right finger he will sprinkle some of the oil seven times in the Lord's presence.
Leviticus14:28 The priest will put some of the oil that is in his hand on the right ear lobe
Leviticus14:29 In the Lord's presence
Leviticus14:30 Then the one to be cleansed must take one of the mourning doves or pigeons (whichever he can afford)
Leviticus14:31 and sacrifice it as an offering for sin. He will take the other and sacrifice it as a burnt offering together with the grain offering. So in the Lord's presence the priest will make peace with the Lord for the one who is being cleansed.
Leviticus14:32 These are the instructions for one who has an infectious skin disease but cannot afford what is needed for his cleansing.
Leviticus14:33 The Lord spoke to Moses and Aaron
Leviticus14:34 When you come to Canaan that I am going to give to you
Leviticus14:35 The owner of that house must come and tell the priest that there is something that looks like mildew in his house.
Leviticus14:36 Before the priest examines the house
Leviticus14:37 He will examine the mildew area on the walls. If it is green and red in sunken areas that are deeper than the rest of the wall
Leviticus14:38 the priest will go out to the door of the house and close up the house for seven days.
Leviticus14:39 On the seventh day the priest will go back and examine it again. If the mildew in the walls of the house has spread
Leviticus14:40 the priest must order the stones that have the mildew to be torn out and thrown outside the city in an unclean place.
Leviticus14:41 He must have the entire inside of the house scraped. The plaster dust scraped off the walls must be dumped in an unclean place outside the city.
Leviticus14:42 The stones must be replaced
Leviticus14:43 If the mildew develops again in the house after all this
Leviticus14:44 the priest will examine it one more time. If it is a spreading type of mildew
Leviticus14:45 The house—stones
Leviticus14:46 Whoever goes into the house any time it is closed up will be unclean until evening.
Leviticus14:47 Whoever sleeps or eats in the house must wash his clothes.
Leviticus14:48 But if the priest comes and makes an examination and the mildew has not spread in the house after it is plastered again
Leviticus14:49 The priest must take two birds
Leviticus14:50 He must kill the one bird over a clay bowl containing fresh water.
Leviticus14:51 He must take the cedar wood
Leviticus14:52 So he must use the bird's blood
Leviticus14:53 Then he will let the living bird fly from the city into the open country. He will make peace with the Lord for the house
Leviticus14:54 These are the instructions for any kind of mildew or fungus
Leviticus14:55 that infects clothing or houses
Leviticus14:56 and for skin diseases where there is a sore
Leviticus14:57 These instructions for skin diseases and mildew help you distinguish between what is clean and what is unclean.
Leviticus15:1 The Lord spoke to Moses and Aaron
Leviticus15:2 Tell the Israelites: If a man has a discharge from his penis
Leviticus15:3 He is unclean because of the discharge from his penis. Whether it is chronic or not makes no difference; he is still unclean.
Leviticus15:4 The man who has a discharge makes everything he lies on or sits on unclean.
Leviticus15:5 Those who touch his bed must wash their clothes and their bodies. They will be unclean until evening.
Leviticus15:6 Those who sit on anything he sat on must wash their clothes and their bodies. They will be unclean until evening.
Leviticus15:7 Those who touch a man who has a discharge must wash their clothes and their bodies. They will be unclean until evening.
Leviticus15:8 If a man who has a discharge spits on anyone who is clean
Leviticus15:9 When a man who has a discharge sits on a saddle
Leviticus15:10 Those who carry such things must wash their clothes and their bodies. They will be unclean until evening.
Leviticus15:11 If a man who has a discharge touches anyone without first rinsing his hands
Leviticus15:12 When a man who has a discharge touches pottery
Leviticus15:13 When a man's discharge stops
Leviticus15:14 On the eighth day he must take two mourning doves or two pigeons and come into the Lord's presence at the entrance to the tent of meeting. He will give these birds to the priest.
Leviticus15:15 The priest will sacrifice one as an offering for sin and the other as a burnt offering. So in the Lord's presence
Leviticus15:16 If a man has an emission of semen
Leviticus15:17 Any clothes or any leather with semen on it must be washed. It will be unclean until evening.
Leviticus15:18 When a man has sexual intercourse with a woman and has an emission of semen
Leviticus15:19 When a woman has her monthly period
Leviticus15:20 Everything she lies on or sits on during her period will be unclean.
Leviticus15:21 Those who touch her bed must wash their clothes and their bodies. They will be unclean until evening.
Leviticus15:22 Those who touch anything she sits on must wash their clothes and their bodies. They will be unclean until evening.
Leviticus15:23 If her blood touches anything on the bed or anything she sits on
Leviticus15:24 If a man has sexual intercourse with her while she has her period
Leviticus15:25 If a woman has a discharge of blood for many days other than her monthly period
Leviticus15:26 As long as she has a discharge
Leviticus15:27 Those who touch these things are unclean and must wash their clothes and their bodies. They will be unclean until evening.
Leviticus15:28 When her discharge stops
Leviticus15:29 On the eighth day she must take two mourning doves or two pigeons and bring them to the priest at the entrance to the tent of meeting.
Leviticus15:30 The priest will offer one as an offering for sin and the other as a burnt offering. So in the Lord's presence the priest will make peace with the Lord for the woman who had an unclean discharge.
Leviticus15:31 You must separate the Israelites from anything that keeps them from being presentable to me. Otherwise
Leviticus15:32 These are the instructions for any man who has a discharge or an emission of semen that makes him unclean
Leviticus15:33 for any woman who has her period
Leviticus16:1 The Lord spoke to Moses after Aaron's two sons had come into the Lord's presence and died.
Leviticus16:2 The Lord said
Leviticus16:3 This is what Aaron must do in order to come into the holy place: He must take a bull as an offering for sin and a ram as a burnt offering.
Leviticus16:4 He must put on a holy linen robe and wear linen undergarments. He must wear a linen belt and turban. These are holy clothes. He must wash his body and put them on.
Leviticus16:5 He will take two male goats from the congregation of Israel as an offering for sin and a ram as a burnt offering.
Leviticus16:6 Aaron must sacrifice the bull as his own offering for sin. By doing this
Leviticus16:7 He must take the two male goats and bring them into the Lord's presence at the entrance to the tent of meeting.
Leviticus16:8 Then Aaron must throw lots for the two goats. One lot will be for the Lord and the other for Azazel.
Leviticus16:9 Aaron must sacrifice the goat chosen by lot for the Lord as an offering for sin.
Leviticus16:10 But he must bring the goat chosen by lot for Azazel into the Lord's presence. He will release it in the desert to Azazel in order to make peace with the Lord for himself and his family.
Leviticus16:11 Aaron will bring the bull. He will then slaughter it as his own offering for sin. By doing this he will make peace with the Lord for himself and his family.
Leviticus16:12 He will take an incense burner full of burning coals from the altar
Leviticus16:13 Then he will put the incense on the fire in the Lord's presence. The cloud of incense will cover the throne of mercy
Leviticus16:14 He will take some of the bull's blood and sprinkle it with his finger on the east side of the throne of mercy. Then he will sprinkle some of the blood with his finger seven times in front of the throne of mercy.
Leviticus16:15 Next
Leviticus16:16 So he will make peace with the Lord for all the sins the Israelites committed against the holy place. These sins happened because the Israelites were unclean and because they committed rebellious acts. He will do the same for the tent of meeting which is among an unclean people.
Leviticus16:17 No one may be in the tent of meeting from the time Aaron enters the holy place to do this until he comes out. Aaron will make peace with the Lord for his own sins
Leviticus16:18 Then he will go out to the altar that is in the Lord's presence and make peace with the Lord there for the sins committed. He will take some of the blood from the bull and some of the goat's blood and put it all around the horns of the altar.
Leviticus16:19 With his finger he will sprinkle some of the blood on it seven times. Because the Israelites made it unclean
Leviticus16:20 When he finishes making peace with the Lord at the holy place
Leviticus16:21 Aaron will place both hands on its head. He will confess over it all the sins
Leviticus16:22 The goat will take all their sins away to a deserted place. The man must release the goat in the desert.
Leviticus16:23 Then Aaron will go to the tent of meeting
Leviticus16:24 He will wash his body in the holy place and put on his other clothes. Then he will come out and sacrifice the burnt offering for himself and for the people to make peace with the Lord for his own sins and the sins of the people.
Leviticus16:25 He will burn the fat of the offering for sin on the altar.
Leviticus16:26 The man who released the goat to Azazel must wash his clothes and his body. Then he may return to the camp.
Leviticus16:27 He must take the bull and the goat outside the camp. These animals were the offering for sin whose blood was brought into the holy place to make peace with the Lord for sins. The skin
Leviticus16:28 Whoever burns them must wash his clothes and his body. Then he may return to the camp.
Leviticus16:29 This will be a permanent law for you: On the tenth day of the seventh month both native Israelites and foreigners must humble themselves. They must do no work.
Leviticus16:30 On this day Aaron will make peace with the Lord to make you clean. Then you will be clean from all your sins in the Lord's presence.
Leviticus16:31 This is the most important worship festival there is for you. You will humble yourselves. It is a permanent law.
Leviticus16:32 The priest who is anointed and ordained to serve as chief priest in his father's place will pay for sins. He will put on the holy linen clothes
Leviticus16:33 and will make peace with the Lord at the holy place
Leviticus16:34 This permanent law tells you how to make peace with the Lord once a year for all the sins the Israelites committed. Aaron did as the Lord had commanded Moses.
Leviticus17:1 The Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus17:2 Tell Aaron
Leviticus17:3 Any Israelite who slaughters a bull
Leviticus17:4 is guilty of bloodshed. He has shed blood and must be excluded from the people. Bring the animal to the entrance of the tent of meeting. Offer it to the Lord in front of the Lord's tent.
Leviticus17:5 {This means} that the people of Israel must take the sacrifices they have been making in the open fields and bring them to the Lord. They must bring them to the priest at the entrance to the tent of meeting. The people will sacrifice them as fellowship offerings to the Lord.
Leviticus17:6 The priest will pour the blood against the Lord's altar at the entrance to the tent of meeting. He will burn the fat as a soothing aroma to the Lord.
Leviticus17:7 The people must stop sacrificing to goat idols and chasing after them as though they were prostitutes. This is a permanent law for the people and for future generations.
Leviticus17:8 Tell them: If Israelites or foreigners make burnt offerings or sacrifices
Leviticus17:9 but do not bring them to the entrance of the tent of meeting to offer them to the Lord
Leviticus17:10 If Israelites or foreigners eat any blood
Leviticus17:11 because blood contains life. I have given this blood to you to make peace with me on the altar. Blood is needed to make peace with me.
Leviticus17:12 That is why I have said to the people of Israel: Neither you nor foreigners should ever eat blood.
Leviticus17:13 If Israelites or foreigners hunt any animal or bird that may be eaten
Leviticus17:14 This is because the life of any creature is in its blood. So I have said to the people of Israel: Never eat any blood
Leviticus17:15 Native Israelites or foreigners who eat the body of an animal that dies naturally or is killed by another animal must wash their clothes and their bodies. They will be unclean until evening. Then they will be clean.
Leviticus17:16 If they don't wash their clothes and their bodies
Leviticus18:1 The Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus18:2 Tell the Israelites: I am the Lord your God.
Leviticus18:3 You used to live in Egypt. Don't live the way the Egyptians do. I am bringing you to Canaan. Don't live the way the Canaanites do. Never live by their standards.
Leviticus18:4 Follow my rules
Leviticus18:5 Live by my standards
Leviticus18:6 Never have sexual intercourse with anyone related to you by blood. I am the Lord.
Leviticus18:7 Never have sexual intercourse with your mother. She is your own mother. Never have sexual intercourse with her.
Leviticus18:8 Never have sexual intercourse with your stepmother. She is related to you through your father.
Leviticus18:9 Never have sexual intercourse with your stepsister
Leviticus18:10 Never have sexual intercourse with your granddaughter
Leviticus18:11 Never have sexual intercourse with a daughter of your father and his wife. She is your own sister.
Leviticus18:12 Never have sexual intercourse with your father's sister. She is your paternal aunt.
Leviticus18:13 Never have sexual intercourse with your mother's sister. She is your maternal aunt.
Leviticus18:14 Never have sexual intercourse with the wife of your father's brother. She
Leviticus18:15 Never have sexual intercourse with your daughter-in-law. She is your son's wife. Never have sexual intercourse with her.
Leviticus18:16 Never have sexual intercourse with your sister-in-law. She is your brother's wife.
Leviticus18:17 Never have sexual intercourse with a woman and her daughter or a woman and her granddaughter. They are related. Doing this is perverted.
Leviticus18:18 While your wife is living
Leviticus18:19 Never have sexual intercourse with a woman while she is unclean during her monthly period.
Leviticus18:20 Never have sexual intercourse with your neighbor's wife and become unclean with her.
Leviticus18:21 Never give your children as sacrifices to the god Molech {by burning them alive}. If you do
Leviticus18:22 Never have sexual intercourse with a man as with a woman. It is disgusting.
Leviticus18:23 Never have sexual intercourse with any animal and become unclean with it. A woman must never offer herself to an animal for sexual intercourse. It is unnatural.
Leviticus18:24 Do not become unclean in any of these ways. By these practices all the nations which I am forcing out of your way have become unclean.
Leviticus18:25 The land has become unclean. I will punish it for its sins. The land will vomit out those who live in it.
Leviticus18:26 Live by my standards
Leviticus18:27 The people of the land who were there before you did all these disgusting things. As a result
Leviticus18:28 If you make the land unclean
Leviticus18:29 Whoever does any of these disgusting things must be excluded from the people.
Leviticus18:30 So you must follow my instructions. Don't live by the standards of the people who lived there before you. What they do is disgusting. Never become unclean that way. I am the Lord your God.
Leviticus19:1 The Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus19:2 Tell the whole congregation of Israel: Be holy because I
Leviticus19:3 Respect your mother and father. Observe my days of worship. I am the Lord your God.
Leviticus19:4 Don't turn to worthless gods or cast metal idols. Never make any gods for yourselves. I am the Lord your God.
Leviticus19:5 When you bring a fellowship offering to the Lord
Leviticus19:6 Eat your sacrifice on the day you bring it and on the next day. On the third day burn whatever is left over.
Leviticus19:7 If you eat any of it on the third day
Leviticus19:8 Those who eat it will be punished because they have dishonored what is holy to the Lord. They must be excluded from the people.
Leviticus19:9 When you harvest the grain in your land
Leviticus19:10 Don't harvest your vineyard a second time or pick up fallen grapes. Leave them for poor people and foreigners. I am the Lord your God.
Leviticus19:11 Never steal
Leviticus19:12 Never swear by my name in order to deceive anyone. This dishonors the name of your God. I am the Lord.
Leviticus19:13 Never oppress or rob your neighbor. Never keep the pay you owe a hired worker overnight.
Leviticus19:14 Never curse deaf people or put anything in the way of blind people to make them stumble. Instead
Leviticus19:15 Don't be corrupt when administering justice. Never give special favors to poor people
Leviticus19:16 Never gossip. Never endanger your neighbor's life. I am the Lord.
Leviticus19:17 Never hate another Israelite. Be sure to correct your neighbor so that you will not be guilty of sinning along with him.
Leviticus19:18 Never get revenge. Never hold a grudge against any of your people. Instead
Leviticus19:19 Obey my laws. Never crossbreed different kinds of animals. Never plant two kinds of crops in your field. Never wear clothes made from two kinds of material.
Leviticus19:20 If a man has sexual intercourse with a female slave who is engaged to another man and if her freedom was never bought or given to her
Leviticus19:21 He must bring a ram for his guilt offering to the Lord at the entrance to the tent of meeting.
Leviticus19:22 In the Lord's presence the priest will use them to make peace with the Lord for this sin. The man will be forgiven for this sin.
Leviticus19:23 When you come into the land and plant all kinds of fruit trees
Leviticus19:24 In the fourth year all the fruit will be a holy offering of praise to the Lord.
Leviticus19:25 In the fifth year you may eat the fruit. Do this to make the trees produce more for you. I am the Lord your God.
Leviticus19:26 Never eat any meat with blood still in it. Never cast evil spells
Leviticus19:27 Never shave the hair on your foreheads
Leviticus19:28 Never slash your body to mourn the dead
Leviticus19:29 Never dishonor your daughter by making her a prostitute
Leviticus19:30 Observe my days of worship and respect my holy tent. I am the Lord.
Leviticus19:31 Don't turn to psychics or mediums to get help. That will make you unclean. I am the Lord your God.
Leviticus19:32 Show respect to the elderly
Leviticus19:33 Never mistreat a foreigner living in your land.
Leviticus19:34 Foreigners living among you will be like your own people. Love them as you love yourself
Leviticus19:35 Don't be corrupt when administering justice concerning length
Leviticus19:36 Use honest scales
Leviticus19:37 Obey all my laws and all my rules
Leviticus20:1 The Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus20:2 Tell the Israelites: If Israelites or foreigners living among you give one of their children as a sacrifice to {the god} Molech
Leviticus20:3 I will condemn them and exclude them from the people. They gave one of their children to Molech
Leviticus20:4 If the common people ignore those who give their children to Molech and do not put them to death
Leviticus20:5 I will condemn them and their families. I will exclude them from the people. I will exclude from the people everyone who chases after Molech as if he were a prostitute.
Leviticus20:6 I will condemn people who turn to mediums and psychics and chase after them as though they were prostitutes. I will exclude them from the people.
Leviticus20:7 Live holy lives. Be holy because I am the Lord your God.
Leviticus20:8 Obey my laws
Leviticus20:9 Whoever curses his father or mother must be put to death. He has cursed his father or mother and deserves to die.
Leviticus20:10 If a man commits adultery with another man's wife or with his neighbor's wife
Leviticus20:11 Whoever has sexual intercourse with his father's wife has violated his father's marriage. Both he and his father's wife must be put to death. They deserve to die.
Leviticus20:12 If a man has sexual intercourse with his daughter-in-law
Leviticus20:13 When a man has sexual intercourse with another man as with a woman
Leviticus20:14 When a man marries a woman and her mother
Leviticus20:15 A man who has sexual intercourse with an animal must be put to death. You must kill the animal
Leviticus20:16 When a woman offers herself sexually to any animal
Leviticus20:17 Whoever takes his sister
Leviticus20:18 If a man has sexual intercourse with a woman while she has her monthly period
Leviticus20:19 Never have sexual intercourse with your mother's sister or your father's sister. Whoever has sexual intercourse with a close relative must be punished.
Leviticus20:20 Whoever has sexual intercourse with his uncle's wife violates his uncle's marriage. That man and woman are guilty of sin. They will die without children.
Leviticus20:21 Whoever marries his brother's wife violates his brother's marriage and does an unclean thing. That man and woman will have no children.
Leviticus20:22 If you carefully obey all my laws and my rules
Leviticus20:23 Never follow the practices of the people I am forcing out of your way. I cannot stand them because they did all these things.
Leviticus20:24 I have told you that you will take their land. I will give it to you as your own. It is a land flowing with milk and honey. I am the Lord your God who separated you from other people.
Leviticus20:25 Separate clean and unclean animals and birds. Never become disgusting by eating any animal or bird or anything that crawls on the ground. I have separated you from every unclean thing.
Leviticus20:26 Be my holy people because I
Leviticus20:27 Every man or woman who is a medium or a psychic must be put to death. They must be stoned to death because they deserve to die.

Leviticus21:2 However
Leviticus21:3 and especially an unmarried virgin sister who is still close to you.

Leviticus21:5 You should never mourn by shaving bald spots on your heads

Leviticus21:12 He must not leave the holy tent of his God. If he does

Leviticus22:1 The Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus22:2 Tell Aaron and his sons that they must respect the holy offerings which the Israelites set apart for me. In this way they will not dishonor my holy name. I am the Lord.
Leviticus22:3 Tell them: In future generations if any of your descendants
Leviticus22:4 No descendant of Aaron who has a skin disease or a discharge may eat any of the holy offerings until he is clean. Any person who has an emission of semen or touches a dead body
Leviticus22:5 an unclean swarming creature
Leviticus22:6 will be unclean until evening. He must not eat any of the holy offerings unless he has washed himself.
Leviticus22:7 When the sun has set
Leviticus22:8 He must never eat the meat of an animal that dies naturally or is killed by wild animals. It will make him unclean. I am the Lord.
Leviticus22:9 The priests must do what I order
Leviticus22:10 Laypeople must never eat any holy offering
Leviticus22:11 But if a priest buys a slave
Leviticus22:12 However
Leviticus22:13 If a priest's daughter is widowed or divorced
Leviticus22:14 Those who eat a holy offering by mistake must give another holy offering to the priest and add one-fifth more to it.
Leviticus22:15 Priests must not dishonor the holy offerings that the Israelites contribute to the Lord.
Leviticus22:16 They must make those people pay the penalty for their guilt because they have eaten the priests' holy offerings. I am the Lord

Leviticus22:18 Tell Aaron
Leviticus22:19 The offering must be a male that has no defects from your cattle
Leviticus22:20 Never bring any animal with a physical defect
Leviticus22:21 A person may bring the Lord a fellowship offering to fulfill a vow or for a freewill offering. Whether it is from the cattle
Leviticus22:22 Never bring the Lord an animal that is blind
Leviticus22:23 You may use a bull or a sheep with a deformity or one that is stunted in growth as a freewill offering. However
Leviticus22:24 Never bring the Lord an animal that has bruised
Leviticus22:25 Never bring any kind of castrated animal received from a foreigner as a food offering for your God. A castrated animal will not be accepted on your behalf because castration is a physical defect.
