Leviticus22:27 When a calf
Leviticus22:28 Never slaughter a cow or a sheep and its young the same day.
Leviticus22:29 When you sacrifice a thank offering to the Lord
Leviticus22:30 Eat it the same day. Never leave any of it until morning. I am the Lord.
Leviticus22:31 Carefully obey my commands. I am the Lord.
Leviticus22:32 Never dishonor my holy name. I will show my holiness among the Israelites. I am the Lord
Leviticus22:33 I brought you out of Egypt to be your God. I am the Lord.
Leviticus23:1 The Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus23:2 Tell the Israelites: These are the appointed festivals with the Lord
Leviticus23:3 You may work for six days. But the seventh day is a day of worship
Leviticus23:4 The following are the Lord's appointed festivals with holy assemblies
Leviticus23:5 The fourteenth day of the first month
Leviticus23:6 The fifteenth day of this same month is the Lord's Festival of Unleavened Bread. For seven days you must eat unleavened bread.
Leviticus23:7 On the first day there will be a holy assembly. Don't do any regular work.
Leviticus23:8 Bring the Lord a sacrifice by fire for seven days. On the seventh day there will be a holy assembly. Don't do any regular work.
Leviticus23:9 The Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus23:10 Tell the Israelites: When you come to the land I am going to give you and you harvest grain
Leviticus23:11 He will present it to the Lord so that you will be accepted. He will present it on the day after Passover.
Leviticus23:12 On the day you present the bundle
Leviticus23:13 Bring a grain offering of four quarts of flour mixed with olive oil with it. This will be a sacrifice by fire made to the Lord
Leviticus23:14 Don't eat bread
Leviticus23:15 Count seven full weeks from the day after Passover (the day you bring the bundle of grain as an offering presented to the Lord)
Leviticus23:16 until the day after the seventh week. This is a total of fifty days. Then bring a new grain offering to the Lord.
Leviticus23:17 Bring two loaves of bread from your homes to present to the Lord. Bake them with four quarts of flour. They are the first harvested grain for the Lord.
Leviticus23:18 With the bread bring seven one-year-old lambs that have no defects
Leviticus23:19 Also sacrifice one male goat as an offering for sin and two one-year-old lambs as a fellowship offering.
Leviticus23:20 The priest must present them along with the bread of the first harvested grain as an offering to the Lord. All this
Leviticus23:21 Make an announcement that there will be a holy assembly on that same day. Don't do any regular work. It is a permanent law for generations to come wherever you live.
Leviticus23:22 When you harvest the grain in your land
Leviticus23:23 The Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus23:24 Tell the Israelites: On the first day of the seventh month hold a worship festival. It will be a memorial day
Leviticus23:25 Don't do any regular work. Bring a sacrifice by fire to the Lord.
Leviticus23:26 The Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus23:27 In addition
Leviticus23:28 Don't do any work that day. It is a special day for the payment for sins. It is a time when you make peace with the Lord your God.
Leviticus23:29 Those who do not humble themselves on that day will be excluded from the people.
Leviticus23:30 I will kill those who do any work on that day.
Leviticus23:31 Don't do any work. It is a permanent law for generations to come wherever you live.
Leviticus23:32 It is a day of worship
Leviticus23:33 The Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus23:34 Tell the Israelites: The fifteenth day of this seventh month is the Festival of Booths to the Lord. It will last seven days.
Leviticus23:35 On the first day there will be a holy assembly. Don't do any regular work.
Leviticus23:36 For seven consecutive days bring a sacrifice by fire to the Lord. On the eighth day there will be a holy assembly. Bring the Lord a sacrifice by fire. This is the last festival of the year. Don't do any regular work.
Leviticus23:37 These are the Lord's appointed festivals. Announce them as holy assemblies for bringing sacrifices by fire to the Lord. Bring burnt offerings
Leviticus23:38 This is in addition to the Lord's days of worship
Leviticus23:39 However
Leviticus23:40 On the first day take the best fruits
Leviticus23:41 It is the Lord's festival. Celebrate it for seven days each year. This is a permanent law for generations to come. Celebrate this festival in the seventh month.
Leviticus23:42 Live in booths for seven days. Everyone born in Israel must live in booths
Leviticus23:43 so that generations to come may learn how I made the people of Israel live in booths when I brought them out of Egypt. I am the Lord your God.
Leviticus23:44 So Moses told the Israelites about the Lord's appointed festivals.
Leviticus24:1 The Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus24:2 Command the Israelites to bring you pure
Leviticus24:3 In the tent of meeting
Leviticus24:4 Aaron must keep the lamps on the pure gold lamp stand lit in the Lord's presence.
Leviticus24:5 Also take flour and bake twelve rings of bread. Each ring will contain four quarts of flour.
Leviticus24:6 Put them in two stacks of six each on the gold table in the Lord's presence.
Leviticus24:7 Lay pure incense on top of each stack. The incense on the bread will be a reminder
Leviticus24:8 Every day of worship {a priest} must arrange the bread in the Lord's presence. It is a continual reminder of my promise to the Israelites.
Leviticus24:9 The bread will belong to Aaron and his sons. They will eat it in a holy place. It is very holy
Leviticus24:10 A man
Leviticus24:11 The Israelite woman's son began cursing the Lord's name and treating it with contempt. So they brought him to Moses.
Leviticus24:12 They kept him in custody until the Lord told them what to do.
Leviticus24:13 The Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus24:14 The man who cursed {my name} must be taken outside the camp. All who heard him curse {my name} must lay their hands on his head. Then the whole congregation must stone him to death.
Leviticus24:15 Also tell the Israelites: Those who treat their God with contempt will be punished for their sin.
Leviticus24:16 But those who curse the Lord's name must be put to death. The whole congregation must stone them to death. It makes no difference whether they are Israelites or foreigners. Whoever curses the Lord's name must die.
Leviticus24:17 Whoever kills another person must be put to death.
Leviticus24:18 Whoever kills an animal must replace it
Leviticus24:19 Whoever injures a neighbor must receive the same injury in return—
Leviticus24:20 a broken bone for a broken bone
Leviticus24:21 Whoever kills an animal must replace it. Whoever kills a person must be put to death.
Leviticus24:22 The same rule applies to every one of you. It makes no difference whether you are a foreigner or an Israelite
Leviticus24:23 Moses spoke to the people of Israel. So the man who had cursed the Lord's name was taken outside the camp. There they stoned him to death as the Lord commanded Moses. The Israelites did as the Lord commanded Moses.
Leviticus25:1 The Lord spoke to Moses on Mount Sinai
Leviticus25:2 Tell the Israelites: When you come into the land I'm giving you
Leviticus25:3 Then
Leviticus25:4 However
Leviticus25:5 Don't harvest what grows by itself or harvest grapes from your vines. That year will be a festival for the land.
Leviticus25:6 Whatever the land produces during that year is for all of you to eat—for you
Leviticus25:7 your animals
Leviticus25:8 Count seven of these years seven times for a total of 49 years.
Leviticus25:9 On the tenth day of the seventh month
Leviticus25:10 Set apart the fiftieth year as holy
Leviticus25:11 That fiftieth year will be your jubilee year. Don't plant or harvest what grows by itself or pick grapes from the vines in the land.
Leviticus25:12 The jubilee {year} will be holy to you. You will eat what the field itself produces.
Leviticus25:13 In this jubilee year every slave will be freed in order to return to his property.
Leviticus25:14 If you sell anything to your neighbor or buy anything from him
Leviticus25:15 When you buy property from your neighbor
Leviticus25:16 If there are still many years {until the jubilee}
Leviticus25:17 Never take advantage of each other. Fear your God
Leviticus25:18 Obey my laws
Leviticus25:19 The land will give you its products
Leviticus25:20 You may ask
Leviticus25:21 I will give you my blessing in the sixth year so that the land will produce enough for three years.
Leviticus25:22 You will plant {again} in the eighth year but live on what the land already produced. You will eat it
Leviticus25:23 Land must never be sold permanently
Leviticus25:24 People must always have the right to buy their property back.
Leviticus25:25 If your brother becomes poor and sells some of his property
Leviticus25:26 If a man doesn't have anyone to buy it back for him
Leviticus25:27 he must count the years from its sale. Then he will pay what is left to the man to whom he sold it
Leviticus25:28 However
Leviticus25:29 If anyone sells a home in a walled city
Leviticus25:30 If he does not buy it back during that year
Leviticus25:31 However
Leviticus25:32 The Levites always have the right to buy back their property in the cities they own.
Leviticus25:33 If any Levite buys back {a house}
Leviticus25:34 But a field that belongs to their cities must not be sold
Leviticus25:35 If an Israelite becomes poor and cannot support himself
Leviticus25:36 Don't collect interest or make any profit from him. Fear your God by respecting other Israelites' lives.
Leviticus25:37 Never collect any kind of interest on your money or on the food you give them.
Leviticus25:38 I am the Lord your God
Leviticus25:39 If an Israelite becomes poor and sells himself to you
Leviticus25:40 He will be like a hired worker or a visitor to you. He may work with you until the year of jubilee.
Leviticus25:41 Then you will release him and his children to go back to their family and the property of their ancestors.
Leviticus25:42 They are my servants. I brought them out of Egypt. They must never be sold as slaves.
Leviticus25:43 Do not treat them harshly. Fear your God.
Leviticus25:44 You may have male and female slaves
Leviticus25:45 You may also buy them from the foreigners living among you and from their families born in your country. They will be your property.
Leviticus25:46 You may acquire them for yourselves and for your descendants as permanent property. You may work them as slaves. However
Leviticus25:47 Someone who is a foreigner without a permanent home among you may become rich
Leviticus25:48 After he has sold himself
Leviticus25:49 His uncle
Leviticus25:50 Then he and his buyer must take into account the number of years from the year he was bought until the year of jubilee. His sale price will be adjusted based on the number of years he was with his buyer
Leviticus25:51 If there are many years left
Leviticus25:52 If there are only a few years left until the year of jubilee
Leviticus25:53 During those years he should serve his buyer as a hired worker. His buyer should not treat him harshly.
Leviticus25:54 If he cannot buy his freedom in these ways
Leviticus25:55 The Israelites belong to me as servants. They are my servants. I brought them out of Egypt. I am the Lord your God.
Leviticus26:1 {The Lord continued
Leviticus26:2 Observe my days of worship and respect my holy tent. I am the Lord.
Leviticus26:3 This is what I will do if you will live by my laws and carefully obey my commands:
Leviticus26:4 I will give you rain at the right time. The land will produce its crops
Leviticus26:5 Threshing time will last until grape gathering
Leviticus26:6 I will bring peace to your land. You will lie down with no one to scare you. I will remove dangerous animals
Leviticus26:7 You will chase your enemies
Leviticus26:8 Five of you will chase a hundred of them
Leviticus26:9 and I will be pleased with you. Your families will be large
Leviticus26:10 You will clear out old food supplies to make room for new ones.
Leviticus26:11 I will put my tent among you
Leviticus26:12 So I will live among you and be your God
Leviticus26:13 I am the Lord your God. I brought you out of Egypt so that you are no longer slaves of the Egyptians. I have broken their power over you and made you live as a free people.
Leviticus26:14 If you will not listen to me and obey all these commands
Leviticus26:15 if you reject my laws and look at my rules with disgust
Leviticus26:16 then this is what I will do to you: I will terrorize you with disease and fever. You will suffer from eye problems and depression. You will plant your crops and get nothing because your enemies will eat them.
Leviticus26:17 I will condemn you so that you will go down in defeat in front of your enemies. Those who hate you will be your rulers. You will run away even when no one is chasing you.
Leviticus26:18 If you still will not listen to me
Leviticus26:19 I will crush your arrogance. You will have no rain
Leviticus26:20 You will work hard for nothing because your land will produce no crops and the trees will produce no fruit.
Leviticus26:21 If you resist and don't listen to me
Leviticus26:22 I will send wild animals among you. They will rob you of your children
Leviticus26:23 If this discipline does not help and you still resist
Leviticus26:24 then I
Leviticus26:25 I will bring war on you to get revenge for my promise {that you rejected}. When you gather in your cities
Leviticus26:26 I will destroy your food supply. Ten women will need only one oven to prepare your food. You will eat and go away hungry.
Leviticus26:27 If in spite of this you do not listen to me and still resist me
Leviticus26:28 I will fiercely resist you. I will discipline you seven times for your sins.
Leviticus26:29 You will eat the bodies of your sons and daughters.
Leviticus26:30 I will destroy your worship sites
Leviticus26:31 I will make your cities deserted and ruin your sacred places. I will no longer accept the soothing aroma from your sacrifices.
Leviticus26:32 I will make your land so deserted that your enemies will be shocked as they settle in it.
Leviticus26:33 I will scatter you among the nations. War will follow you. Your country will be in ruins. Your cities will be deserted.
Leviticus26:34 Then the land will enjoy its time {to honor the Lord} while it lies deserted and you are in your enemies' land. Then the land will joyfully celebrate its time {to honor the Lord}.
Leviticus26:35 All the days it lies deserted
Leviticus26:36 I will fill with despair those who are left in the land of their enemies. The sound of a windblown leaf will make them run. They will run away and fall
Leviticus26:37 They will stumble over each other
Leviticus26:38 They will be destroyed among the nations. The land of their enemies will devour them.
Leviticus26:39 Those who are left will waste away in the lands of their enemies because of their sins and the sins of their ancestors.
Leviticus26:40 But if they confess their sins and the sins of their ancestors—the treacherous things they did to oppose me—
Leviticus26:41 I will oppose them and bring them into the lands of their enemies. Then
Leviticus26:42 I will remember my promise to Jacob
Leviticus26:43 The land
Leviticus26:44 Even when they are in the land of their enemies
Leviticus26:45 But for their sake
Leviticus26:46 These are the laws

Leviticus27:24 In the jubilee year the field will go back to the person from whom it was bought

Numbers1:1 The Lord spoke to Moses in the tent of meeting in the Desert of Sinai. It was the first day of the second month in the second year after leaving Egypt. He said
Numbers1:2 Take a census of the whole community of Israel by families and households. List every man by name
Numbers1:3 who is at least 20 years old. You and Aaron must register everyone in Israel who is eligible for military duty. List them by divisions.
Numbers1:4 One man from each tribe will help you. Each of these men must be the head of a household.
Numbers1:5 Here are the names of the men who will help you: Elizur
Numbers1:6 Shelumiel
Numbers1:7 Nahshon
Numbers1:8 Nethanel
Numbers1:9 Eliab
Numbers1:10 Elishama
Numbers1:11 Abidan
Numbers1:12 Ahiezer
Numbers1:13 Pagiel
Numbers1:14 Eliasaph
Numbers1:15 Ahira
Numbers1:16 These were the men chosen from the community
Numbers1:17 Moses and Aaron took the men who had been named
Numbers1:18 and assembled the whole community on the first day of the second month. Each man at least 20 years old provided his genealogy by family and household. Then his name was listed.
Numbers1:19 So Moses registered the men of Israel in the Desert of Sinai as the Lord had commanded him.
Numbers1:20 The roster of families and households for the descendants of Reuben
Numbers1:21 The total for the tribe of Reuben was 46
Numbers1:22 The roster of families and households for the descendants of Simeon registered and listed every man by name who was at least 20 years old and eligible for military duty.
Numbers1:23 The total for the tribe of Simeon was 59
Numbers1:24 The roster of families and households for the descendants of Gad listed the men by name who were at least 20 years old and eligible for military duty.
Numbers1:25 The total for the tribe of Gad was 45
Numbers1:26 The roster of families and households for the descendants of Judah listed the men by name who were at least 20 years old and eligible for military duty.
Numbers1:27 The total for the tribe of Judah was 74
Numbers1:28 The roster of families and households for the descendants of Issachar listed the men by name who were at least 20 years old and eligible for military duty.
Numbers1:29 The total for the tribe of Issachar was 54
Numbers1:30 The roster of families and households for the descendants of Zebulun listed the men by name who were at least 20 years old and eligible for military duty.
Numbers1:31 The total for the tribe of Zebulun was 57
Numbers1:32 The roster of families and households for the descendants of Joseph—those from Ephraim—listed the men by name who were at least 20 years old and eligible for military duty.
Numbers1:33 The total for the tribe of Ephraim was 40
Numbers1:34 The roster of families and households for the descendants of Manasseh listed the men by name who were at least 20 years old and eligible for military duty.
Numbers1:35 The total for the tribe of Manasseh was 32
Numbers1:36 The roster of families and households for the descendants of Benjamin listed the men by name who were at least 20 years old and eligible for military duty.
Numbers1:37 The total for the tribe of Benjamin was 35
Numbers1:38 The roster of families and households for the descendants of Dan listed the men by name who were at least 20 years old and eligible for military duty.
Numbers1:39 The total for the tribe of Dan was 62
Numbers1:40 The roster of families and households for the descendants of Asher listed the men by name who were at least 20 years old and eligible for military duty.
Numbers1:41 The total for the tribe of Asher was 41
Numbers1:42 The roster of families and households for the descendants of Naphtali listed the men by name who were at least 20 years old and eligible for military duty.
Numbers1:43 The total for the tribe of Naphtali was 53
Numbers1:44 Moses
Numbers1:45 So the Israelites were registered by households. The grand total of men who were at least 20 years old and eligible for military duty
Numbers1:46 was 603
Numbers1:47 But the households from the tribe of Levi were not registered along with the other Israelites.
Numbers1:48 The Lord had said to Moses
Numbers1:49 Don't register the tribe of Levi or include them in the census with the other Israelites.
Numbers1:50 Put the Levites in charge of the tent of God's words
Numbers1:51 When the tent has to be moved
Numbers1:52 The other Israelites will camp with each family in its own area under its own flag.
Numbers1:53 The Levites will camp all around the tent of God's words. In this way {the Lord} won't be angry with the community of Israel. So the Levites will be in charge of the tent of God's words.
Numbers1:54 The Israelites did everything as the Lord commanded Moses.

Numbers3:3 These are the names of Aaron's sons
Numbers3:4 Nadab and Abihu died in the Lord's presence because they offered unauthorized fire in his presence in the Desert of Sinai. They had no children. So only Eleazar and Ithamar served as priests during the lifetime of their father Aaron.
Numbers3:5 The Lord said to Moses
Numbers3:6 Bring the tribe of Levi
Numbers3:7 They will work for him and the whole community in front of the tent of meeting
Numbers3:8 They will take care of all the furnishings in the tent of meeting and work for the Israelites
Numbers3:9 Give the Levites to Aaron and his sons. The Levites will be the only Israelites given to them.
Numbers3:10 Appoint Aaron and his sons to serve as priests. Anyone else who tries to do the priests' duties must be put to death.

Numbers3:12 Out of all the Israelites

Numbers3:46 There are 273 more firstborn male Israelites than there are Levites.

Numbers5:1 The Lord said to Moses
Numbers5:2 Command the Israelites to send outside the camp anyone who has a serious skin disease or a discharge or anyone who is unclean from touching a dead body.
Numbers5:3 Send all of these unclean men and women outside the camp. They must not make this camp where I live among you unclean.
Numbers5:4 So the Israelites did as the Lord had told Moses. They sent these unclean people outside the camp.
Numbers5:5 The Lord said to Moses
Numbers5:6 Tell the Israelites: If you do something wrong to another person
Numbers5:7 you must confess your sin
Numbers5:8 But there may be no heir to whom the payment can be made. In that case
Numbers5:9 Any contribution over and above the holy offerings that the Israelites bring to the priest will belong to the priest.
Numbers5:10 Each person's holy offerings will belong to that person
Numbers5:11 The Lord said to Moses:
Numbers5:12 Speak to the Israelites and tell them: A man's wife may have been unfaithful to him
Numbers5:13 and may have had sexual intercourse with another man without her husband's knowledge. She may have kept it secret if there were no witnesses to accuse her and she wasn't caught in the act.
Numbers5:14 A husband may have a fit of jealousy and suspect his wife
Numbers5:15 He must then take his wife to the priest along with eight cups of barley flour as an offering for her. He must not pour olive oil on the flour or put frankincense on it
Numbers5:16 The priest will have the woman come forward and stand in the Lord's presence.
Numbers5:17 Then the priest will take holy water in a piece of pottery and put some dust from the floor of the tent into the water.
Numbers5:18 The priest will bring the woman into the Lord's presence and loosen her hair. In her hands he will put the offering used for a confession (that is
Numbers5:19 Then the priest will say to her
Numbers5:20 If

Numbers6:1 The Lord said to Moses
Numbers6:2 Speak to the Israelites and tell them: A man or a woman may make a special vow to live as a Nazirite dedicated to the Lord.
Numbers6:3 Nazirites must never drink wine
Numbers6:4 As long as they are Nazirites
Numbers6:5 As long as they are under the Nazirite vow
Numbers6:6 While they are dedicated to the Lord as Nazirites
Numbers6:7 Even if their own father
Numbers6:8 As long as they are Nazirites
Numbers6:9 Someone might suddenly drop dead next to a Nazirite and make the Nazirite's hair unclean. Seven days later he must shave his head in order to be declared clean.
Numbers6:10 On the eighth day he must bring two mourning doves or two young pigeons to the priest at the entrance to the tent of meeting.
Numbers6:11 The priest will sacrifice one as an offering for sin and the other one as a burnt offering. The priest will make peace with the Lord for the person who touched the dead body. That same day the person must dedicate his head again.
Numbers6:12 Once again he will dedicate himself to the Lord as a Nazirite for the same length of time as before. He must bring a one-year-old male lamb as an offering for guilt. The first time period won't count. He has to start over from when he became unclean.
Numbers6:13 These are the instructions for Nazirites who complete their vows: They must come to the entrance of the tent of meeting.
Numbers6:14 They must bring these offerings to the Lord: a one-year-old male lamb as a burnt offering
Numbers6:15 They must also bring a basket of unleavened bread containing some rings of bread made with olive oil and wafers of unleavened bread brushed with olive oil
Numbers6:16 The priest will bring these offerings to the Lord and make the offering for sin and the burnt offering.
Numbers6:17 He will sacrifice the ram as a fellowship offering to the Lord
Numbers6:18 Then the Nazirites will shave their heads at the entrance to the tent of meeting
Numbers6:19 Then the priest will take one of the shoulders from a boiled ram
Numbers6:20 The priest will present them as an offering to the Lord. They are holy and belong to the priest
Numbers6:21 These are the instructions for those who have vowed to bring their offerings to the Lord because they were Nazirites. They must bring these offerings in addition to anything else they can afford. They must fulfill the requirements of these instructions for Nazirites and finish whatever they vowed to do.

Numbers15:4 Whoever brings the offering must also give the Lord a grain offering of eight cups of flour mixed with one quart of olive oil.
Numbers15:5 With each sheep or goat for the burnt offering or any other sacrifice
Numbers15:6 With a ram
Numbers15:7 and an offering of 1 1/4 quarts of wine. Offer them as a soothing aroma to the Lord.
Numbers15:8 Suppose you sacrifice a young bull as a burnt offering to the Lord or make any other kind of sacrifice—to keep a vow or as a fellowship offering.
Numbers15:9 Offer with the young bull a grain offering of 24 cups of flour mixed with two quarts of olive oil.
Numbers15:10 Also give an offering of two quarts of wine. It is an offering by fire

Numbers15:16 The instructions and rules are the same for you as well as foreigners who are living with you.

Numbers15:19 and eat any of the food from the land

Numbers15:21 For generations to come
Numbers15:22 Suppose you unintentionally do something wrong by not obeying all these commands the Lord gave Moses.
Numbers15:23 (Everything the Lord commanded you through Moses holds as true for generations to come as it did the day the Lord gave the commands.)
Numbers15:24 If it was unintentional and no one else knows about it
Numbers15:25 The priest will make peace with the Lord for the whole community of Israel. Then they will be forgiven because the wrongdoing was unintentional and they brought these two offerings to the Lord for their sin: an offering by fire and an offering for sin.
Numbers15:26 So the whole community of Israel will be forgiven
Numbers15:27 If one person unintentionally does something wrong
Numbers15:28 The priest will offer the sacrifice to make peace with the Lord for that person
Numbers15:29 You must give the same instructions to everyone who does something wrong unintentionally
Numbers15:30 But any native-born Israelite or foreigner who deliberately does something wrong insults the Lord and must be excluded from the people.
Numbers15:31 That person has despised the word of the Lord and broken the Lord's command. He must be excluded completely. He remains guilty.

Numbers19:1 The Lord said to Moses and Aaron
Numbers19:2 This is what the Lord's teachings have commanded: Tell the Israelites to bring you a red cow that is perfect
Numbers19:3 Give it to the priest Eleazar. It must be taken outside the camp and slaughtered in his presence.
Numbers19:4 The priest Eleazar will take some of the blood with his finger and sprinkle it seven times toward the front of the tent of meeting.
Numbers19:5 Then the entire cow (the skin
Numbers19:6 The priest will take some cedar wood
Numbers19:7 The priest must then wash his clothes and his body. After that
Numbers19:8 The person who burned the calf must also wash his clothes and his body. He
Numbers19:9 A man who is clean will collect the ashes from the cow and put them in a clean place outside the camp. They will be kept by the community of Israel and used in the water that takes away uncleanness. The cow is an offering for sin.
Numbers19:10 The person who collected the ashes from the cow must also wash his clothes. He will be unclean until evening. This will be a permanent law for the Israelites and for the foreigners who live with them.
Numbers19:11 Whoever touches the dead body of any human being will be unclean for seven days.
Numbers19:12 The unclean person must use this water on the third day and the seventh day to take away his sin. Then he will be clean. But if he doesn't use this water on the third day and the seventh day
Numbers19:13 Whoever touches the dead body of a human being and doesn't use this water to take away his sin makes the Lord's tent unclean. That person must be excluded from Israel
Numbers19:14 These are your instructions for when a person dies in a tent: Everyone who goes into the tent and everyone who is in the tent will be unclean for seven days.
Numbers19:15 Every container without a lid fastened on it is unclean.
Numbers19:16 Whoever is outdoors and touches someone who was killed or has died naturally or anyone who touches a human bone or a grave will be unclean for seven days.
Numbers19:17 {This is what you must do} for people who become unclean from touching a dead body. Put some of the ashes from the red cow that was burned as an offering for sin into a container. Then pour fresh water on them.
Numbers19:18 A person who is clean will take a sprig of hyssop
Numbers19:19 A person who is clean will sprinkle these types of unclean people on the third day and the seventh day. On the seventh day the clean person will finish taking away their sins. Then they must wash their clothes and bodies
Numbers19:20 But if the person who becomes unclean doesn't have his sin taken away
Numbers19:21 This will be a permanent law for them. Whoever sprinkles the water to take away uncleanness must wash his clothes. And whoever touches this water will be unclean until evening.
Numbers19:22 Anything that an unclean person touches becomes unclean

Numbers28:1 The Lord said to Moses
Numbers28:2 Give this command to the Israelites: Be sure to bring me my offerings at the right times. They are my food. They are offerings by fire
Numbers28:3 These are the offerings by fire that you must bring to the Lord. Every day you must bring as a daily burnt offering two one-year-old lambs that have no defects.
Numbers28:4 Offer one in the morning and the other at dusk.
Numbers28:5 {With each of them} also bring a grain offering of eight cups of flour mixed with one quart of virgin olive oil.
Numbers28:6 This is the daily burnt offering which was established on Mount Sinai. This offering is a soothing aroma
Numbers28:7 Also bring a wine offering of one quart of wine for each lamb. Pour it out to the Lord in a holy place.
Numbers28:8 Offer the other lamb at dusk along with the same grain offering and wine offering as you brought in the morning. This is an offering by fire
Numbers28:9 On the day of worship offer two one-year-old lambs that have no defects
Numbers28:10 This burnt offering is for every day of worship in addition to the daily burnt offerings and the wine offerings that go with them.
Numbers28:11 On the first of every month bring the Lord a burnt offering of two young bulls
Numbers28:12 With each bull there will be a grain offering of 24 cups of flour mixed with olive oil
Numbers28:13 and with each one-year-old lamb a grain offering of 8 cups of flour mixed with olive oil. This is a burnt offering
Numbers28:14 The wine offering that goes with each bull will be 2 quarts of wine
Numbers28:15 In addition to the daily burnt offering with its wine offering
Numbers28:16 The fourteenth day of the first month is the Lord's Passover.
Numbers28:17 The fifteenth of this same month is a pilgrimage festival. For seven days you must eat only unleavened bread.
Numbers28:18 On the first day there will be a holy assembly. Don't do any regular work.
Numbers28:19 Instead
Numbers28:20 Along with them bring grain offerings of flour mixed with olive oil. Bring 24 cups for each bull
Numbers28:21 and 8 cups for each of the seven lambs.
Numbers28:22 Also bring one male goat as an offering for sin to make peace with the Lord.
Numbers28:23 Offer these in addition to the morning burnt offering.
Numbers28:24 Bring all these offerings on each of the seven days. They are food. They are offerings by fire
Numbers28:25 On the seventh day you must have a holy assembly. You must not do any regular work.

Numbers30:1 Moses said to the heads of the tribes of Israel
Numbers30:2 If a man makes a vow to the Lord that he will do something or swears an oath that he won't do something
Numbers30:3 A young girl
Numbers30:4 If her father says nothing to her when he hears about it
Numbers30:5 But if her father objects when he hears about it
Numbers30:6 An unmarried woman might make a vow that she will do something or carelessly promise that she won't do something. When she marries
Numbers30:7 her husband may hear about it but say nothing to her. Then her vow or oath must be kept.
Numbers30:8 But if her husband objects when he hears about it
Numbers30:9 But a widow or a divorced woman must keep her vow or her promise.
Numbers30:10 A married woman might make a vow that she will do something or swear an oath that she won't do something.
Numbers30:11 Her husband may hear about it but may say nothing and not object. Then her vow or oath must be kept.
Numbers30:12 But if her husband cancels it when he hears about it
Numbers30:13 A husband decides whether or not his wife has to keep any vow to do something or any oath to do without something.
Numbers30:14 If he says nothing to her about it day after day
Numbers30:15 But if he cancels it later
Numbers30:16 These are the laws the Lord gave Moses for husbands and wives

Numbers35:1 The Lord spoke to Moses on the plains of Moab near the Jordan River across from Jericho. He said
Numbers35:2 Tell the Israelites to give the Levites some cities from their own property. They must also give the Levites the pastureland around those cities.
Numbers35:3 Then the Levites will have cities to live in and pastureland for their cattle
Numbers35:4 The land around the cities that you give the Levites will extend 1
Numbers35:5 Outside the city measure off 3
Numbers35:6 Six of the cities you give the Levites will be cities of refuge. You must allow murderers to escape to these cities. In addition
Numbers35:7 So you will give a total of 48 cities with pastureland to the Levites.
Numbers35:8 The cities you give the Levites from the property of the other Israelites must be given based on the amount of land each tribe owns. Take more cities from larger tribes and fewer from smaller tribes.
Numbers35:9 The Lord said to Moses
Numbers35:10 Tell the Israelites: When you cross the Jordan River and enter Canaan
Numbers35:11 select certain cities to be places of refuge. Anyone who unintentionally kills another person may run to them.
Numbers35:12 These cities will be places of refuge from any relative who can avenge the death. So anyone accused of murder will not have to die until he has had a trial in front of the community.
Numbers35:13 There will be six cities you select as places of refuge
Numbers35:14 three on the east side of the Jordan River and three in Canaan.
Numbers35:15 These six cities will be places of refuge for Israelites
Numbers35:16 But if any of you uses an iron weapon to kill another person
Numbers35:17 If any of you picks up a stone as a weapon and uses it to kill another person
Numbers35:18 Or if any of you picks up a piece of wood as a weapon and uses it to kill another person
Numbers35:19 The relative who can avenge the death must make sure a murderer is put to death. When he catches up with the murderer
Numbers35:20 If any of you kills someone you hate by shoving him or by deliberately throwing something at him
Numbers35:21 or if you beat your enemy to death with your bare hands
Numbers35:22 But suppose you accidentally kill someone who wasn't your enemy. Maybe you shoved him or threw something at him but didn't mean to kill him.
Numbers35:23 Or suppose you drop a big stone
Numbers35:24 Then the community must use these rules in order to decide if you {are innocent} or if the dead person's relative can avenge the death.
Numbers35:25 {If you are innocent
Numbers35:26 But don't go outside the city of refuge you fled to.
Numbers35:27 If the relative who can avenge the death finds you outside the city of refuge and kills you
Numbers35:28 Accused murderers must stay in their city of refuge until the death of the chief priest. They may go back to their own property only after his death.
Numbers35:29 These will be the rules for future generations wherever you live.
Numbers35:30 Whoever kills another person will be put to death as a murderer only on the testimony of more than one witness. No one can be put to death on the testimony of only one witness.
Numbers35:31 Never accept a cash payment in exchange for the life of a convicted murderer who has been given the death penalty. Murderers must be put to death.
Numbers35:32 An accused murderer who has fled to a city of refuge must never go back and live on his own land before the death of the chief priest. Don't accept a cash payment to allow him to do this.
Numbers35:33 You must not pollute the land where you live. Murder is what pollutes the land. The land where a murder was committed can never make peace with the Lord except through the death of the murderer.
Numbers35:34 Never make the land where you and I live unclean. I

Deuteronomy1:33 who went ahead of you to find places for you to camp. He appeared in a column of fire at night and in a column of smoke during the day to show you which route to take.

Deuteronomy15:23 But never eat the blood. Pour it on the ground like water.
Deuteronomy16:1 Honor the Lord your God by celebrating Passover in the month of Abib. In the month of Abib the Lord your God brought you out of Egypt at night.
Deuteronomy16:2 Slaughter an animal from your flock or herd as the Passover sacrifice to the Lord your God. Do this at the place where the Lord will choose for his name to live.
Deuteronomy16:3 Never eat leavened bread with the meat from this sacrifice. Instead
Deuteronomy16:4 There should be no yeast anywhere in your land for seven days. Never leave until morning any of the meat you slaughter on the evening of the first day.
Deuteronomy16:5 You're not allowed to slaughter the animals for Passover in any of the cities the Lord your God is giving you.
Deuteronomy16:6 Instead
Deuteronomy16:7 Cook the meat
Deuteronomy16:8 For six days eat unleavened bread
Deuteronomy16:9 Count seven weeks from the time you start harvesting grain.
Deuteronomy16:10 Then celebrate the Festival of Weeks to the Lord your God. Bring a freewill offering in proportion to the blessings the Lord your God has given you.
Deuteronomy16:11 Enjoy yourselves in the presence of the Lord your God along with your sons
Deuteronomy16:12 Remember that you were slaves in Egypt
Deuteronomy16:13 After you have gathered the grain from your threshing floor and made your wine
Deuteronomy16:14 Enjoy yourselves at the festival along with your sons
Deuteronomy16:15 For seven days you will celebrate this festival dedicated to the Lord your God in the place he will choose. You will enjoy yourselves
Deuteronomy16:16 Three times a year all your men must come into the presence of the Lord your God at the place he will choose: at the Festival of Unleavened Bread
Deuteronomy16:17 Each man must bring a gift in proportion to the blessings the Lord your God has given him.
Deuteronomy16:18 Appoint judges and officers for your tribes in every city that the Lord your God is giving you. They are to judge the people fairly.
Deuteronomy16:19 Never pervert justice. Instead
Deuteronomy16:20 Strive for nothing but justice so that you will live and take possession of the land that the Lord your God is giving you.
Deuteronomy16:21 When you build the altar for the Lord your God
Deuteronomy16:22 Never set up a sacred stone. These are things the Lord your God hates.

Joshua4:21 He said to the people of Israel
Joshua4:22 the children should be told that Israel crossed the Jordan River on dry ground.
Joshua4:23 The Lord your God dried up the Jordan ahead of you until you had crossed
Joshua4:24 The Lord did this so that everyone in the world would know his mighty power and that you would fear the Lord your God every day of your life.
Joshua5:1 All the Amorite kings west of the Jordan River and all the Canaanite kings along the Mediterranean Sea heard that the Lord had dried up the Jordan River so that the Israelites could cross. So they lost heart and had no courage left to face the people of Israel.
Joshua5:2 At that time the Lord spoke to Joshua
Joshua5:3 So Joshua made flint knives and circumcised the men of Israel at the Hill of Circumcision.
Joshua5:4 This is the reason Joshua circumcised them: All the soldiers had died on the way through the desert after they left Egypt.
Joshua5:5 The men who left Egypt had been circumcised. However
Joshua5:6 For 40 years the Israelites wandered through the desert until all their soldiers who left Egypt died. They died because they disobeyed the Lord. The Lord swore that he would not let them see this land flowing with milk and honey which he had sworn to give our ancestors.
Joshua5:7 The sons who took their place had not been circumcised on the way. So Joshua circumcised them.
Joshua5:8 When all the men had been circumcised
Joshua5:9 The Lord said to Joshua
Joshua5:10 The people of Israel camped at Gilgal in the Jericho plain. There they celebrated the Passover on the evening of the fourteenth day of the month.
Joshua5:11 On the day after the Passover
Joshua5:12 The day after that
Joshua5:13 When Joshua was near Jericho
Joshua5:14 He answered
Joshua5:15 The commander of the Lord's army said to Joshua
Joshua6:1 Jericho was bolted and barred shut because the people were afraid of the Israelites. No one could enter or leave.
Joshua6:2 The Lord said to Joshua
Joshua6:3 All the soldiers will march around the city once a day for six days.
Joshua6:4 Seven priests will carry rams' horns ahead of the ark. But on the seventh day you must march around the city seven times while the priests blow their horns.
Joshua6:5 When you hear a long blast on the horn
Joshua6:6 Joshua
Joshua6:7 He told the troops
Joshua6:8 After Joshua had given orders to the troops
Joshua6:9 The armed men went ahead of the priests
Joshua6:10 Joshua ordered the troops
Joshua6:11 So the Lord's ark went around the city once. Then they went back to the camp and stayed there for the night.
Joshua6:12 Joshua got up early in the morning. The priests carried the Lord's ark.
Joshua6:13 The seven priests carrying the seven rams' horns were ahead of it. The priests blew their horns as they went. The armed men were ahead of them
Joshua6:14 They went around the city once on the second day and returned to the camp. They did this for six days.
Joshua6:15 On the seventh day they got up at dawn. They marched around the city seven times the same way they had done it before. That was the only day they marched around it seven times.
Joshua6:16 When they went around the seventh time
Joshua6:17 The city has been claimed by the Lord. Everything in it belongs to the Lord. Only the prostitute Rahab and all who are in the house with her will live because she hid the messengers we sent.
Joshua6:18 But stay away from what has been claimed by the Lord for destruction
Joshua6:19 All the silver and gold and everything made of bronze and iron are holy and belong to the Lord. They must go into the Lord's treasury.
Joshua6:20 So the troops shouted very loudly when they heard the blast of the rams' horns
Joshua6:21 They claimed everything in it for the Lord. With their swords they killed men and women
Joshua6:22 But Joshua said to the two spies
Joshua6:23 The spies went and brought out Rahab
Joshua6:24 Then Israel burned the city and everything in it. But they put the silver and gold and everything made of bronze and iron into the Lord's treasury.
Joshua6:25 Joshua spared the prostitute Rahab
Joshua6:26 At that time Joshua pronounced this curse: The Lord will curse whoever comes to rebuild the city of Jericho. It will cost him his firstborn son to lay the foundation. It will cost him his youngest son to set up the city doors.
Joshua6:27 So the Lord was with Joshua
Joshua7:1 The people of Israel proved to be disloyal about the things claimed by the Lord. Achan
Joshua7:2 Joshua sent men from Jericho to Ai. Ai is near Beth Aven
Joshua7:3 They came back to Joshua and told him
Joshua7:4 So about three thousand men were sent. However
Joshua7:5 The men of Ai killed about thirty-six of them
Joshua7:6 Joshua and the leaders of Israel tore their clothes in grief. They put dust on their heads and bowed down to the ground in front of the Lord's ark. They stayed there until evening.
Joshua7:7 Joshua said
Joshua7:8 Lord
Joshua7:9 When the Canaanites and everyone who lives in the land hears about it
Joshua7:10 The Lord said to Joshua
Joshua7:11 Israel has sinned. They have ignored the requirements that I have placed on them. They have taken what I claimed for myself and put it among their own goods. They have not only stolen
Joshua7:12 The people of Israel will not be able to defend themselves against their enemies. They will run away from their enemies because the people of Israel are now claimed for destruction. I will not be with you anymore unless you destroy what I have claimed for myself.
Joshua7:13 Get up! Tell the people
Joshua7:14 In the morning come forward by tribes {to the tent of meeting}. The tribe the Lord selects will come forward by families. Then the family the Lord selects will come forward by households
Joshua7:15 The man who is selected
Joshua7:16 Joshua got up early in the morning. He had Israel come forward by tribes. The tribe of Judah was selected.
Joshua7:17 Then he had the families of Judah come forward
Joshua7:18 Then he had Zabdi's household come forward man by man
Joshua7:19 Joshua said to Achan
Joshua7:20 Then Achan answered Joshua
Joshua7:21 I saw a fine robe from Babylonia
Joshua7:22 Joshua sent messengers
Joshua7:23 They took the loot from the tent and brought it to Joshua and all the people of Israel. Then they laid it out in the presence of the Lord.
Joshua7:24 Joshua and all Israel took Achan (son of Zerah)
Joshua7:25 Then Joshua said
Joshua7:26 They made such a large pile of stones over Achan that it is still there today. Then the Lord withdrew his burning anger. For this reason that place is still called the valley of Achor today.
Joshua8:1 The Lord said to Joshua
Joshua8:2 You will do the same thing to Ai and its king that you did to Jericho and its king. However
Joshua8:3 So Joshua and all the soldiers started to march against Ai. Joshua picked 30
Joshua8:4 with these orders: Set an ambush behind the city. Don't go very far away from the city. Everyone must be ready.
Joshua8:5 I'll approach the city with the rest of the troops. When they come out to attack us as they did the first time
Joshua8:6 They'll come out after us
Joshua8:7 you come out of hiding and capture the city. The Lord your God will hand it over to you.
Joshua8:8 When you have captured the city
Joshua8:9 So Joshua sent them out
Joshua8:10 Joshua got up early in the morning and assembled the troops. Then he and the leaders of Israel led the army to Ai.
Joshua8:11 All the troops with him marched until they were near the city. They camped north of Ai with the ravine between them and Ai.
Joshua8:12 Joshua had taken about five thousand men and had them hide between Bethel and Ai
Joshua8:13 All the troops were positioned. The main camp was north of the city
Joshua8:14 When the king of Ai saw the main camp
Joshua8:15 Joshua and all Israel pretended to be defeated. They ran away toward the desert.
Joshua8:16 All the troops in the city were called out to chase them. As they chased Joshua
Joshua8:17 Not one man was left in Ai or Bethel; they all went after Israel. So the city was left unprotected as they chased Israel.
Joshua8:18 Then the Lord said to Joshua
Joshua8:19 The men who were hiding got up as soon as he stretched out his hand. They entered the city
Joshua8:20 When the men of Ai looked back
Joshua8:21 When Joshua and all Israel saw that the men who had been hiding had captured the city and that it was going up in smoke
Joshua8:22 The men who had captured the city also came out and attacked them. The men of Ai were caught between the battle lines of Israel. So Israel attacked them on both sides. None of them survived or escaped.
Joshua8:23 But they captured the king of Ai alive and brought him to Joshua.
Joshua8:24 Israel had finished killing all the inhabitants of Ai in the fields and in the desert where they had been pursued. They put them all to death; not one person survived. Then the Israelites went back to Ai and killed everyone left there.
Joshua8:25 Twelve thousand men and women from Ai died that day.
Joshua8:26 Joshua did not lower his hand holding the spear until he had completely destroyed all the inhabitants of Ai.
Joshua8:27 Israel took the loot and the livestock of that city for themselves
Joshua8:28 So Joshua burned Ai and made it a deserted mound of ruins. It is still in ruins today.
Joshua8:29 Joshua hung the king of Ai's {dead body} on a pole and left him there until evening. When the sun went down
Joshua8:30 At that time Joshua built an altar on Mount Ebal to the Lord God of Israel.
Joshua8:31 He built an altar with uncut stones on which no iron chisels had been used. This was as the Lord's servant Moses had commanded the people of Israel in the Book of Moses' Teachings. They made burnt offerings to the Lord and sacrificed fellowship offerings on the altar.
Joshua8:32 There in front of the people of Israel he wrote on stone slabs a copy of the Teachings which Moses had written down.
Joshua8:33 All the people of Israel
Joshua8:34 Afterwards
Joshua8:35 Joshua read {Moses' Teachings} in front of the whole assembly of Israel
Joshua9:1 When all the kings west of the Jordan River heard about these events
Joshua9:2 they joined together to fight Joshua and Israel. (They were the kings in the mountains
Joshua9:3 When the people living in Gibeon heard what Joshua had done to Jericho and Ai
Joshua9:4 they devised a scheme. They posed as messengers. They took worn-out sacks on their donkeys. Their wineskins were old
Joshua9:5 Their sandals were worn-out and repaired
Joshua9:6 They came to Joshua in the camp at Gilgal. They told Joshua and the men of Israel
Joshua9:7 The men of Israel said to the Hivites
Joshua9:8 They responded to Joshua
Joshua9:9 They answered him
Joshua9:10 We also heard everything he did to the two kings of the Amorites east of the Jordan
Joshua9:11 Our leaders and everyone who lives in our country told us
Joshua9:12 Our bread was warm when we left home to meet with you. Look at it now! It's dry and crumbling.
Joshua9:13 These were new wineskins when we filled them. Look at them now! See how they are splitting! Our clothes and sandals are also worn-out because we have come such a long way.
Joshua9:14 The men believed the evidence they were shown
Joshua9:15 So Joshua made peace with them by making a treaty which allowed them to live. The leaders of the congregation swore to it with an oath.
Joshua9:16 But three days after the treaty was made
Joshua9:17 The Israelites broke camp. They came to the cities of Gibeon
Joshua9:18 The Israelites didn't destroy these other people
Joshua9:19 But all the leaders said to them
Joshua9:20 We must let them live to avoid {the Lord's} anger because of the oath we swore.
Joshua9:21 The leaders said that they should be allowed to live. So they became woodcutters and water carriers for the whole congregation
Joshua9:22 Joshua sent for the people of Gibeon and asked
Joshua9:23 You are under a curse now. You will always be servants. You will be woodcutters and water carriers for the house of my God.
Joshua9:24 They answered Joshua
Joshua9:25 Now we're at your mercy. Do to us what you think is good and right.
Joshua9:26 So Joshua rescued them and did not let the people of Israel kill them.
Joshua9:27 But that day Joshua made them woodcutters and water carriers for the congregation. They served the Lord's altar
Joshua10:1 King Adoni Zedek of Jerusalem heard that Joshua had captured Ai and claimed it for the Lord the same way he had destroyed Jericho and its king. He also heard that the people of Gibeon had made peace with the people of Israel and were living with them.
Joshua10:2 He and his people were terribly afraid because Gibeon was a large city. It was like one of the royal cities
Joshua10:3 So King Adoni Zedek of Jerusalem sent {this message} to King Hoham of Hebron
Joshua10:4 Come
Joshua10:5 So the five Amorite kings of Jerusalem
Joshua10:6 The men of Gibeon sent this message to Joshua at the camp in Gilgal: Don't abandon us! Come quickly
Joshua10:7 So Joshua
Joshua10:8 The Lord told Joshua
Joshua10:9 So Joshua marched all night from Gilgal and took them by surprise.
Joshua10:10 The Lord threw the enemy into disorder in front of Israel and defeated them decisively at Gibeon. He chased them along the road that goes to the slope of Beth Horon and continued to defeat them all the way to Azekah and Makkedah.
Joshua10:11 As they fled from the Israelites down the slope of Beth Horon toward Azekah
Joshua10:12 The day the Lord handed the Amorites over to the people of Israel
Joshua10:13 The sun stood still
Joshua10:14 Never before or after this day was there anything like it. The Lord did what a man told him to do
Joshua10:15 Then Joshua and all Israel returned to the camp at Gilgal.
Joshua10:16 The five kings ran away and hid in the cave at Makkedah.
Joshua10:17 Someone told Joshua
Joshua10:18 Joshua replied
Joshua10:19 But don't stop. Chase your enemies! Cut off their rear guard. Don't let them get back into their own cities
Joshua10:20 Joshua and the Israelites defeated them decisively
Joshua10:21 Then the whole army returned safely to Joshua in the camp at Makkedah. Not a single person dared to speak against any of the Israelites.
Joshua10:22 Joshua said
Joshua10:23 So they brought him the kings of Jerusalem
Joshua10:24 When they brought them to Joshua
Joshua10:25 Joshua told them
Joshua10:26 After this
Joshua10:27 When the sun went down
Joshua10:28 That same day Joshua captured Makkedah
Joshua10:29 Joshua and all Israel marched from Makkedah to Libnah and attacked it.
Joshua10:30 The Lord also handed Libnah and its king over to Israel. He killed all the people. There were no survivors. He did the same thing to the king of Libnah that he had done to the king of Jericho.
Joshua10:31 Joshua and all Israel marched from Libnah to Lachish
Joshua10:32 The Lord handed Lachish over to Israel. He captured it on the next day and killed all the people
Joshua10:33 At that time King Horam of Gezer had come to help Lachish. But Joshua killed him and his troops. There were no survivors.
Joshua10:34 Joshua and all Israel marched from Lachish to Eglon
Joshua10:35 They captured it that day and killed everyone in it. He claimed it for the Lord by destroying it the same way he had destroyed Lachish.
Joshua10:36 Then Joshua and all Israel marched from Eglon to Hebron and attacked it.
Joshua10:37 They captured it and its neighboring villages and killed its king and all the people. There were no survivors
Joshua10:38 Then Joshua and all Israel went back to Debir and attacked it.
Joshua10:39 He captured it and its king and all its neighboring villages and killed everyone. So they claimed them all for the Lord by destroying them. There were no survivors. He did the same thing to Debir and its king that he had done to Hebron and Libnah and their kings.
Joshua10:40 So Joshua captured the whole land—the mountains
Joshua10:41 So Joshua defeated the people from Kadesh Barnea to Gaza and from all the country of Goshen as far as Gibeon.
Joshua10:42 Joshua captured all these kings and their territories in one campaign because the Lord God of Israel fought for Israel.
Joshua10:43 Then Joshua and all Israel returned to the camp at Gilgal.
Joshua11:1 King Jabin of Hazor heard {what had happened}. So he sent messengers to King Jobab of Madon and to the kings of Shimron and Achshaph.
Joshua11:2 He also sent messengers to the northern kings in the mountains
Joshua11:3 the Canaanites from east and west
Joshua11:4 They came out with all their armies. Their troops were as numerous as the grains of sand on the seashore. They also had horses and chariots.
Joshua11:5 All these kings camped together by the Springs of Merom in order to fight Israel.
Joshua11:6 The Lord told Joshua
Joshua11:7 Joshua and all his troops arrived suddenly at the Springs of Merom and attacked the Canaanite armies.
Joshua11:8 The Lord handed them over to Israel
Joshua11:9 Joshua disabled their horses and burned their chariots
Joshua11:10 Then Joshua turned back and captured Hazor. He killed its king with a sword. (Hazor was formerly the head of all these kingdoms.)
Joshua11:11 They claimed everyone for the Lord by destroying them with swords. Not one person survived. Joshua also burned Hazor.
Joshua11:12 So Joshua captured all these cities and their kings. He claimed them for the Lord by destroying them
Joshua11:13 Israel did not burn cities built on mounds. However
Joshua11:14 The people of Israel took all the loot and livestock from these cities. But they put everyone to death until they were all destroyed. Not one person survived.
Joshua11:15 So Joshua carried out what the Lord had commanded his servant Moses and what Moses had commanded him. He did not leave out anything the Lord had commanded Moses.
Joshua11:16 Joshua took all this land
Joshua11:17 The land extended from Mount Halak which ascends to Seir as far as Baal Gad in the Lebanon Valley at the foot of Mount Hermon. He captured all their kings and killed them.
Joshua11:18 Joshua waged war with all these kings for a long time.
Joshua11:19 Not one city had made a peace treaty with the people of Israel except Gibeon
Joshua11:20 The Lord made their enemies stubborn enough to continue fighting against Israel so that he could claim them all for destruction without mercy
Joshua11:21 At that time Joshua also wiped out the people of Anak in the mountains
Joshua11:22 None of the people of Anak remained in Israel. Some of them were left in Gaza
Joshua11:23 Joshua captured the whole land as the Lord had promised Moses. He gave it to Israel as a possession
Joshua12:1 These are the kings of the land east of the Jordan River that the people of Israel defeated. Israel also took possession of their lands from the Arnon Valley to Mount Hermon
Joshua12:2 Sihon was the Amorite king who lived in Heshbon. His rule extended from Aroer on the edge of the Arnon Valley to the Jabbok River
Joshua12:3 It included the eastern plains from the Sea of Galilee to the Sea of the Plains (the Dead Sea) and the road that goes south from Beth Jeshimoth to the foot of the slopes of Pisgah.
Joshua12:4 The territory of King Og of Bashan who lived in Ashtaroth and Edrei was captured. He was the last of the Rephaim.
Joshua12:5 He ruled Mount Hermon
Joshua12:6 The Lord's servant Moses and the people of Israel defeated them. Then he gave their land as a possession to the tribes of Reuben and Gad and half of the tribe of Manasseh.

Joshua13:12 (the whole kingdom of Og in Bashan). Og ruled in Ashtaroth and Edrei. He was the last of the Rephaim. Moses had defeated them and forced them out.
Joshua13:13 But the Israelites did not force out the people of Geshur and Maacath. So they still live in Israel today.

Joshua13:28 This was Gad's inheritance for its families. It included cities with their villages.

Joshua13:31 It also included half of Gilead with Ashtaroth and Edrei
Joshua13:32 This is the land that Moses distributed on Moab's plains
Joshua13:33 Moses did not give any land as an inheritance to the tribe of Levi. The Lord God of Israel is what they inherited

Joshua14:2 The land inherited by the nine-and-a-half tribes was determined by drawing lots as the Lord had commanded through Moses.

Joshua14:4 because Joseph's descendants

Joshua14:6 Then the people of Judah came to Joshua at Gilgal. Caleb
Joshua14:7 I was 40 years old when the Lord's servant Moses sent me from Kadesh Barnea to explore the land. I reported to him exactly what I thought.
Joshua14:8 But my companions discouraged the people. However
Joshua14:9 On that day Moses swore this oath: 'The land your feet walked on will be a permanent inheritance for you and your descendants because you were completely loyal to the Lord my God.'
Joshua14:10 So look at me. The Lord has kept me alive as he promised. It's been 45 years since Israel wandered in the desert when the Lord made this promise to Moses. So now look at me today. I'm 85 years old.
Joshua14:11 I'm still as fit to go to war now as I was when Moses sent me out.

Joshua15:1 The lot was drawn for the families of the tribe of Judah. Their territory extends as far south as the territory of Edom and the desert of Zin.
Joshua15:2 The southern border starts from the south end of the Dead Sea
Joshua15:3 and goes south of the Akrabbim Pass. It then passes Zin and goes up south of Kadesh Barnea. From there it goes to Hezron
Joshua15:4 and on to Azmon. It comes out at the River of Egypt so that the border ends at the Mediterranean Sea. This is the southern border.
Joshua15:5 The eastern border is the Dead Sea as far north as the mouth of the Jordan River. The northern border starts from the north end of the Dead Sea at the mouth of the Jordan
Joshua15:6 and goes up to Beth Hoglah. It then passes north to Beth Arabah and goes up to the Rock of Bohan
Joshua15:7 From the valley of Achor
Joshua15:8 It continues up the valley of Ben Hinnom to the south slope of the Jebusite city Jerusalem. It then goes to the top of the mountain that overlooks the valley of Hinnom to the west at the north end of the valley of Rephaim.
Joshua15:9 From the top of that mountain the border goes around to the spring of Nephtoah. From there it goes to the cities of Mount Ephron and around to Baalah (now called Kiriath Jearim).
Joshua15:10 From Baalah the border turns west to Mount Seir and over to the north slope of Mount Jearim (now called Chesalon). Then it goes down to Beth Shemesh and on to Timnah.
Joshua15:11 From there the border goes on the north side of Ekron and turns to Shikkeron
Joshua15:12 The western border is the coastline of the Mediterranean Sea. These are the borders around Judah that belong to their families.

Joshua18:1 The whole congregation of Israel gathered at Shiloh and set up the tent of meeting there. The land was under their control.
Joshua18:2 There were still seven tribes in Israel who had not yet received any land as their inheritance.

Joshua18:4 Choose three men from each tribe
Joshua18:5 They will divide the land into seven parts. Judah will stay within its territory in the south

Joshua18:7 Levi's tribe has no separate region among you
Joshua18:8 As the men got ready to go
Joshua18:9 The men surveyed the land. They described it in a book. The land was divided into seven parts according to its cities. Then they returned to Joshua at the camp at Shiloh.
Joshua18:10 So Joshua drew lots for them in the presence of the Lord in Shiloh. There Joshua divided the land among the tribes of Israel.
Joshua18:11 The lot was drawn for the families of the tribe of Benjamin. Their territory lies between Judah's and Joseph's.
Joshua18:12 Their northern border starts at the Jordan
Joshua18:13 From there the border goes to the south slope of Luz (now called Bethel). Then the border goes down to Ataroth Addar over the mountains south of Lower Beth Horon.
Joshua18:14 The border turns and goes around on the west side
Joshua18:15 The southern border begins just outside Kiriath Jearim and goes west
Joshua18:16 Then the border descends to the foot of the mountain that overlooks the valley of Ben Hinnom
Joshua18:17 Then it turns north and goes to En Shemesh and from there to the region opposite the Adummim Pass. It descends to the Rock of Bohan
Joshua18:18 Then it continues on to the north side of the slope facing the plains and down into the plains.
Joshua18:19 The border continues to the north slope of Beth Hoglah and ends at the northern bay of the Dead Sea at the south end of the Jordan River. This is its southern border.
Joshua18:20 The Jordan River is its eastern border. These are the borders surrounding the inheritance given to Benjamin for its families.

Joshua23:6 Now you must be very strong to keep and to do everything written in the Book of Moses' Teachings. Don't turn away from them.
Joshua23:7 Don't get mixed up with the nations left in your territory. Don't ever mention the names of their gods or swear an oath to them. Don't ever serve their gods or bow down to them.
Joshua23:8 But you must be loyal to the Lord your God
Joshua23:9 The Lord has forced important and powerful nations out of your way. Not one person has ever been able to stand up to you.
Joshua23:10 One of you used to chase a thousand. That was because the Lord your God was fighting for you
Joshua23:11 Be very careful to love the Lord your God.

Judges1:28 When the Israelites were strong enough

Judges1:31 The tribe of Asher did not force out those who lived at Acco or Sidon
Judges1:32 So the tribe of Asher continued to live with the Canaanites because they did not force them out.
Judges1:33 The tribe of Naphtali did not force out those who lived at Beth Shemesh or Beth Anath. So they continued to live with the Canaanites. But the people of Beth Shemesh and Beth Anath were made to do forced labor.

1Kings6:1 Solomon began to build the Lord's temple 480 years after Israel left Egypt. He began building in the month of Ziv (the second month) of the fourth year of his reign over Israel.
1Kings6:2 The temple that King Solomon built for the Lord was 90 feet long
1Kings6:3 The entrance hall in front of the main room of the temple was the same length as the shorter side of the temple. It extended 15 feet in front of the temple.
1Kings6:4 He also made latticed windows for the temple.
1Kings6:5 He built an annex containing side rooms all around the temple. This annex was next to the walls of the main building and the inner sanctuary.
1Kings6:6 The {interior of} the lowest story of the annex was 7 1/2 feet wide
1Kings6:7 The temple was built with stone blocks that were finished at the quarry. No hammer
1Kings6:8 The entrance to the first story was on the south side of the temple. A staircase went up to the middle story and then to the third story.
1Kings6:9 When he had finished building the walls
1Kings6:10 He built {each story of the} annex 7 1/2 feet high alongside the entire temple. Its cedar beams were attached to the temple.

1Kings6:12 This concerns the temple you are building: If you live by my laws
1Kings6:13 I will live among the Israelites and never abandon my people.
1Kings6:14 When Solomon had finished building the temple's {frame}
1Kings6:15 he began to line the inside walls of the temple with cedar boards. He paneled the inside of the temple with wood from floor to ceiling. He covered the floor of the temple with cypress planks.
1Kings6:16 He sectioned off a 30-foot-long room at the rear of the temple with cedar boards from the floor to the rafters. He built it to serve as an inner room
1Kings6:17 The 60-foot-long room at the front of the temple served as the main hall.
1Kings6:18 Gourds and flowers were carved into the cedar paneling inside the temple. Everything was {covered with} cedar. No stone could be seen.
1Kings6:19 He prepared the inner room of the temple in order to put the ark of the Lord's promise there.
1Kings6:20 The inner room was 30 feet long
1Kings6:21 He covered the inside of the temple with pure gold. He put golden chains across the front of the inner room which was covered with gold.
1Kings6:22 He covered the entire inside of the temple with gold. He also covered the entire altar in the inner room with gold.
1Kings6:23 In the inner room he made two 15-foot-tall angels out of olive wood.
1Kings6:24 Each wing of the angels was 7 1/2 feet long. The distance from the tip of one wing to the tip of the other was 15 feet.
1Kings6:25 Both angels had a 15-foot {wingspan}. Both had the same measurements and the same shape.
1Kings6:26 Each was 15 feet high.
1Kings6:27 Solomon put the angels in the inner room of the temple. The wings of the angels extended so that the wing of one of the angels touched the one wall
1Kings6:28 He covered the angels with gold.
1Kings6:29 He carved angels
1Kings6:30 He covered the floor of the inner and outer rooms of the temple with gold.

2Kings16:1 Pekah

2Kings16:10 Then King Ahaz went to Damascus to meet King Tiglath Pileser of Assyria. He saw an altar there in Damascus. So King Ahaz sent the priest Urijah a model of the altar and a set of detailed plans.
2Kings16:11 Urijah built an altar exactly like the model King Ahaz sent from Damascus. He finished it before Ahaz returned home from Damascus.
2Kings16:12 When the king came from Damascus
2Kings16:13 He sacrificed his burnt offering and grain offering
2Kings16:14 But he moved the bronze altar dedicated to the Lord. It had been in front of the temple between his altar and the Lord's temple. Ahaz put it on the north side of his altar.
2Kings16:15 King Ahaz gave this command to the priest Urijah: On this great altar you must burn the morning burnt offerings and the evening grain offering
2Kings16:16 The priest Urijah did what King Ahaz had commanded.
2Kings16:17 King Ahaz cut off the side panels of the {bronze} stands {used in the temple} and removed the basin from each of them. He took the bronze pool down from the bronze bulls that were under it and set it on a stone base.
2Kings16:18 Ahaz removed the covered walkway used on the day of worship. This walkway had been built in the temple. He also removed the outer entrance for the king from the Lord's temple. He did this to please the king of Assyria.
2Kings16:19 Isn't everything else about Ahaz—the things he did—written in the official records of the kings of Judah?

2Kings17:2 He did what the Lord considered evil

2Kings17:5 Then the king of Assyria attacked the entire country. He attacked Samaria and blockaded it for three years.
2Kings17:6 In Hoshea's ninth year as king of Israel
2Kings17:7 The Israelites sinned against the Lord their God
2Kings17:8 and lived by the customs of the nations that the Lord had forced out of the Israelites' way. They also did what their kings wanted them to do.
2Kings17:9 The Israelites secretly did things against the Lord their God that weren't right: They built for themselves illegal places of worship in all of their cities
2Kings17:10 They set up sacred stones and poles dedicated to the goddess Asherah on every high hill and under every large tree.
2Kings17:11 At all the illegal places of worship
2Kings17:12 They served idols
2Kings17:13 The Lord had warned Israel and Judah through every kind of prophet and seer
2Kings17:14 But they refused to listen. They became as impossible to deal with as their ancestors who refused to trust the Lord their God.
2Kings17:15 They rejected his decrees
2Kings17:16 They abandoned all the commands of the Lord their God: They made two calves out of cast metal. They made a pole dedicated to the goddess Asherah. They prayed to the entire army of heaven. They worshiped Baal.
2Kings17:17 They sacrificed their sons and daughters by burning them alive. They practiced black magic and cast evil spells. They sold themselves by doing what the Lord considered evil
2Kings17:18 The Lord became so angry with Israel that he removed them from his sight. Only the tribe of Judah was left.
2Kings17:19 Even Judah didn't obey the commands of the Lord their God but lived according to Israel's customs.
2Kings17:20 So the Lord rejected all of Israel's descendants
2Kings17:21 When he tore Israel away from the family of David
2Kings17:22 The Israelites followed all the sins Jeroboam committed and never turned away from them.

2Kings17:28 So one of the priests who had been taken prisoner from Samaria went to live in Bethel. He taught them how to worship the Lord.
2Kings17:29 But each group {that settled in Samaria} continued to make its own gods. They put them at the illegal places of worship
2Kings17:30 The people from Babylon made Succoth Benoth. The people from Cuth made Nergal. The people from Hamath made Ashima.
2Kings17:31 The people from Avva made Nibhaz and Tartak. The people from Sepharvaim burned their children for Adrammelech and Anammelech
2Kings17:32 So while these people were worshiping the Lord
2Kings17:33 They worshiped the Lord but also served their own gods according to the customs of the nations from which they had come.
2Kings17:34 Today they are still following their customs

2Kings17:36 Instead

2Kings17:39 Instead
2Kings17:40 The people of Israel had refused to listen and made up their own rules

2Kings25:13 The Babylonians broke apart the bronze pillars of the Lord's temple
2Kings25:14 They took the pots
2Kings25:15 The captain of the guard took all of the incense burners and bowls that were made of gold or silver.
2Kings25:16 The bronze from the two pillars
2Kings25:17 One pillar was 27 feet high and had a bronze capital on it that was 4 1/2 feet high. The filigree and the pomegranates around the capital were all made of bronze. The second pillar and its filigree were the same.
2Kings25:18 The captain of the guard took the chief priest Seraiah
2Kings25:19 From the city he also took an army commander
2Kings25:20 Nebuzaradan
2Kings25:21 The king of Babylon executed them at Riblah in the territory of Hamath. So the people of Judah were captives when they left their land.
2Kings25:22 King Nebuchadnezzar of Babylon appointed Gedaliah
2Kings25:23 When all the army commanders and their men heard that the king of Babylon had appointed Gedaliah
2Kings25:24 Gedaliah swore an oath to them and their men. He said
2Kings25:25 In the seventh month Ishmael (son of Nethaniah and grandson of Elishama
2Kings25:26 Then people of all classes and the army commanders left for Egypt because they were afraid of the Babylonians.
2Kings25:27 On the twenty-seventh day of the twelfth month of the thirty-seventh year of the imprisonment of King Jehoiakin of Judah
2Kings25:28 He treated him well and gave him a special position higher than the other kings who were with him in Babylon.
2Kings25:29 Jehoiakin no longer wore prison clothes
2Kings25:30 The king of Babylon gave him a daily food allowance as long as he lived.

2Chronicles3:1 Solomon began to build the Lord's temple in Jerusalem on Mount Moriah
2Chronicles3:2 He began to build on the second day in the second month of the fourth year of his reign.
2Chronicles3:3 This is how Solomon laid the foundation to build God's temple. It was 90 feet long and 30 feet wide. (They used the old standard measurement.)
2Chronicles3:4 The entrance hall in front of {the main room} was 30 feet wide (the same as the width of the temple) and 30 feet high. He covered its inside walls with pure gold.
2Chronicles3:5 He paneled the larger building with cypress
2Chronicles3:6 He covered the building with gems to beautify it and used gold from Parvaim.
2Chronicles3:7 He also overlaid the building
2Chronicles3:8 He made the most holy place. It was as long as the temple was wide
2Chronicles3:9 The gold nails weighed 20 ounces. He also overlaid the upper rooms with gold.
2Chronicles3:10 In the most holy place he made two sculptured angels and covered them with gold.
2Chronicles3:11 The combined length of the angels' wings was 30 feet. A wing of one of the angels was 7 1/2 feet long and touched the wall of the building. Its other wing was 7 1/2 feet long and touched one wing of the other.
2Chronicles3:12 The wing of the other one of the angels was 7 1/2 feet long and touched the other wall of the building. Its other wing was 7 1/2 feet long and touched the wing of the first. So the angels' combined wingspan was 30 feet.
2Chronicles3:13 They stood on their feet and faced the main hall.
2Chronicles3:14 Solomon made the canopy of violet
2Chronicles3:15 He made two pillars for the front of the temple. They were 53 feet long
2Chronicles3:16 He made chains for the inner room and {also} put them on the capitals. He made 100 pomegranates and put them on the chains.
2Chronicles3:17 He set up the pillars in front of the temple

2Chronicles4:3 Under the rim were two rows of figurines shaped like bulls all around the 45-foot circumference of the pool. They were cast in metal when the pool was cast.
2Chronicles4:4 The pool was set on 12 metal bulls. Three bulls faced north
2Chronicles4:5 The pool was three inches thick. Its rim was like the rim of a cup
2Chronicles4:6 Huram also made ten basins for washing and put five on the south side and five on the north side. The priests rinsed the meat prepared for the burnt offerings in them. They used the pool to wash themselves.

2Chronicles4:8 He made ten tables and put them in the temple

This includes all his sins and unfaithfulness and the places where he built illegal worship sites and set up idols and poles dedicated to the goddess Asherah.
2Chronicles33:20 Manasseh lay down in death with his ancestors. They buried him in his own palace. His son Amon succeeded him as king.
2Chronicles33:21 Amon was 22 years old when he began to rule
2Chronicles33:22 He did what the Lord considered evil
2Chronicles33:23 He didn't humble himself in front of the Lord as his father Manasseh had humbled himself. Instead
2Chronicles33:24 His officials plotted against him and killed him in his palace.
2Chronicles33:25 Then the people of the land killed everyone who had plotted against King Amon. They made his son Josiah king in his place.
2Chronicles34:1 Josiah was 8 years old when he began to rule
2Chronicles34:2 He did what the Lord considered right. He lived in the ways of his ancestor David and never stopped living this way.
2Chronicles34:3 In the eighth year of his reign
2Chronicles34:4 He had the altars of the various Baal gods torn down. He cut down the incense altars that were above them. He destroyed the Asherah poles
2Chronicles34:5 He burned the bones of the priests on their altars. So he made Judah and Jerusalem clean.
2Chronicles34:6 In the cities of Manasseh
2Chronicles34:7 tore down the altars
2Chronicles34:8 In the eighteenth year of his reign as he was making the land and the temple clean
2Chronicles34:9 They came to the chief priest Hilkiah and gave him the money that had been brought to God's temple
2Chronicles34:10 They gave the money to the foremen who were in charge of the Lord's temple. These foremen gave it to the workmen who were restoring and repairing the temple.
2Chronicles34:11 (These workers included carpenters and builders.) They were to buy quarried stones and wood for the fittings and beams of the buildings that the kings of Judah had allowed to become run-down.
2Chronicles34:12 The men did their work faithfully under the supervision of Jahath and Obadiah (Levites descended from Merari)
2Chronicles34:13 also supervised the workers and directed all the workmen on the various jobs. Some of the Levites served as scribes
