2Chronicles34:18 Then the scribe Shaphan told the king
2Chronicles34:19 When the king heard what the Teachings said
2Chronicles34:20 Then the king gave an order to Hilkiah
2Chronicles34:21 On behalf of those who are left in Israel and Judah and me
2Chronicles34:22 So Hilkiah and the king's officials went to talk to the prophet Huldah about this matter. She was the wife of Shallum
2Chronicles34:23 She told them
2Chronicles34:24 'This is what the Lord says: I'm going to bring disaster on this place and on the people living here according to the curses written in the book that was read to the king of Judah.
2Chronicles34:25 I will do this because they have abandoned me and sacrificed to other gods in order to make me furious. Therefore
2Chronicles34:26 {Huldah added

2Chronicles34:28 That is why I'm going to bring you to your ancestors. I'm going to bring you to your grave in peace

2Chronicles34:33 Josiah got rid of all the disgusting idols throughout Israelite territory. He made all people found in Israel serve the Lord their God. As long as he lived
2Chronicles35:1 Josiah celebrated the Passover for the Lord in Jerusalem. The Passover lamb was slaughtered on the fourteenth day of the first month.
2Chronicles35:2 Josiah appointed the priests to their duties and encouraged them to serve in the Lord's temple.
2Chronicles35:3 He told the Levites
2Chronicles35:4 Get yourselves ready with the family groups of your divisions
2Chronicles35:5 Stand in the holy place representing the family divisions of your relatives
2Chronicles35:6 Slaughter the Passover lamb
2Chronicles35:7 Josiah provided the people with 33
2Chronicles35:8 His officials also voluntarily gave animals to the people
2Chronicles35:9 Conaniah and his brothers Shemaiah and Nethanel
2Chronicles35:10 So the service was prepared. The priests took their positions with the Levites according to their divisions
2Chronicles35:11 They slaughtered the Passover lambs. The priests sprinkled the blood with their hands while the Levites skinned the lambs.
2Chronicles35:12 They set aside the burnt offerings to give them to the laypeople according to their family divisions. The laypeople could then present them to the Lord as written in the Book of Moses. The Levites did the same with the bulls.
2Chronicles35:13 They roasted the Passover lambs according to the directions. They boiled the holy offerings in pots
2Chronicles35:14 Later
2Chronicles35:15 The singers (Asaph's descendants) were in their places as David
2Chronicles35:16 So everything was arranged that day for the worship of the Lord. The Passover was celebrated
2Chronicles35:17 The Israelites who were present celebrated the Passover at that time. They also celebrated the Festival of Unleavened Bread for seven days.
2Chronicles35:18 Never had a Passover like this been celebrated in Israel during the time of the prophet Samuel or the kings of Israel. They did not celebrate the Passover as Josiah celebrated it with priests
2Chronicles35:19 In the eighteenth year of Josiah's reign
2Chronicles35:20 After all this
2Chronicles35:21 But Neco sent messengers to Josiah to say
2Chronicles35:22 But Josiah would not stop his attack. He disguised himself as he went into battle. He refused to listen to Neco's words
2Chronicles35:23 Some archers shot King Josiah. The king told his officers
2Chronicles35:24 His officers took him out of the chariot and brought him to Jerusalem in his other chariot. He died and was buried in the tombs of his ancestors. All Judah and Jerusalem mourned for Josiah.
2Chronicles35:25 Jeremiah sang a funeral song about Josiah. All the male and female singers still sing funeral songs about Josiah today. This became a tradition in Israel. They are written in {the Book of} the Funeral Songs.
2Chronicles35:26 Everything else about Josiah—including his devotion to God by following what is written in the Lord's Teachings
2Chronicles35:27 and his acts from first to last—are written in the records of the kings of Israel and Judah.
2Chronicles36:1 Then people of the land took Josiah's son Jehoahaz and made him king in Jerusalem in place of his father.
2Chronicles36:2 Jehoahaz was 23 years old when he became king
2Chronicles36:3 The king of Egypt removed him from office in Jerusalem and fined the country 7
2Chronicles36:4 The king of Egypt made Jehoahaz's brother Eliakim king of Judah and Jerusalem and changed Eliakim's name to Jehoiakim. Neco took Jehoahaz away to Egypt.
2Chronicles36:5 Jehoiakim was 25 years old when he began to rule
2Chronicles36:6 King Nebuchadnezzar of Babylon attacked Jehoiakim and put him in bronze shackles to take him to Babylon.
2Chronicles36:7 Nebuchadnezzar also brought some of the utensils of the Lord's temple to Babylon. He put them in his palace in Babylon.
2Chronicles36:8 Everything else about Jehoiakim—the disgusting things he did and all the charges against him—is written in the Book of the Kings of Israel and Judah. His son Jehoiakin succeeded him as king.
2Chronicles36:9 Jehoiakin was eight years old when he began to rule as king. He was king for three months and ten days in Jerusalem. He did what the Lord considered evil.
2Chronicles36:10 In the spring King Nebuchadnezzar sent for Jehoiakin and brought him to Babylon with the valuable utensils from the Lord's temple. Nebuchadnezzar made Jehoiakin's uncle Zedekiah king of Judah and Jerusalem.
2Chronicles36:11 Zedekiah was 21 years old when he began to rule
2Chronicles36:12 He did what the Lord his God considered evil and didn't humble himself in front of the prophet Jeremiah
2Chronicles36:13 Zedekiah also rebelled against King Nebuchadnezzar. Nebuchadnezzar had made Zedekiah swear an oath of allegiance to him in God's name. But Zedekiah became so stubborn and so impossible to deal with that he refused to turn back to the Lord God of Israel.
2Chronicles36:14 All the officials
2Chronicles36:15 The Lord God of their ancestors repeatedly sent messages through his messengers because he wanted to spare his people and his dwelling place.
2Chronicles36:16 But they mocked God's messengers
2Chronicles36:17 So he had the Babylonian king attack them and execute their best young men in their holy temple. He didn't spare the best men or the unmarried women
2Chronicles36:18 He brought to Babylon each of the utensils from God's temple
2Chronicles36:19 They burned God's temple
2Chronicles36:20 The king of Babylon took those who weren't executed to Babylon to be slaves for him and his sons. They remained captives until the Persian Empire began to rule.
2Chronicles36:21 This happened so that the Lord's words spoken through Jeremiah would be fulfilled. The land had its years of rest and was made acceptable {again}. While it lay in ruins
2Chronicles36:22 The promise the Lord had spoken through Jeremiah was about to come true in Cyrus' first year as king of Persia. The Lord inspired the king to make this announcement throughout his whole kingdom and then to put it in writing.
2Chronicles36:23 This is what King Cyrus of Persia says: The Lord God of heaven has given me all the kingdoms of the world. Then he ordered me to build a temple for him in Jerusalem (which is in Judah). May the Lord God be with all of you who are his people. You may go.

Nehemiah9:1 When the Israelites assembled on the twenty-fourth day of this month
Nehemiah9:2 Those who were descendants of Israel separated themselves from all foreigners. They stood and confessed their sins as well as the wicked things their ancestors had done.
Nehemiah9:3 They stood in their places
Nehemiah9:4 Then Jeshua
Nehemiah9:5 Then the Levites—Jeshua
Nehemiah9:6 You alone are the Lord. You made heaven
Nehemiah9:7 You are the Lord
Nehemiah9:8 You found that his heart was faithful to you. You made a promise to him to give the land of the Canaanites
Nehemiah9:9 You saw how our ancestors suffered in Egypt
Nehemiah9:10 You performed miraculous signs and did amazing things to Pharaoh and all his servants and all the people in his land because you knew how arrogantly they were treating our ancestors. You made a name for yourself
Nehemiah9:11 You divided the sea in front of them so that they could walk through the sea on dry ground. You threw into deep water those who pursued your people as someone throws a stone into raging water.
Nehemiah9:12 You led them during the day by a column of smoke and during the night by a column of fire to give them light to see the way they should go.
Nehemiah9:13 You came from heaven to Mount Sinai and spoke with them from heaven. You gave them fair rules
Nehemiah9:14 You taught them about your holy day of worship. You gave them commandments
Nehemiah9:15 You gave them bread from heaven to satisfy their hunger and made water flow from a rock to quench their thirst. You told them to take possession of the land that you swore you would give them.
Nehemiah9:16 But they—our own ancestors—acted arrogantly. They became stubborn and wouldn't obey your commands.
Nehemiah9:17 They refused to listen. They forgot the miracles you performed for them. They became stubborn and appointed a leader to take them back to slavery {in Egypt}. But you are a forgiving God
Nehemiah9:18 even when they made a metal statue of a calf for themselves and said
Nehemiah9:19 But because of your endless compassion
Nehemiah9:20 You gave them your good Spirit to teach them. You didn't keep your manna to yourself. You gave them water to quench their thirst.
Nehemiah9:21 You provided for them in the desert for 40 years
Nehemiah9:22 You gave kingdoms and nations to the Israelites and assigned them their boundaries. So they took possession of the land of Sihon
Nehemiah9:23 You made their children as numerous as the stars in the sky. You brought them into the land you told their parents to enter and possess.
Nehemiah9:24 Their children took possession of the land. You defeated for them the Canaanites
Nehemiah9:25 The Israelites captured fortified cities and a rich land. They took possession of houses filled with all sorts of good things
Nehemiah9:26 But they were defiant and rebelled against you. They threw your teachings over their shoulders and killed your prophets who warned them to turn back to you. They committed outrageous sins.
Nehemiah9:27 You handed them over to their enemies
Nehemiah9:28 As soon as they felt some relief
Nehemiah9:29 You warned them in order to bring them back to your teachings
Nehemiah9:30 You were patient with them for many years. You warned them by your Spirit through your prophets. However
Nehemiah9:31 But your compassion is endless. You didn't destroy them or abandon them. You are a merciful and compassionate God.
Nehemiah9:32 And now
Nehemiah9:33 But you were fair about everything that has happened to us. You have been faithful
Nehemiah9:34 Our kings
Nehemiah9:35 When they lived in their own kingdom and enjoyed the many good things that you gave them in a vast
Nehemiah9:36 Look {at us} now. We're slaves! In the land you gave our ancestors
Nehemiah9:37 The many products {from our land} go to the kings you put over us. This is because of our sins. These kings have control over our bodies
Nehemiah9:38 We are making a binding agreement {and putting it} in writing because of all this. Our leaders

Job28:18 be exchanged for it. Wisdom is more valuable than gems.

Psalms72:15 May he live long. May the gold from Sheba be given to him. May {the people} pray for him continually. May {they} praise him all day long.
Psalms72:16 May there be plenty of grain in the land. May it wave {in the breeze} on the mountaintops
Psalms72:17 May his name endure forever. May his name continue as long as the sun {shines}. May all nations be blessed through him and call him blessed.
Psalms72:18 Thank the Lord God
Psalms72:19 Thanks be to his glorious name forever. May the whole earth be filled with his glory. Amen and amen!
Psalms72:20 The prayers by David
Psalms73:1 A psalm by Asaph. God is truly good to Israel
Psalms73:2 But my feet had almost stumbled. They had almost slipped
Psalms73:3 because I was envious of arrogant people when I saw the prosperity that wicked people enjoy.
Psalms73:4 They suffer no pain. Their bodies are healthy.
Psalms73:5 They have no drudgery in their lives like ordinary people. They are not plagued {with problems} like others.
Psalms73:6 That is why they wear arrogance like a necklace and acts of violence like clothing.
Psalms73:7 Their eyes peer out from their fat faces
Psalms73:8 They ridicule. They speak maliciously. They speak arrogantly about oppression.
Psalms73:9 They verbally attack heaven
Psalms73:10 That is why God's people turn to wickedness and swallow their words.
Psalms73:11 Then wicked people ask
Psalms73:12 Look how wicked they are! They never have a worry. They grow more and more wealthy.
Psalms73:13 I've received no reward for keeping my life pure and washing my hands of any blame.
Psalms73:14 I'm plagued {with problems} all day long
Psalms73:15 If I had said
Psalms73:16 But when I tried to understand this
Psalms73:17 Only when I came into God's holy place did I {finally} understand what would happen to them.
Psalms73:18 You put them in slippery places and make them fall into ruin.
Psalms73:19 They are suddenly destroyed. They are completely swept away by terror!
Psalms73:20 As {someone} gets rid of a dream when he wakes up
Psalms73:21 When my heart was filled with bitterness and my mind was seized {with envy}
Psalms73:22 I was stupid
Psalms73:23 Yet
Psalms73:24 With your advice you guide me
Psalms73:25 As long as I have you
Psalms73:26 My body and mind may waste away
Psalms73:27 Without a doubt
Psalms73:28 Being united with God is my highest good. I have made the Almighty Lord my refuge so that I may report everything that he has done.
Psalms74:1 A maskil by Asaph. Why

Psalms74:4 Your opponents have roared inside your meeting place. They have set up their own emblems as symbols.
Psalms74:5 Starting from its entrance
Psalms74:6 They smashed all its carved paneling with axes and hatchets.
Psalms74:7 They burned your holy place to the ground. They dishonored the place where you live among us.
Psalms74:8 They said to themselves
Psalms74:9 We no longer see miraculous signs. There are no prophets anymore. No one knows how long this will last.
Psalms74:10 How long
Psalms74:11 Why do you hold back your hand
Psalms74:12 And yet
Psalms74:13 You stirred up the sea with your own strength. You smashed the heads of sea monsters in the water.
Psalms74:14 You crushed the heads of Leviathan and gave them to the creatures of the desert for food.
Psalms74:15 You opened the springs and brooks. You dried up the ever-flowing rivers.
Psalms74:16 The day and the night are yours. You set the moon and the sun in their places.
Psalms74:17 You determined all the boundaries of the earth. You created summer and winter.
Psalms74:18 Remember how the enemy insulted you
Psalms74:19 Do not hand over the soul of your dove to wild animals. Do not forget the life of your oppressed people forever.
Psalms74:20 Consider your promise because every dark corner of the land is filled with violence.
Psalms74:21 Do not let oppressed people come back in disgrace. Let weak and needy people praise your name.

Psalms74:23 Do not forget the shouting of your opponents. Do not forget the uproar made by those who attack you.

Psalms78:1 A maskil by Asaph. Open your ears to my teachings
Psalms78:2 I will open my mouth to illustrate points. I will explain what has been hidden long ago
Psalms78:3 things that we have heard and known about
Psalms78:4 We will not hide them from our children. We will tell the next generation about the Lord's power and great deeds and the miraculous things he has done.
Psalms78:5 He established written instructions for Jacob's people. He gave his teachings to Israel. He commanded our ancestors to make them known to their children
Psalms78:6 so that the next generation would know them. Children yet to be born {would learn them}. They will grow up and tell their children
Psalms78:7 to trust God
Psalms78:8 Then they will not be like their ancestors
Psalms78:9 The men of Ephraim
Psalms78:10 They had not been faithful to God's promise. They refused to follow his teachings.
Psalms78:11 They forgot what he had done— the miracles that he had shown them.
Psalms78:12 In front of their ancestors he performed miracles in the land of Egypt
Psalms78:13 He divided the sea and led them through it. He made the waters stand up like a wall.
Psalms78:14 He guided them by a cloud during the day and by a fiery light throughout the night.
Psalms78:15 He split rocks in the desert. He gave them plenty to drink
Psalms78:16 He made streams come out of a rock. He made the water flow like rivers.
Psalms78:17 They continued to sin against him
Psalms78:18 They deliberately tested God by demanding the food they craved.
Psalms78:19 They spoke against God by saying
Psalms78:20 True
Psalms78:21 When the Lord heard this
Psalms78:22 because they did not believe God or trust him to save them.
Psalms78:23 In spite of that
Psalms78:24 He rained manna down on them to eat and gave them grain from heaven.
Psalms78:25 Humans ate the bread of the mighty ones
Psalms78:26 He made the east wind blow in the heavens and guided the south wind with his might.
Psalms78:27 He rained meat down on them like dust
Psalms78:28 He made the birds fall in the middle of his camp
Psalms78:29 They ate more than enough. He gave them what they wanted
Psalms78:30 but they still wanted more. While the food was still in their mouths
Psalms78:31 the anger of God flared up against them. He killed their strongest men and slaughtered the best young men in Israel.
Psalms78:32 In spite of all this
Psalms78:33 He brought their days to an end like a whisper in the wind. He brought their years to an end in terror.
Psalms78:34 When he killed {some of} them
Psalms78:35 They remembered that God was their rock
Psalms78:36 They flattered him with their mouths and lied to him with their tongues.
Psalms78:37 Their hearts were not loyal to him. They were not faithful to his promise.
Psalms78:38 But he is compassionate. He forgave their sin. He did not destroy them. He restrained his anger many times. He did not display all of his fury.
Psalms78:39 He remembered that they were only flesh and blood
Psalms78:40 How often they rebelled against him in the wilderness! How often they caused him grief in the desert!
Psalms78:41 Again and again they tested God
Psalms78:42 They did not remember his power— the day he freed them from their oppressor
Psalms78:43 when he performed his miraculous signs in Egypt
Psalms78:44 He turned their rivers into blood so that they could not drink from their streams.
Psalms78:45 He sent a swarm of flies that bit them and frogs that ruined them.
Psalms78:46 He gave their crops to grasshoppers and their produce to locusts.
Psalms78:47 He killed their vines with hail and their fig trees with frost.
Psalms78:48 He let the hail strike their cattle and bolts of lightning strike their livestock.
Psalms78:49 He sent his burning anger
Psalms78:50 He cleared a path for his anger. He did not spare them. He let the plague take their lives.
Psalms78:51 He slaughtered every firstborn in Egypt
Psalms78:52 But he led his own people out like sheep and guided them like a flock through the wilderness.
Psalms78:53 He led them safely. They had no fear while the sea covered their enemies.
Psalms78:54 He brought them into his holy land
Psalms78:55 He forced nations out of their way and gave them the land of the nations as their inheritance. He settled the tribes of Israel in their own tents.
Psalms78:56 They tested God Most High and rebelled against him. They did not obey his written instructions.
Psalms78:57 They were disloyal and treacherous like their ancestors. They were like arrows shot from a defective bow.
Psalms78:58 They made him angry because of their illegal worship sites. They made him furious because they worshiped idols.
Psalms78:59 When God heard
Psalms78:60 He abandoned his dwelling place in Shiloh
Psalms78:61 He allowed his power to be taken captive and handed his glory over to an oppressor.
Psalms78:62 He let swords kill his people. He was furious with those who belonged to him.
Psalms78:63 Fire consumed his best young men
Psalms78:64 His priests were cut down with swords. The widows {of his priests} could not even weep {for them}.
Psalms78:65 Then the Lord woke up like one who had been sleeping
Psalms78:66 He struck his enemies from behind and disgraced them forever.
Psalms78:67 He rejected the tent of Joseph. He did not choose the tribe of Ephraim
Psalms78:68 but he chose the tribe of Judah
Psalms78:69 He built his holy place to be like the high heavens
Psalms78:70 He chose his servant David. He took him from the sheep pens.
Psalms78:71 He brought him from tending the ewes that had lambs so that David could be the shepherd of the people of Jacob
Psalms78:72 With unselfish devotion David became their shepherd. With skill he guided them.

Psalms89:34 I will not dishonor my promise or alter my own agreement.

Psalms90:2 Before the mountains were born
Psalms90:3 You turn mortals back into dust and say
Psalms90:4 Indeed
Psalms90:5 You sweep mortals away. They are a dream. They sprout again in the morning like cut grass.
Psalms90:6 In the morning they blossom and sprout. In the evening they wither and dry up.
Psalms90:7 Indeed
Psalms90:8 You have set our sins in front of you. You have put our secret sins in the light of your presence.
Psalms90:9 Indeed
Psalms90:10 Each of us lives for 70 years— or even 80 if we are in good health. But the best of them {bring} trouble and misery. Indeed
Psalms90:11 Who fully understands the power of your anger? A person fears you more when he better understands your fury.
Psalms90:12 Teach us to number each of our days so that we may grow in wisdom.

Proverbs1:17 It does no good to spread a net within the sight of any bird.
Proverbs1:18 But these people set an ambush for their own murder. They go into hiding only to lose their lives.
Proverbs1:19 This is what happens to everyone who is greedy for unjust gain. Greed takes away his life.
Proverbs1:20 Wisdom sings her song in the streets. In the public squares she raises her voice.
Proverbs1:21 At the corners of noisy streets she calls out. At the entrances to the city she speaks her words
Proverbs1:22 How long will you gullible people love being so gullible? How long will you mockers find joy in your mocking? How long will you fools hate knowledge?

Proverbs1:27 when panic strikes you like a violent storm
Proverbs1:28 They will call to me at that time
Proverbs1:29 because they hated knowledge and did not choose the fear of the Lord.
Proverbs1:30 They refused my advice. They despised my every warning.
Proverbs1:31 They will eat the fruit of their lifestyle. They will be stuffed with their own schemes.
Proverbs1:32 Gullible people kill themselves because of their turning away. Fools destroy themselves because of their indifference.
Proverbs1:33 But whoever listens to me will live without worry and will be free from the dread of disaster.
Proverbs2:1 My son
Proverbs2:2 if you pay close attention to wisdom
Proverbs2:3 if indeed you call out for insight
Proverbs2:4 if you search for wisdom as if it were money and hunt for it as if it were hidden treasure
Proverbs2:5 then you will understand the fear of the Lord and you will find the knowledge of God.
Proverbs2:6 The Lord gives wisdom. From his mouth come knowledge and understanding.
Proverbs2:7 He has reserved priceless wisdom for decent people. He is a shield for those who walk in integrity
Proverbs2:8 in order to guard those on paths of justice and to watch over the way of his godly ones.
Proverbs2:9 Then you will understand what is right and just and fair— every good course {in life}.
Proverbs2:10 Wisdom will come into your heart. Knowledge will be pleasant to your soul.
Proverbs2:11 Foresight will protect you. Understanding will guard you.
Proverbs2:12 {Wisdom will} save you from the way of evil
Proverbs2:13 from those who abandon the paths of righteousness to walk the ways of darkness
Proverbs2:14 from those who enjoy doing evil
Proverbs2:15 Their paths are crooked. Their ways are devious.

Proverbs2:17 who leaves {her husband
Proverbs2:18 Her house sinks down to death. Her ways lead to the souls of the dead.
Proverbs2:19 None who have sex with her come back. Nor do they ever reach the paths of life.
Proverbs2:20 So walk in the way of good people and stay on the paths of righteous people.
Proverbs2:21 Decent people will live in the land. People of integrity will remain in it.
Proverbs2:22 But wicked people will be cut off from the land and treacherous people will be torn from it.

Proverbs4:26 Carefully walk a straight path
Proverbs4:27 Do not lean to the right or to the left. Walk away from evil.

Proverbs6:1 My son
Proverbs6:2 you are trapped by the words of your own mouth
Proverbs6:3 Do the following things
Proverbs6:4 Don't let your eyes rest or your eyelids close.
Proverbs6:5 Free yourself like a gazelle from the hand of a hunter and like a bird from the hand of a hunter.
Proverbs6:6 Consider the ant
Proverbs6:7 Although it has no overseer
Proverbs6:8 in summertime it stores its food supply. At harvest time it gathers its food.
Proverbs6:9 How long will you lie there
Proverbs6:10 Just a little sleep
Proverbs6:11 Then your poverty will come {to you} like a drifter
Proverbs6:12 A good-for-nothing scoundrel is a person who has a dishonest mouth.
Proverbs6:13 He winks his eye
Proverbs6:14 He devises evil all the time with a twisted mind. He spreads conflict.
Proverbs6:15 That is why disaster will come on him suddenly. In a moment he will be crushed beyond recovery.
Proverbs6:16 There are six things that the Lord hates
Proverbs6:17 arrogant eyes
Proverbs6:18 a mind devising wicked plans
Proverbs6:19 a dishonest witness spitting out lies
Proverbs6:20 My son
Proverbs6:21 Fasten them on your heart forever. Hang them around your neck.
Proverbs6:22 When you walk around
Proverbs6:23 because the command is a lamp
Proverbs6:24 to keep you from an evil woman and from the smooth talk of a loose woman.
Proverbs6:25 Do not desire her beauty in your heart. Do not let her catch you with her eyes.
Proverbs6:26 A prostitute's price is {only} a loaf of bread
Proverbs6:27 Can a man carry fire in his lap without burning his clothes?
Proverbs6:28 Can anyone walk on red-hot coals without burning his feet?
Proverbs6:29 So it is with a man who has sex with his neighbor's wife. None who touch her will escape punishment.
Proverbs6:30 People do not despise a thief who is hungry when he steals to satisfy his appetite
Proverbs6:31 but when he is caught
Proverbs6:32 Whoever commits adultery with a woman has no sense. Whoever does this destroys himself.
Proverbs6:33 An adulterous man will find disease and dishonor
Proverbs6:34 because jealousy arouses a husband's fury. The husband will show no mercy when he takes revenge.
Proverbs6:35 No amount of money will change his mind. The largest bribe will not satisfy him.
Proverbs7:1 My son
Proverbs7:2 Obey my commands so that you may live. Follow my teachings just as you protect the pupil of your eye.
Proverbs7:3 Tie them on your fingers. Write them on the tablet of your heart.
Proverbs7:4 Say to wisdom
Proverbs7:5 in order to guard yourself from an adulterous woman
Proverbs7:6 From a window in my house I looked through my screen.
Proverbs7:7 I was looking at gullible people when I saw a young man without much sense among youths.
Proverbs7:8 He was crossing a street near her corner and walking toward her house
Proverbs7:9 in the twilight
Proverbs7:10 A woman with an ulterior motive meets him. She is dressed as a prostitute.
Proverbs7:11 She is loud and rebellious. Her feet will not stay at home.
Proverbs7:12 One moment she is out on the street
Proverbs7:13 She grabs him and kisses him and brazenly says to him
Proverbs7:14 I have some sacrificial meat. Today I kept my vows.
Proverbs7:15 That's why I came to meet you. Eagerly
Proverbs7:16 I've made my bed
Proverbs7:17 I've sprinkled my bed with myrrh
Proverbs7:18 Come
Proverbs7:19 because my husband's not home. He has gone on a long trip.
Proverbs7:20 He took lots of money with him. He won't be home for a couple of weeks.
Proverbs7:21 With all her seductive charms
Proverbs7:22 He immediately follows her like a steer on its way to be slaughtered
Proverbs7:23 until an arrow pierces his heart
Proverbs7:24 Now
Proverbs7:25 Do not let your heart be turned to her ways. Do not wander onto her paths
Proverbs7:26 because she has brought down many victims
Proverbs7:27 Her home is the way to hell and leads to the darkest vaults of death.

Proverbs8:2 {Wisdom} takes its stand on high ground
Proverbs8:3 near the gates to the city. At the entrance {wisdom} sings its song
Proverbs8:4 I am calling to all of you
Proverbs8:5 You gullible people
Proverbs8:6 Listen! I am speaking about noble things

Proverbs8:8 Everything I say is fair
Proverbs8:9 All of it is clear to a person who has understanding and right to those who have acquired knowledge.

Proverbs8:11 because wisdom is better than jewels. Nothing you desire can equal it.
Proverbs8:12 I

Proverbs8:14 Advice and priceless wisdom are mine. I
Proverbs8:15 Through me kings reign
Proverbs8:16 Through me princes rule
Proverbs8:17 I love those who love me. Those eagerly looking for me will find me.
Proverbs8:18 I have riches and honor
Proverbs8:19 What I produce is better than gold
Proverbs8:20 I walk in the way of righteousness
Proverbs8:21 to give an inheritance to those who love me and to fill their treasuries.
Proverbs8:22 The Lord already possessed me long ago
Proverbs8:23 I was appointed from everlasting from the first
Proverbs8:24 I was born before there were oceans
Proverbs8:25 I was born before the mountains were settled in their places and before the hills
Proverbs8:26 when he had not yet made land or fields or the first dust of the world.
Proverbs8:27 When he set up the heavens
Proverbs8:28 when he established the skies above
Proverbs8:29 when he set a limit for the sea so the waters would not overstep his command
Proverbs8:30 I was beside him as a master craftsman. I made him happy day after day
Proverbs8:31 found joy in his inhabited world
Proverbs8:32 Now
Proverbs8:33 Listen to discipline
Proverbs8:34 Blessed is the person who listens to me
Proverbs8:35 Whoever finds me finds life and obtains favor from the Lord.
Proverbs8:36 Whoever sins against me harms himself. All those who hate me love death.

Ecclesiastes1:1 The words of the spokesman
Ecclesiastes1:2 Absolutely pointless! says the spokesman. Absolutely pointless! Everything is pointless.
Ecclesiastes1:3 What do people gain from all their hard work under the sun?
Ecclesiastes1:4 Generations come
Ecclesiastes1:5 The sun rises
Ecclesiastes1:6 The wind blows toward the south and shifts toward the north. Round and round it blows. It blows in a full circle.
Ecclesiastes1:7 All streams flow into the sea
Ecclesiastes1:8 All of these sayings are worn-out phrases. They are more than anyone can express
Ecclesiastes1:9 Whatever has happened before will happen {again}. Whatever has been done before will be done {again}. There is nothing new under the sun.
Ecclesiastes1:10 Can you say that anything is new? It has already been here long before us.
Ecclesiastes1:11 Nothing from the past is remembered. Even in the future
Ecclesiastes1:12 I
Ecclesiastes1:13 With all my heart I used wisdom to study and explore everything done under heaven. Mortals are weighed down with a terrible burden that God has placed on them.
Ecclesiastes1:14 I have seen everything that is done under the sun. Look at it! It's all pointless. {It's like} trying to catch the wind.
Ecclesiastes1:15 No one can straighten what is bent. No one can count what is not there.
Ecclesiastes1:16 I thought to myself
Ecclesiastes1:17 I've used my mind to understand wisdom and knowledge as well as madness and stupidity. {Now} I know that this is {like} trying to catch the wind.
Ecclesiastes1:18 With a lot of wisdom {comes} a lot of heartache. The greater {your} knowledge
Ecclesiastes2:1 I thought to myself
Ecclesiastes2:2 I thought
Ecclesiastes2:3 I explored ways to make myself feel better by drinking wine. I also explored ways to do {some} foolish things. During all that time
Ecclesiastes2:4 I accomplished some great things: I built houses for myself. I planted vineyards for myself.
Ecclesiastes2:5 I made gardens and parks for myself. I planted every kind of fruit tree in them.
Ecclesiastes2:6 I made pools to water the forest of growing trees.
Ecclesiastes2:7 I bought male and female slaves. In addition
Ecclesiastes2:8 I also gathered silver and gold for myself. I gathered the treasures of kings and provinces. I provided myself with male and female singers and the pleasures men have with one concubine after another.
Ecclesiastes2:9 So I grew richer than anyone in Jerusalem before me. Yet
Ecclesiastes2:10 If something appealed to me
Ecclesiastes2:11 But when I turned to look at all that I had accomplished and all the hard work I had put into it
Ecclesiastes2:12 Then I turned {my attention} to experience wisdom
Ecclesiastes2:13 But I saw that wisdom has an advantage over foolishness as light has an advantage over darkness.
Ecclesiastes2:14 A wise person uses the eyes in his head
Ecclesiastes2:15 I thought to myself
Ecclesiastes2:16 Neither the wise person nor the fool will be remembered for long
Ecclesiastes2:17 So I came to hate life because everything done under the sun seemed wrong to me. Everything was pointless. {It was like} trying to catch the wind.
Ecclesiastes2:18 I came to hate everything for which I had worked so hard under the sun
Ecclesiastes2:19 Who knows whether that person will be wise or foolish? He will still have control over everything under the sun for which I worked so hard and used my wisdom. Even this is pointless.
Ecclesiastes2:20 Then I fell into despair over everything for which I had worked so hard under the sun.
Ecclesiastes2:21 Here is someone who had worked hard with wisdom
Ecclesiastes2:22 What do people get from all of their hard work and struggles under the sun?
Ecclesiastes2:23 Their entire life is filled with pain
Ecclesiastes2:24 There is nothing better for people to do than to eat
Ecclesiastes2:25 Who can eat or enjoy themselves without God?
Ecclesiastes2:26 God gives wisdom
Ecclesiastes3:1 Everything has its own time
Ecclesiastes3:2 a time to be born and a time to die
Ecclesiastes3:3 a time to kill and a time to heal
Ecclesiastes3:4 a time to cry and a time to laugh

Ecclesiastes3:12 I realize that there's nothing better for them to do than to be cheerful and enjoy what is good in their lives.

Ecclesiastes3:15 Whatever has happened {in the past} is present now. Whatever is going to happen {in the future} has already happened {in the past}. God will call the past to account.

Isaiah1:5 Why do you still want to be beaten? Why do you continue to rebel? Your whole head is infected. Your whole heart is failing.
Isaiah1:6 From the bottom of your feet to the top of your head there is no healthy spot left on your {body}— only bruises

Isaiah1:16 Wash yourselves! Become clean! Get your evil deeds out of my sight. Stop doing evil.

Isaiah1:23 Your rulers are rebels

Isaiah10:16 That is why the Almighty Lord of Armies will send a degenerative disease against brave men. A flame will be turned into a raging fire under his power.
Isaiah10:17 Israel's light will become a flame. Its Holy One will become a fire. He will burn up and devour the weeds and thornbushes in one day.

Whoever offers a grain sacrifice is like someone who {offers} pig's blood. Whoever burns incense is like someone who worships an idol. People have certainly chosen their own ways

Isaiah66:10 All who love Jerusalem
Isaiah66:11 You will nurse and be satisfied from her comforting breasts. You will nurse to your heart's delight at her full breasts.

Isaiah66:23 From one month to the next and from one week to the next all people will come to worship me
Isaiah66:24 Then they will go out and look at the corpses of those who have rebelled against me. The worms that eat them will not die. The fire that burns them will not go out. All humanity will be disgusted by them.

Jeremiah1:2 The Lord spoke his word to Jeremiah when King Josiah
Jeremiah1:3 The Lord also spoke when Jehoiakim
Jeremiah1:4 The Lord spoke his word to me
Jeremiah1:5 Before I formed you in the womb
Jeremiah1:6 I
Jeremiah1:7 But the Lord said to me
Jeremiah1:8 Don't be afraid of people. I am with you
Jeremiah1:9 Then the Lord stretched out his hand and touched my mouth. The Lord said to me
Jeremiah1:10 Today I have put you in charge of nations and kingdoms. You will uproot and tear down. You will destroy and overthrow. You will build and plant.
Jeremiah1:11 Again the Lord spoke his word to me and asked
Jeremiah1:12 Then the Lord said to me
Jeremiah1:13 Again the Lord spoke his word to me and asked
Jeremiah1:14 Then the Lord said to me
Jeremiah1:15 I am going to call every family and kingdom from the north
Jeremiah1:16 I will pass sentence on my people because of all their wickedness. They abandoned me
Jeremiah1:17 Brace yourself
Jeremiah1:18 Today I have made you like a fortified city
Jeremiah1:19 They will fight you
Jeremiah2:1 The Lord spoke his word to me
Jeremiah2:2 Go and announce to Jerusalem
Jeremiah2:3 Israel was set apart for the Lord. It was the best part of the harvest. All who devoured it became guilty
Jeremiah2:4 Listen to the word of the Lord
Jeremiah2:5 This is what the Lord says: What did your ancestors find wrong with me that they went so far away from me? They followed worthless idols and became worthless themselves.
Jeremiah2:6 They didn't ask
Jeremiah2:7 I brought them into a fertile land to eat its fruit and its produce. They came and made my land unclean. They made my property disgusting.
Jeremiah2:8 The priests didn't ask
Jeremiah2:9 That is why I am bringing charges against you
Jeremiah2:10 Go over to the coasts of Cyprus
Jeremiah2:11 Has any nation ever exchanged gods? (Their gods aren't really gods.) Yet
Jeremiah2:12 Be horrified over this
Jeremiah2:13 My people have done two things wrong. They have abandoned me
Jeremiah2:14 Are the people of Israel slaves? Were they born into slavery? Why
Jeremiah2:15 Young lions have roared very loudly at them. Young lions have turned the land into a wasteland. The cities have been burned down
Jeremiah2:16 People from Noph and Tahpanhes have cracked your skulls
Jeremiah2:17 You have brought this on yourself by abandoning the Lord your God when he led you on his way.
Jeremiah2:18 You won't gain anything by going to Egypt to drink water from the Nile River. You won't gain anything by going to Assyria to drink water from the Euphrates River.
Jeremiah2:19 Your own wickedness will correct you
Jeremiah2:20 Long ago you broke off your yoke
Jeremiah2:21 I planted you like a choice grapevine from the very best seed. Now you have turned against me and have become a wild vine.
Jeremiah2:22 Even if you wash with detergent and use a lot of soap
Jeremiah2:23 How can you say that you haven't dishonored yourselves and haven't followed other gods—the Baals? Look how you've behaved in the valley. Acknowledge what you've done. You are like a young camel that swiftly runs here and there.
Jeremiah2:24 You are like a wild donkey that is used to the desert
Jeremiah2:25 Don't run until your feet are bare and your throats are dry. But you say that it's useless. You love foreign gods and follow them.
Jeremiah2:26 As a thief feels ashamed when he's caught
Jeremiah2:27 You call wood your father. You call stone your mother. You've turned your backs
Jeremiah2:28 Where are the gods that you made for yourselves? Let them come and rescue you when you're in trouble. You have as many gods as you have cities
Jeremiah2:29 Why do you complain about me? All of you have rebelled against me
Jeremiah2:30 I have punished your children without results. They didn't respond to correction. You killed my prophets like a raging lion.
Jeremiah2:31 Consider the word of the Lord
Jeremiah2:32 A young woman can't forget her jewelry or a bride her veils. Yet
Jeremiah2:33 You carefully planned ways to look for love. You taught your ways to wicked women.
Jeremiah2:34 You have the blood from poor and innocent people on your clothes. You didn't kill them for breaking in to your home.
Jeremiah2:35 In spite of all this you say
Jeremiah2:36 You change your mind so easily. You will be put to shame by Egypt as you were put to shame by Assyria.
Jeremiah2:37 You will also leave this place with your hands over your head

Jeremiah16:2 Don't marry! Don't have any sons or daughters in this place!

Jeremiah16:4 They will die horrible deaths. No one will mourn for them or bury them. They will be like manure on the ground. Wars and famines will bring them to an end. Their bodies will be food for birds and animals.
Jeremiah16:5 This is what the Lord says: Don't go into a house where people are grieving. Don't go to mourn or to grieve for them. I'm taking my peace
Jeremiah16:6 Old and young alike will die in this land. No one will mourn for them or bury them. No one will cut his own body or shave his own head for them.
Jeremiah16:7 No one will offer food to comfort those who mourn the dead. No one will give a consoling drink to those who have lost their fathers or mothers.
Jeremiah16:8 Don't even go into a home where there is a banquet. Don't sit with them to eat and drink.

Jeremiah52:2 Zedekiah did what the Lord considered evil

Jeremiah52:4 On the tenth day of the tenth month of the ninth year of Zedekiah's reign
Jeremiah52:5 The blockade of the city lasted until Zedekiah's eleventh year as king.
Jeremiah52:6 On the ninth day of the fourth month
Jeremiah52:7 The enemy broke through the city walls
Jeremiah52:8 The Babylonian army pursued King Zedekiah and caught up with him in the plain of Jericho. His entire army had deserted him.
Jeremiah52:9 The Babylonians captured the king and brought him to the king of Babylon at Riblah in Hamath
Jeremiah52:10 The king of Babylon slaughtered Zedekiah's sons as Zedekiah watched. He also slaughtered all the officials of Judah at Riblah.
Jeremiah52:11 Then he blinded Zedekiah and put him in bronze shackles. The king of Babylon took him to Babylon and put him in a prison

Jeremiah52:20 The bronze from the 2 pillars
Jeremiah52:21 One pillar was 27 feet high and 18 feet in circumference. It was three inches thick and hollow.
Jeremiah52:22 The capital that was on it was 7 1/2 feet high with a filigree and pomegranates around it. They were all made of bronze. The second pillar was the same. It also had pomegranates.
Jeremiah52:23 There were 96 pomegranates on the sides. The total number of pomegranates on the surrounding filigree was 100.

Jeremiah52:32 He treated him well and gave him a special position higher than the other kings who were with him in Babylon.
Jeremiah52:33 Jehoiakin no longer wore prison clothes

Lamentations1:1 Look how deserted Jerusalem is! Once the city was crowded with people. Once it was important among the nations. Now it is a widow. Once it was a princess among the provinces. Now it does forced labor.
Lamentations1:2 Jerusalem cries bitterly at night with tears running down its cheeks. Out of all those who love the city
Lamentations1:3 Judah has been exiled after {much} suffering and harsh treatment. Its {people} live among the nations; they find no rest. Those who chased them caught up with them in places where there was no way out.
Lamentations1:4 The roads to Zion are deserted. No one comes to the annual festivals. No one passes through any of its gates. Its priests are groaning. Its young women are made to suffer. Zion is bitter.
Lamentations1:5 Its opponents are now in control. Its enemies have no worries. The Lord made Zion suffer for its many rebellious acts. Its children go ahead of their opponents into captivity.
Lamentations1:6 All splendor has abandoned the people of Zion. Its influential people were like deer that couldn't find any pasture. They ran without any strength ahead of the hunters.
Lamentations1:7 Now
Lamentations1:8 Jerusalem has sinned so much that it has become a filthy thing. Everyone who used to honor it now despises it. They've seen it naked. Jerusalem groans and turns away.
Lamentations1:9 Jerusalem's own filth {covers} its clothes. It gave no thought to its future. Its downfall was shocking. No one offers it comfort. 'O Lord
Lamentations1:10 The enemies laid their hands on all of the city's treasures. Jerusalem has seen the nations enter the holy place. 'O Lord
Lamentations1:11 All the people are groaning as they beg for bread. They trade their treasures for food to keep themselves alive. 'O Lord
Lamentations1:12 Doesn't this affect all of you who pass by? Look and see if there's any pain like the pain that the Lord has caused me
Lamentations1:13 He sent fire from above. He made it go deep into my bones. He spread a net for my feet. He made me turn back. He has left me devastated. He has made me sick all day long.
Lamentations1:14 My rebellious acts are a heavy burden for me. They were tied together by God's own hands. They were tied around my neck. He has weakened me {with them}. The Lord has handed me over to people I cannot oppose.
Lamentations1:15 The Lord has treated all the warriors inside my {walls} with contempt. He called an army to defeat my young men. The Lord trampled the people of Judah in a winepress.
Lamentations1:16 I'm crying because of {all} these things. My eyes—my eyes flow with tears. No one can give me the comfort I need to keep me alive. Everyone is too far away from me. My children are devastated because my enemies have won.
Lamentations1:17 Zion holds out its hands. No one offers it comfort. The Lord has given this order about Jacob: His own neighbors will become his opponents. Jerusalem has become a filthy thing among them.
Lamentations1:18 The Lord is right in what he did
Lamentations1:19 I called for those who love me
Lamentations1:20 O Lord
Lamentations1:21 All my enemies have heard that I am groaning. No one offers me comfort. All my enemies have heard about my disaster. They are happy that you did it. You have allowed the day to come
Lamentations1:22 Recall all of their wickedness. Then deal with them as you have dealt with me because of all my rebellious acts. I groan so much and feel so sick at heart.
Lamentations2:1 Look how the Lord has covered the people of Zion with the cloud of his anger! He has thrown down Israel's beauty from heaven to earth. He didn't {even} remember his footstool on the day of his anger.
Lamentations2:2 The Lord swallowed up all of Jacob's pastures without any pity. He tore down the fortified cities of Judah in his fury. He brought the kingdom {of Judah} and its leaders down to the ground in dishonor.
Lamentations2:3 In his burning anger he cut off all of Israel's strength. He withdrew his right hand when they faced their enemy. He burned like a raging fire in {the land of} Jacob
Lamentations2:4 Like an enemy he bent his bow. Like an opponent his right hand held the arrow steady. He killed all the beautiful people. He poured out his fury like fire on the tent of Zion's people.
Lamentations2:5 The Lord became an enemy. He swallowed up Israel. He swallowed up all of its palaces. He destroyed its strongholds. He made the people of Judah mourn and moan.
Lamentations2:6 He stripped his own booth as if it were a garden and destroyed his own festivals. The Lord wiped out the memory of festivals and days of worship in Zion. He expelled kings and priests because of his fierce anger.
Lamentations2:7 The Lord rejected his altar and disowned his holy place. He handed the walls of Zion's palaces over to its enemies. The enemies made noise in the Lord's temple as though it were a festival day.
Lamentations2:8 The Lord planned to destroy the wall of Zion's people. He marked it off with a line. He didn't take his hand away until he had swallowed it up. He made the towers and walls mourn. They are completely dejected.
Lamentations2:9 {Zion's} gates have sunk into the ground. {The Lord} destroyed and shattered the bars across its {gates}. Its king and influential people are {scattered} among the nations. There is no longer any instruction {from Moses' Teachings}. Its prophets can find no visions from the Lord.
Lamentations2:10 The respected leaders of Zion's people sit silently on the ground. They throw dirt on their heads and put on sackcloth. The young women of Jerusalem bow their heads to the ground.
Lamentations2:11 My eyes are worn out with tears. My stomach is churning. My heart is poured out on the ground because of the destruction of my people. Little children and infants faint in the city streets.
Lamentations2:12 They're asking their mothers for some bread and wine as they faint like wounded people in the city streets. Their lives dwindle away in their mothers' arms.
Lamentations2:13 What example can I give you? What parallel can I show you
Lamentations2:14 Your prophets saw misleading visions about you. They painted a good picture of you. They didn't expose your guilt in order to make things better again. They gave you false prophecies that misled you.
Lamentations2:15 Everyone who walks along the road shakes a fist at you. They hiss and shake their heads at Jerusalem's people: 'Is this the city they used to call absolutely beautiful
Lamentations2:16 All your enemies gawk at you. They hiss and grit their teeth. They say
Lamentations2:17 The Lord has accomplished what he had planned to do. He carried out the threat he announced long ago. He tore you down without any pity
Lamentations2:18 The hearts of Jerusalem's people cried out to the Lord
Lamentations2:19 Get up! Cry out at night
Lamentations2:20 O Lord
Lamentations2:21 Young and old lie on the ground in the streets. My young women and men are cut down by swords. You killed them on the day of your anger. You slaughtered them without any pity.
Lamentations2:22 You have invited those who terrorize me on every side

Lamentations3:2 God has driven me away and made me walk in darkness instead of light.

Lamentations3:22 the Lord's mercy. We were not completely wiped out. His compassion is never limited.
Lamentations3:23 It is new every morning. His faithfulness is great.
Lamentations3:24 My soul can say

Lamentations3:26 It is good to continue to hope and wait silently for the Lord to save us.

Lamentations3:32 Even if he makes us suffer

Lamentations3:35 deny people their rights in the presence of the Most High God
Lamentations3:36 or deprive people of justice in court. The Lord isn't happy to see {these things}.
Lamentations3:37 Who was it who spoke and it came into being? It was the Lord who gave the order.
Lamentations3:38 Both good and bad come from the mouth of the Most High God.
Lamentations3:39 Why should any living mortal (any person) complain about being punished for sin?
Lamentations3:40 Let us look closely at our ways and examine them and then return to the Lord.
Lamentations3:41 Let us raise our hearts and hands to God in heaven.
Lamentations3:42 We have been disobedient and rebellious. You haven't forgiven us.
Lamentations3:43 You covered yourself with anger and pursued us. You killed without pity.
Lamentations3:44 You covered yourself with a cloud so that no prayer could get through it.
Lamentations3:45 You made us the scum and trash of the nations.
Lamentations3:46 All our enemies gawk at us.
Lamentations3:47 Panic and pitfalls have found us
Lamentations3:48 Streams of tears run down from my eyes over the ruin of my dear people.
Lamentations3:49 My eyes will keep flowing without stopping for a moment
Lamentations3:50 until the Lord looks down from heaven and sees.
Lamentations3:51 What I see with my eyes disturbs me deeply because of all the young women in my city.
Lamentations3:52 Those who were my enemies for no reason hunted me like a bird.
Lamentations3:53 They threw me alive into a pit and threw rocks at me.
Lamentations3:54 Water flowed over my head. I thought I was finished.
Lamentations3:55 I call your name from the deepest pit
Lamentations3:56 Listen to my cry {for help}. Don't close your ears when I cry out for relief.

Lamentations4:7 Zion's princes were purer than snow
Lamentations4:8 Their faces are {now} blacker than soot. No one recognizes them on the streets. Their skin has shriveled on their bones. It has become as dry as bark.
Lamentations4:9 Those who were killed with swords are better off than those who are dying from starvation. Those who were stabbed bled to death. The others are dying because there is nothing in the fields to eat.
Lamentations4:10 The hands of loving mothers cooked their own children. The children were used for food by my people when they were being destroyed {by a blockade}.

Lamentations4:13 {They got through} because of the sins of Jerusalem's prophets and the crimes of its priests

Lamentations4:21 Rejoice and be glad
Lamentations4:22 People of Zion

Ezekiel1:11 That is what their faces looked like. Their wings were spread out

Ezekiel31:14 Then all the other trees growing by the water were kept from becoming arrogant because of their height

Ezekiel40:1 It was the tenth day of the month in the beginning of the twenty-fifth year of our captivity and fourteen years after Jerusalem was captured. At that time the Lord's power came over me
Ezekiel40:2 In visions
Ezekiel40:3 He brought me closer. I saw a man who looked like he was covered with bronze. The man was holding a linen tape measure and a measuring stick
Ezekiel40:4 He said to me
Ezekiel40:5 I saw a wall that surrounded the temple. The man had a measuring stick that was 10 1/2 feet long. He measured the wall. It was 10 1/2 feet thick and 10 1/2 feet high.
Ezekiel40:6 Then the man went to the gateway that faced east. He went up its steps and measured the entrance to the gateway. It was 10 1/2 feet wide.
Ezekiel40:7 There were also guardrooms. Each guardroom was 10 1/2 feet long and 10 1/2 feet wide. The space between the guardrooms was 9 feet thick. And the entrance to the gateway by the entrance hall of the temple was 10 1/2 feet wide.
Ezekiel40:8 He also measured the entrance hall of the gateway.
Ezekiel40:9 It extended 14 feet from the temple. Its recessed walls were 3 1/2 feet thick. The gateway's entrance hall faced the temple.
Ezekiel40:10 Now
Ezekiel40:11 Then the man measured the width of the entrance to the gateway. It was 17 1/2 feet wide
Ezekiel40:12 There was a barrier about 21 inches in front of each guardroom. The guardrooms were 10 1/2 feet square.
Ezekiel40:13 He measured the gateway from the top of one guardroom to the top of the opposite guardroom. It was 44 feet wide from one door to the opposite door.
Ezekiel40:14 He also measured the entrance hall. It was 35 feet wide. In front of the entrance hall to the gateway was a courtyard on all sides.
Ezekiel40:15 The total length of the gateway from the front of the outer part to the front of the inner part of the entrance hall was 87 1/2 feet.
Ezekiel40:16 The guardrooms and recessed walls inside the gateway had small windows all around. The entrance hall also had windows all around on the inside. Pictures of palm trees were carved on the recessed walls.
Ezekiel40:17 Then the man brought me into the outer courtyard. I saw rooms there and pavement all around the courtyard. There were 30 rooms along the edge of the pavement.
Ezekiel40:18 The pavement in the lower courtyard ran alongside the gateways. It was as wide as it was long.
Ezekiel40:19 The man measured the distance from the inside of the lower gateway to the outside of the inner courtyard. It was 175 feet from east to north.

Ezekiel40:21 Its three guardrooms
Ezekiel40:22 Its windows
Ezekiel40:23 The inner courtyard had a gateway opposite the north gate just like the east gateway. The man measured the distance from one gate to the other gate. It was 175 feet.
Ezekiel40:24 Then the man led me to the south side
Ezekiel40:25 The gateway and its entrance hall had windows on all sides like the windows in the other gateways. It was 87 1/2 feet long and 44 feet wide.
Ezekiel40:26 Seven steps went up to it and led to its entrance hall. Pictures of palm trees were carved on the recessed walls
Ezekiel40:27 The inner courtyard had a gateway facing south. The man measured the distance from the gateway on the south side to its opposite gateway. It was 175 feet.
Ezekiel40:28 Then the man brought me to the inner courtyard through the south gateway. He measured the south gateway. It was the same size as the others.
Ezekiel40:29 Its guardrooms
Ezekiel40:30 There were entrance halls all around the inner courtyard. They were all 44 feet long and 9 feet wide.
Ezekiel40:31 The entrance halls faced the outer courtyard. Pictures of palm trees were carved on the recessed walls

Ezekiel40:34 Its entrance hall faced the outer courtyard. Pictures of palm trees were carved on the recessed walls
Ezekiel40:35 Then the man brought me to the north gateway. He measured it. It was the same size as the others.
Ezekiel40:36 Its guardrooms
Ezekiel40:37 Its recessed walls faced the outer courtyard. Pictures of palm trees were carved on the recessed walls

Ezekiel43:1 Then the man took me to the east gate.
Ezekiel43:2 I saw the glory of the God of Israel coming from the east. His voice was like the sound of rushing water
Ezekiel43:3 This vision was like the one I saw when he came to destroy Jerusalem and like the one I saw by the Chebar River. I immediately bowed down.
Ezekiel43:4 The Lord's glory came into the temple through the east gate.
Ezekiel43:5 The Spirit lifted me and brought me into the inner courtyard. I saw the Lord's glory fill the temple.
Ezekiel43:6 I heard someone speaking to me from inside the temple while the man was standing beside me.

Daniel1:2 The Lord handed King Jehoiakim of Judah and some utensils from God's temple over to Nebuchadnezzar. Nebuchadnezzar took the utensils to the temple of his god in Babylonia and put them in the temple treasury.

Daniel1:4 They were to be young men who were healthy

Daniel1:7 The chief-of-staff gave them {Babylonian} names: To Daniel he gave the name Belteshazzar. To Hananiah he gave the name Shadrach. To Mishael he gave the name Meshach. And to Azariah he gave the name Abednego.
Daniel1:8 Daniel made up his mind not to harm himself by eating the king's rich food and drinking the king's wine. So he asked the chief-of-staff for permission not to harm himself in this way.
Daniel1:9 God made the chief-of-staff kind and compassionate toward Daniel.
Daniel1:10 The chief-of-staff told Daniel
Daniel1:11 The chief-of-staff put a supervisor in charge of Daniel
Daniel1:12 Please test us for ten days. Give us only vegetables to eat and water to drink.
Daniel1:13 Then compare us to the young men who are eating the king's rich food. Decide how to treat us on the basis of how we look.
Daniel1:14 The supervisor listened to them about this matter and tested them for ten days.
Daniel1:15 After ten days they looked healthier and stronger than the young men who had been eating the king's rich food.
Daniel1:16 So the supervisor took away the king's rich food and wine and gave them vegetables.

Daniel1:19 The king talked to them and found no one like Daniel
Daniel1:20 Whenever the king asked them about things that required wisdom and insight

Joel1:2 Listen to this
Joel1:3 Tell your children about it. Have your children tell their children. Have your grandchildren tell their children.

Micah1:2 Listen
Micah1:3 The Lord is going to come from his place. He is going to come down and step on the worship places of the earth.
Micah1:4 Mountains will melt under him like wax near a fire. Valleys will split apart like water pouring down a steep hill.
Micah1:5 All this is because of Jacob's crime and Israel's sin. What is Jacob's crime? Isn't it Samaria? What is Judah's worship place? Isn't it Jerusalem?
Micah1:6 So I will turn Samaria into a pile of rubble
Micah1:7 All its idols will be smashed to pieces. All its wages for being a prostitute will be burned. All its statues will be turned into a pile of rubble. Samaria collected its wages for being a prostitute. That money will again pay for prostitutes.
Micah1:8 I will mourn and cry because of this. I will walk around barefoot and naked. I will cry like a jackal and mourn like an ostrich.
Micah1:9 Samaria's wounds are incurable. {The news about Samaria} will come to Judah. It will reach the gates of my people in Jerusalem.
Micah1:10 Don't report it in Gath. Don't cry there. Roll in the dust of Beth Leaphrah.

Micah1:13 Harness the horses to the chariots

Nahum1:1 This is a revelation from the Lord about Nineveh. This book contains the vision of Nahum from Elkosh.
Nahum1:2 God does not tolerate rivals. The Lord takes revenge. The Lord takes revenge and is full of anger. The Lord takes revenge against his enemies and holds a grudge against his foes.
Nahum1:3 The Lord is patient and has great strength. The Lord will never let the guilty go unpunished. Raging winds and storms mark his path
Nahum1:4 He yells at the sea and makes it dry. He dries up all the rivers. Bashan and Carmel wither. The flowers of Lebanon wither.
Nahum1:5 The mountains quake because of him. The hills melt. The earth draws back in his presence. The world and all who live in it draw back as well.
Nahum1:6 Who can stand in the presence of his rage? Who can oppose his burning anger? He pours out his rage like fire and smashes the rocky cliffs.
Nahum1:7 The Lord is good. {He is} a fortress in the day of trouble. He knows those who seek shelter in him.
Nahum1:8 He will put an end to Nineveh with a devastating flood. He will pursue his enemies with darkness.
Nahum1:9 What do you think about the Lord? He is the one who will bring Nineveh to an end. This trouble will never happen again.
Nahum1:10 {The people of Nineveh will be} like tangled thorns and like people drunk on their own drink. They will be completely burned up like very dry straw.
Nahum1:11 From you

Habakkuk1:1 The divine revelation that the prophet Habakkuk saw.
Habakkuk1:2 How long
Habakkuk1:3 Why do you make me see wrongdoing? And why do you watch wickedness? Destruction and violence are in front of me. Quarrels and disputes arise.
Habakkuk1:4 That is why your teaching is numbed
Habakkuk1:5 Look among the nations and watch. Be amazed and astonished. I am going to do something in your days that you would not believe even if it were reported to you.
Habakkuk1:6 I am going to send the Babylonians
Habakkuk1:7 They will be terrifying and fearsome. They will carry out their own kind of justice and honor.
Habakkuk1:8 Their horses will be faster than leopards and quicker than wolves in the evening. Their riders will gallop along proudly. Their riders will come from far away. They will fly like an eagle that swoops down for its prey.
Habakkuk1:9 They will all come for violence. Every face will be directed forward. They will gather prisoners like sand.
Habakkuk1:10 They will make fun of kings and treat rulers as a joke. They will laugh at every fortified city and build a dirt ramp to capture it.
Habakkuk1:11 They will move quickly and pass through like the wind. So they will be guilty
Habakkuk1:12 Didn't you exist before time began
Habakkuk1:13 Your eyes are too pure to look at evil. You can't watch wickedness. Why do you keep watching treacherous people? Why are you silent when wicked people swallow those who are more righteous than they are?
Habakkuk1:14 You make all people like the fish in the sea
Habakkuk1:15 The Babylonians pull them all up with fishhooks
Habakkuk1:16 That is why they sacrifice to their nets and burn incense to their dragnets. They are rich and well fed because of them.
Habakkuk1:17 Will they keep on emptying their nets and always kill nations without mercy?

Habakkuk2:5 Also because wine is treacherous he is arrogant and never rests. He has a large appetite like the grave. He is like death—never satisfied. He gathers all the nations to himself. He collects all the people to himself.
Habakkuk2:6 Won't all of them ridicule him
Habakkuk2:7 Won't your creditors suddenly rise up and those who are going to shake you wake up? Then you will become their prize.
Habakkuk2:8 You have looted many nations. All the rest of the people will loot you because of the slaughter and violence done to lands
Habakkuk2:9 'How horrible it will be for the one who uses violence to get things for his own household in order to set his nest up high and save himself from disaster.'
Habakkuk2:10 You have planned disgrace for your household by cutting off many people and forfeiting your own life.
Habakkuk2:11 A stone in the wall will cry out. A beam in the roof will answer it.
Habakkuk2:12 'How horrible it will be for the one who builds a city by slaughter and founds a town by crime.'
Habakkuk2:13 Isn't it from the Lord of Armies that people grow tired only to feed the flames and nations exhaust themselves for nothing?
Habakkuk2:14 But the earth will be filled with the knowledge of the Lord's glory like the water covers the sea.
Habakkuk2:15 'How horrible it will be for the one who makes his neighbor drink from the bowl of God's rage
Habakkuk2:16 You are filled with disgrace rather than glory. Drink! Yes you! And expose yourself as godless. The cup in the Lord's right hand will come around to you
Habakkuk2:17 The violence done to Lebanon will overwhelm you

Habakkuk2:19 'How horrible it will be for the one who says to a piece of wood

Zechariah8:1 The Lord of Armies spoke his word.

Zechariah8:3 This is what the Lord says: I will return to Zion and live in Jerusalem. Jerusalem will be called the City of Truth. The mountain of the Lord of Armies will be called the holy mountain.
Zechariah8:4 This is what the Lord of Armies says: Old men and old women will again sit in the streets of Jerusalem. Each will have a cane in hand because of old age.
Zechariah8:5 The city will be filled with boys and girls playing in the streets.
Zechariah8:6 This is what the Lord of Armies says: It may seem impossible to the few remaining people in those days
Zechariah8:7 This is what the Lord of Armies says: I am going to save my people from the land where the sun rises and from the land where the sun sets.
Zechariah8:8 I will bring them back
Zechariah8:9 This is what the Lord of Armies says: Be strong so that the temple might be rebuilt
Zechariah8:10 Before that time there was no money to hire any person or animal. No one who traveled was safe from the enemy. I turned every person against his neighbor.
Zechariah8:11 But now I won't deal with the few remaining people as I did in earlier times
Zechariah8:12 Seeds will thrive in peacetime. Vines will produce their grapes. The land will yield its crops. The sky will produce its dew. I will give the few remaining people all these things as an inheritance.
Zechariah8:13 Just as you
Zechariah8:14 This is what the Lord of Armies says: When your ancestors made me angry
Zechariah8:15 So now I have again made plans
Zechariah8:16 You must do these things: Speak the truth to each other. Give correct and fair verdicts for peace in your courts.
Zechariah8:17 Don't even think of doing evil to each other. Don't enjoy false testimony. I hate all these things
Zechariah8:18 The Lord of Armies spoke his word to me again.
Zechariah8:19 This is what the Lord of Armies says: The fast in the fourth month

Zechariah9:4 The Lord will take away its possessions. He will throw its wealth into the sea and burn the city down.
Zechariah9:5 Ashkelon will see {this} and be afraid. Gaza will also be in great pain
Zechariah9:6 A mixed race will live in Ashdod

Zechariah9:8 I will camp in front of my house as a guard against those who come and go. No oppressors will pass through them
Zechariah9:9 Rejoice with all your heart
Zechariah9:10 He will make sure there are no chariots in Ephraim or war horses in Jerusalem. There will be no battle bows. He will announce peace to the nations. He will rule from sea to sea and from the {Euphrates} River to the ends of the earth.
Zechariah9:11 I will set your captives free from the waterless pit because of the blood that sealed my promise to you.
Zechariah9:12 Return to your fortress

Zechariah9:16 On that day the Lord their God will rescue them as the flock of his people. They will certainly sparkle in his land like jewels in a crown.
Zechariah9:17 They will be beautiful and lovely. Young men will prosper on grain

Malachi1:2 I loved you
Malachi1:3 but Esau I hated. I turned his mountains into a wasteland and left his inheritance to the jackals in the desert.
Malachi1:4 The descendants of Esau may say
Malachi1:5 You will see these things with your own eyes and say
Malachi1:6 This is what the Lord of Armies says: A son honors his father
Malachi1:7 You offer contaminated food on my altar. But you ask
Malachi1:8 When you bring a blind animal to sacrifice
Malachi1:9 Now try asking God to be kind to you. This is what you are doing! Will he welcome you? asks the Lord of Armies.
Malachi1:10 I wish one of you would shut the doors {to my house} so that you could not light fires on my altar for no reason. I'm not pleased with you
Malachi1:11 From the nations where the sun rises to the nations where the sun sets
Malachi1:12 But you dishonor it when you say that the Lord's table may be contaminated and that its food may be despised.
Malachi1:13 You say
Malachi1:14 Cheaters are under a curse. They have male animals in their flocks that they vow to give {as a sacrifice}. But they sacrifice second-rate ones to the Lord instead. I am a great king
Malachi2:1 And now
Malachi2:2 If you won't listen and if you won't consider giving honor to my name
Malachi2:3 I'm going to punish your descendants. I'm going to spread excrement on your faces
Malachi2:4 Then you will know that I sent you this warning so that my promise to Levi will continue
Malachi2:5 I promised Levi life and peace. I gave them to him so that he would respect me. He respected me and stood in awe of my name.
Malachi2:6 The teaching that came from his mouth was true. Nothing unjust was found on his lips. He lived with me in peace and honesty and turned many people away from sin.
Malachi2:7 A priest's lips should preserve knowledge. Then
Malachi2:8 But you have turned from the {correct} path and caused many to stumble over my teachings. You have corrupted the promise made to Levi
Malachi2:9 So I have made you disgusting
Malachi2:10 Don't all of us have the same father? Hasn't the same God created us? Why are we unfaithful to each other? And why do we dishonor the promise given to our ancestors?
Malachi2:11 Judah has been unfaithful! A disgusting thing has been done in Israel and Jerusalem. Judah has dishonored the holy place that the Lord loves and has married a woman who worships a foreign god.
Malachi2:12 May the Lord exclude anyone who does this
Malachi2:13 Here is another thing you do: You cover the Lord's altar with tears. You moan and groan because he no longer pays attention to your offerings or accepts them from you.
Malachi2:14 But you ask
Malachi2:15 Didn't {God} make you one? Your flesh and spirit belong to him. And what does the same {God} look for but godly descendants? So be careful not to be unfaithful to the wife of your youth.
Malachi2:16 I hate divorce
Malachi2:17 You have tried the patience of the Lord with your words. But you ask

Matthew1:1 This is the list of ancestors of Jesus Christ
Matthew1:2 Abraham was the father of Isaac
Matthew1:3 Judah and Tamar were the father and mother of Perez and Zerah. Perez was the father of Hezron
Matthew1:4 Ram the father of Amminadab
Matthew1:5 Salmon and Rahab were the father and mother of Boaz. Boaz and Ruth were the father and mother of Obed. Obed was the father of Jesse
Matthew1:6 Jesse the father of King David. David and Uriah's wife {Bathsheba} were the father and mother of Solomon.
Matthew1:7 Solomon was the father of Rehoboam
Matthew1:8 Asa the father of Jehoshaphat
Matthew1:9 Uzziah the father of Jotham
Matthew1:10 Hezekiah the father of Manasseh
Matthew1:11 Josiah was the father of Jechoniah and his brothers. They lived at the time when the people were exiled to Babylon.
Matthew1:12 After the exile to Babylon
Matthew1:13 Zerubbabel the father of Abiud
Matthew1:14 Azor the father of Zadok
Matthew1:15 Eliud the father of Eleazar
Matthew1:16 Jacob was the father of Joseph
Matthew1:17 So there were 14 generations from Abraham to David
Matthew1:18 The birth of Jesus Christ took place in this way. His mother Mary had been promised to Joseph in marriage. But before they were married
Matthew1:19 Her husband Joseph was an honorable man and did not want to disgrace her publicly. So he decided to break the marriage agreement with her secretly.
Matthew1:20 Joseph had this in mind when an angel of the Lord appeared to him in a dream. The angel said to him
Matthew1:21 She will give birth to a son
Matthew1:22 All this happened so that what the Lord had spoken through the prophet came true:
Matthew1:23 The virgin will become pregnant and give birth to a son
Matthew1:24 When Joseph woke up
Matthew1:25 He did not have marital relations with her before she gave birth to a son. Joseph named the child Jesus.

Matthew2:2 They asked
Matthew2:3 When King Herod and all Jerusalem heard about this
Matthew2:4 He called together all the chief priests and scribes and tried to find out from them where the Messiah was supposed to be born.
Matthew2:5 They told him
Matthew2:6 Bethlehem in the land of Judah
Matthew2:7 Then Herod secretly called the wise men and found out from them exactly when the star had appeared.
Matthew2:8 As he sent them to Bethlehem
Matthew2:9 After they had heard the king
Matthew2:10 They were overwhelmed with joy to see the star.
Matthew2:11 When they entered the house
Matthew2:12 God warned them in a dream not to go back to Herod. So they left for their country by another road.
Matthew2:13 After they had left
Matthew2:14 Joseph got up
Matthew2:15 He stayed there until Herod died. What the Lord had spoken through the prophet came true: I have called my son out of Egypt.

Matthew2:18 A sound was heard in Ramah
Matthew2:19 After Herod was dead

Matthew2:21 Joseph got up
Matthew2:22 But when he heard that Archelaus had succeeded his father Herod as king of Judea
Matthew2:23 and made his home in a city called Nazareth. So what the prophets had said came true: He will be called a Nazarene.

Matthew4:3 The tempter came to him and said
Matthew4:4 Jesus answered

Matthew4:6 He said to Jesus
Matthew4:7 Jesus said to him

Matthew4:10 Jesus said to him
Matthew4:11 Then the devil left him

Matthew4:14 So what the prophet Isaiah had said came true:
Matthew4:15 Land of Zebulun and land of Naphtali
Matthew4:16 The people who lived in darkness have seen a bright light. A light has risen for those who live in a land overshadowed by death.

Matthew4:19 Jesus said to them

Matthew5:1 When Jesus saw the crowds
Matthew5:2 and he began to teach them:
Matthew5:3 Blessed are those who recognize they are spiritually helpless. The kingdom of heaven belongs to them.
Matthew5:4 Blessed are those who mourn. They will be comforted.
Matthew5:5 Blessed are those who are gentle. They will inherit the earth.
Matthew5:6 Blessed are those who hunger and thirst for God's approval. They will be satisfied.
Matthew5:7 Blessed are those who show mercy. They will be treated mercifully.
Matthew5:8 Blessed are those whose thoughts are pure. They will see God.
Matthew5:9 Blessed are those who make peace. They will be called God's children.
Matthew5:10 Blessed are those who are persecuted for doing what God approves of. The kingdom of heaven belongs to them.
Matthew5:11 Blessed are you when people insult you
Matthew5:12 Rejoice and be glad because you have a great reward in heaven! The prophets who lived before you were persecuted in these ways.
Matthew5:13 You are salt for the earth. But if salt loses its taste
Matthew5:14 You are light for the world. A city cannot be hidden when it is located on a hill.
Matthew5:15 No one lights a lamp and puts it under a basket. Instead
Matthew5:16 In the same way let your light shine in front of people. Then they will see the good that you do and praise your Father in heaven.
Matthew5:17 Don't ever think that I came to set aside Moses' Teachings or the Prophets. I didn't come to set them aside but to make them come true.
Matthew5:18 I can guarantee this truth: Until the earth and the heavens disappear
Matthew5:19 So whoever sets aside any command that seems unimportant and teaches others to do the same will be unimportant in the kingdom of heaven. But whoever does and teaches what the commands say will be called great in the kingdom of heaven.
Matthew5:20 I can guarantee that unless you live a life that has God's approval and do it more faithfully than the scribes and Pharisees
Matthew5:21 You have heard that it was said to your ancestors
Matthew5:22 But I can guarantee that whoever is angry with another believer will answer for it in court. Whoever calls another believer an insulting name will answer for it in the highest court. Whoever calls another believer a fool will answer for it in hellfire.
Matthew5:23 So if you are offering your gift at the altar and remember there that another believer has something against you
Matthew5:24 leave your gift at the altar. First go away and make peace with that person. Then come back and offer your gift.
Matthew5:25 Make peace quickly with your opponent while you are on the way to court with him. Otherwise
Matthew5:26 I can guarantee this truth: You will never get out until you pay every penny of your fine.
Matthew5:27 You have heard that it was said

Matthew10:39 The person who tries to preserve his life will lose it
Matthew10:40 The person who welcomes you welcomes me
Matthew10:41 The person who welcomes a prophet as a prophet will receive a prophet's reward. The person who welcomes a righteous person as a righteous person will receive a righteous person's reward.

Matthew27:58 He went to Pilate and asked for the body of Jesus. Pilate ordered that it be given to him.
Matthew27:59 Joseph took the body and wrapped it in a clean linen cloth.
