Colossians1:4 We thank God because we have heard about your faith in Christ Jesus and your love for all of God's people.
Colossians1:5 You have these because of the hope which is kept safe for you in heaven. Some time ago you heard about this hope in the Good News which is the message of truth.
Colossians1:6 This Good News is present with you now. It is producing results and spreading all over the world as it did among you from the first day you heard it. At that time you came to know what God's kindness truly means.
Colossians1:7 You learned about this Good News from Epaphras
Colossians1:8 and has told us about the love that the Spirit has given you.
Colossians1:9 For this reason we have not stopped praying for you since the day we heard about you. We ask {God} to fill you with the knowledge of his will through every kind of spiritual wisdom and insight.
Colossians1:10 We ask this so that you will live the kind of lives that prove you belong to the Lord. Then you will want to please him in every way as you grow in producing every kind of good work by this knowledge about God.
Colossians1:11 We ask him to strengthen you by his glorious might with all the power you need to patiently endure everything with joy.
Colossians1:12 You will also thank the Father
Colossians1:13 God has rescued us from the power of darkness and has brought us into the kingdom of his Son
Colossians1:14 His Son paid the price to free us
Colossians1:15 He is the image of the invisible God
Colossians1:16 He created all things in heaven and on earth
Colossians1:17 He existed before everything and holds everything together.
Colossians1:18 He is also the head of the church
Colossians1:19 God was pleased to have all of himself live in Christ.
Colossians1:20 God was also pleased to bring everything on earth and in heaven back to himself through Christ. He did this by making peace through Christ's blood sacrificed on the cross.
Colossians1:21 Once you were separated from God. The evil things you did showed your hostile attitude.
Colossians1:22 But now Christ has brought you back to God by dying in his physical body. He did this so that you could come into God's presence without sin
Colossians1:23 This is on the condition that you continue in faith without being moved from the solid foundation of the hope that the Good News contains. You've heard this Good News of which I
Colossians1:24 I am happy to suffer for you now. In my body I am completing whatever remains of Christ's sufferings. I am doing this on behalf of his body
Colossians1:25 I became a servant of the church when God gave me the work of telling you his entire message.
Colossians1:26 In the past God hid this mystery
Colossians1:27 God wanted his people throughout the world to know the glorious riches of this mystery—which is Christ living in you
Colossians1:28 We spread the message about Christ as we instruct and teach everyone with all the wisdom there is. We want to present everyone as mature Christian people.
Colossians1:29 I work hard and struggle to do this while his mighty power works in me.

Colossians2:9 All of God lives in Christ's body
Colossians2:10 and God has made you complete in Christ. Christ is in charge of every ruler and authority.
Colossians2:11 In him you were also circumcised. It was not a circumcision performed by human hands. But it was a removal of the corrupt nature in the circumcision performed by Christ.
Colossians2:12 This happened when you were placed in the tomb with Christ through baptism. In baptism you were also brought back to life with Christ through faith in the power of God
Colossians2:13 You were once dead because of your failures and your uncircumcised corrupt nature. But God made you alive with Christ when he forgave all our failures.
Colossians2:14 He did this by erasing the charges that were brought against us by the written laws God had established. He took the charges away by nailing them to the cross.
Colossians2:15 He stripped the rulers and authorities {of their power} and made a public spectacle of them as he celebrated his victory in Christ.
Colossians2:16 Therefore
Colossians2:17 These are a shadow of the things to come

Colossians2:19 He doesn't hold on to {Christ
Colossians2:20 If you have died with Christ to the world's way of doing things
Colossians2:21 People will tell you
Colossians2:22 All of these things deal with objects that are only used up anyway.
Colossians2:23 These things look like wisdom with their self-imposed worship

2Timothy1:1 From Paul
2Timothy1:2 To Timothy
2Timothy1:3 I constantly remember you in my prayers night and day when I thank God
2Timothy1:4 I remember your tears and want to see you so that I can be filled with happiness.
2Timothy1:5 I'm reminded of how sincere your faith is. That faith first lived in your grandmother Lois and your mother Eunice. I'm convinced that it also lives in you.
2Timothy1:6 You received a gift from God when I placed my hands on you {to ordain you}. Now I'm reminding you to fan that gift into flames.
2Timothy1:7 God didn't give us a cowardly spirit but a spirit of power
2Timothy1:8 So never be ashamed to tell others about our Lord or be ashamed of me
2Timothy1:9 God saved us and called us to be holy
2Timothy1:10 Now with the coming of our Savior Christ Jesus
2Timothy1:11 I was appointed to be a messenger of this Good News
2Timothy1:12 For this reason I suffer as I do. However
2Timothy1:13 With faith and love for Christ Jesus
2Timothy1:14 With the help of the Holy Spirit who lives in us
2Timothy1:15 You know that everyone in the province of Asia has deserted me
2Timothy1:16 May the Lord be merciful to the family of Onesiphorus. He often took care of my needs and wasn't ashamed that I was a prisoner.
2Timothy1:17 When he arrived in Rome
2Timothy1:18 May the Lord grant that Onesiphorus finds mercy when that day comes. You know very well that he did everything possible to help me in Ephesus.
2Timothy2:1 My child
2Timothy2:2 You've heard my message
2Timothy2:3 Join me in suffering like a good soldier of Christ Jesus.
2Timothy2:4 Whoever serves in the military doesn't get mixed up in non-military activities. This pleases his commanding officer.
2Timothy2:5 Whoever enters an athletic competition wins the prize only when playing by the rules.
2Timothy2:6 A hard-working farmer should have the first share of the crops.
2Timothy2:7 Understand what I'm saying. The Lord will help you understand all these things.
2Timothy2:8 Always think about Jesus Christ. He was brought back to life and is a descendant of David. This is the Good News that I tell others.
2Timothy2:9 I'm suffering disgrace for spreading this Good News. I have even been put into prison like a criminal. However
2Timothy2:10 For that reason
2Timothy2:11 This is a statement that can be trusted: If we have died with him
2Timothy2:12 If we endure
2Timothy2:13 If we are unfaithful
2Timothy2:14 Remind believers about these things
2Timothy2:15 Do your best to present yourself to God as a tried-and-true worker who isn't ashamed to teach the word of truth correctly.
2Timothy2:16 Avoid pointless discussions. People who {pay attention to these pointless discussions} will become more ungodly
2Timothy2:17 and what they say will spread like cancer. Hymenaeus and Philetus are like that.
2Timothy2:18 They have abandoned the truth. They are destroying the faith of others by saying that people who have died have already come back to life.
2Timothy2:19 In spite of all that
2Timothy2:20 In a large house there are not only objects made of gold and silver
2Timothy2:21 Those who stop associating with dishonorable people will be honored. They will be set apart for the master's use
2Timothy2:22 Stay away from lusts which tempt young people. Pursue what has God's approval. Pursue faith
2Timothy2:23 Don't have anything to do with foolish and stupid arguments. You know they cause quarrels.
2Timothy2:24 A servant of the Lord must not quarrel. Instead
2Timothy2:25 He must be gentle in correcting those who oppose the Good News. Maybe God will allow them to change the way they think and act and lead them to know the truth.
2Timothy2:26 Then they might come back to their senses and God will free them from the devil's snare so that they can do his will.
2Timothy3:1 You must understand this: In the last days there will be violent periods of time.
2Timothy3:2 People will be selfish and love money. They will brag
2Timothy3:3 and lack normal affection for their families. They will refuse to make peace with anyone. They will be slanderous
2Timothy3:4 They will be traitors. They will be reckless and conceited. They will love pleasure rather than God.
2Timothy3:5 They will appear to have a godly life
2Timothy3:6 Some of these men go into homes and mislead weak-minded women who are burdened with sins and led by all kinds of desires.
2Timothy3:7 These women are always studying but are never able to recognize the truth.
2Timothy3:8 As Jannes and Jambres opposed Moses
2Timothy3:9 Certainly
2Timothy3:10 But you know all about my teachings
2Timothy3:11 You also know about the kind of persecutions and sufferings which happened to me in the cities of Antioch
2Timothy3:12 Those who try to live a godly life because they believe in Christ Jesus will be persecuted.
2Timothy3:13 But evil people and phony preachers will go from bad to worse as they mislead people and are themselves misled.
2Timothy3:14 However
2Timothy3:15 From infancy you have known the Holy Scriptures. They have the power to give you wisdom so that you can be saved through faith in Christ Jesus.
2Timothy3:16 Every Scripture passage is inspired by God. All of them are useful for teaching
2Timothy3:17 They equip God's servants so that they are completely prepared to do good things.
2Timothy4:1 I solemnly call on you in the presence of God and Christ Jesus
2Timothy4:2 Be ready to spread the word whether or not the time is right. Point out errors
2Timothy4:3 A time will come when people will not listen to accurate teachings. Instead
2Timothy4:4 People will refuse to listen to the truth and turn to myths.
2Timothy4:5 But you must keep a clear head in everything. Endure suffering. Do the work of a missionary. Devote yourself completely to your work.
2Timothy4:6 My life is coming to an end
2Timothy4:7 I have fought the good fight. I have completed the race. I have kept the faith.
2Timothy4:8 The prize that shows I have God's approval is now waiting for me. The Lord
2Timothy4:9 Hurry to visit me soon.
2Timothy4:10 Demas has abandoned me. He fell in love with this present world and went to the city of Thessalonica. Crescens went to the province of Galatia
2Timothy4:11 Only Luke is with me. Get Mark and bring him with you. He is useful to me in my work.
2Timothy4:12 I'm sending Tychicus to the city of Ephesus as my representative.
2Timothy4:13 When you come
2Timothy4:14 Alexander the metalworker did me a great deal of harm. The Lord will pay him back for what he did.
2Timothy4:15 Watch out for him. He violently opposed what we said.
2Timothy4:16 At my first hearing no one stood up in my defense. Everyone abandoned me. I pray that it won't be held against them.
2Timothy4:17 However
2Timothy4:18 The Lord will rescue me from all harm and will take me safely to his heavenly kingdom. Glory belongs to him forever! Amen.
2Timothy4:19 Give my greetings to Prisca and Aquila and the family of Onesiphorus.
2Timothy4:20 Erastus stayed in the city of Corinth and I left Trophimus in the city of Miletus because he was sick.
2Timothy4:21 Hurry to visit me before winter comes. Eubulus
2Timothy4:22 The Lord be with you. His good will be with all of you.
Titus1:1 From Paul
Titus1:2 My message is based on the confidence of eternal life. God
Titus1:3 God has revealed this in every era by spreading his word. I was entrusted with this word by the command of God our Savior.
Titus1:4 To Titus
Titus1:5 I left you in Crete to do what still needed to be done—appointing spiritual leaders in every city as I directed you.
Titus1:6 A spiritual leader must have a good reputation. He must have only one wife and have children who are believers. His children shouldn't be known for having wild lifestyles or being rebellious.
Titus1:7 Because a bishop is a supervisor appointed by God
Titus1:8 Instead
Titus1:9 He must be devoted to the trustworthy message we teach. Then he can use these accurate teachings to encourage people and correct those who oppose the word.
Titus1:10 There are many believers
Titus1:11 They must be silenced because they are ruining whole families by teaching what they shouldn't teach. This is the shameful way they make money.
Titus1:12 Even one of their own prophets said
Titus1:13 That statement is true. For this reason
Titus1:14 They shouldn't pay attention to Jewish myths or commands given by people who are always rejecting the truth.
Titus1:15 Everything is clean to those who are clean. But nothing is clean to corrupt unbelievers. Indeed
Titus1:16 They claim to know God
Titus2:1 Tell believers to live the kind of life that goes along with accurate teachings.
Titus2:2 Tell older men to be sober. Tell them to be men of good character
Titus2:3 Tell older women to live their lives in a way that shows they are dedicated to God. Tell them not to be gossips or addicted to alcohol
Titus2:4 In this way they will teach young women to show love to their husbands and children
Titus2:5 to use good judgment
Titus2:6 Encourage young men to use good judgment.
Titus2:7 Always set an example by doing good things. When you teach
Titus2:8 Speak an accurate message that cannot be condemned. Then those who oppose us will be ashamed because they cannot say anything bad about us.
Titus2:9 Tell slaves who are believers to place themselves under their masters' authority in everything they do. Tell them to please their masters
Titus2:10 or steal from them. Instead
Titus2:11 After all
Titus2:12 It trains us to avoid ungodly lives filled with worldly desires so that we can live self-controlled
Titus2:13 At the same time we can expect what we hope for—the appearance of the glory of our great God and Savior
Titus2:14 He gave himself for us to set us free from every sin and to cleanse us so that we can be his special people who are enthusiastic about doing good things.
Titus2:15 Tell these things to the believers. Encourage and correct them
Titus3:1 Remind believers to willingly place themselves under the authority of government officials. Believers should obey them and be ready to help them with every good thing they do.
Titus3:2 Believers shouldn't curse anyone or be quarrelsome
Titus3:3 Indeed
Titus3:4 However
Titus3:5 he saved us
Titus3:6 God poured a generous amount of the Spirit on us through Jesus Christ our Savior.
Titus3:7 As a result
Titus3:8 This is a statement that can be trusted. I want you to insist on these things so that those who believe in God can concentrate on setting an example by doing good things. This is good and helps other people.
Titus3:9 Avoid foolish controversies
Titus3:10 Have nothing to do with people who continue to teach false doctrine after you have warned them once or twice.
Titus3:11 You know that people like this are corrupt. They are sinners condemned by their own actions.
Titus3:12 When I send Artemas or Tychicus to you
Titus3:13 Give Zenas the lawyer and Apollos your best support for their trip so that they will have everything they need.
Titus3:14 Our people should also learn how to set an example by doing good things when urgent needs arise so that they can live productive lives.
Titus3:15 Everyone with me sends you greetings. Greet our faithful friends. {God's} good will be with all of you.

Hebrews1:2 In these last days he has spoken to us through his Son. God made his Son responsible for everything. His Son is the one through whom God made the universe.
Hebrews1:3 His Son is the reflection of God's glory and the exact likeness of God's being. He holds everything together through his powerful words. After he had cleansed people from their sins
Hebrews1:4 The Son has become greater than the angels since he has been given a name that is superior to theirs.

Hebrews1:13 But God never said to any of the angels
Hebrews1:14 What are all the angels? They are spirits sent to serve those who are going to receive salvation.

Hebrews4:9 Therefore
Hebrews4:10 Those who entered his place of rest also rested from their work as God did from his.

Hebrews4:13 No creature can hide from God. Everything is uncovered and exposed for him to see. We must answer to him.
Hebrews4:14 We need to hold on to our declaration of faith: We have a superior chief priest who has gone through the heavens. That person is Jesus
Hebrews4:15 We have a chief priest who is able to sympathize with our weaknesses. He was tempted in every way that we are
Hebrews4:16 So we can go confidently to the throne of God's kindness to receive mercy and find kindness

Hebrews7:7 No one can deny that the more important person blesses the less important person.

Hebrews7:9 We could even say that when Abraham gave Melchizedek a tenth of everything

Hebrews7:25 That is why he is always able to save those who come to God through him. He can do this because he always lives and intercedes for them.

Hebrews9:1 The first promise had rules for the priests' service. It also had a holy place on earth.
Hebrews9:2 A tent was set up. The first part of this tent was called the holy place. The lamp stand
Hebrews9:3 Behind the second curtain was the part of the tent called the most holy place.
Hebrews9:4 It contained the gold incense burner and the ark of the Lord's promise. The ark was completely covered with gold. In the ark were the gold jar filled with manna
Hebrews9:5 Above the ark were the angels of glory {with their wings} overshadowing the throne of mercy. (Discussing these things in detail isn't possible now.)
Hebrews9:6 That is how these two parts of the tent were set up. The priests always went into the first part of the tent to perform their duties.
Hebrews9:7 But only the chief priest went into the second part of the tent. Once a year he entered and brought blood that he offered for himself and for the things that the people did wrong unintentionally.
Hebrews9:8 The Holy Spirit used this to show that the way into the most holy place was not open while the tent was still in use.
Hebrews9:9 The first part of the tent is an example for the present time. The gifts and sacrifices that were brought there could not give the worshiper a clear conscience.
Hebrews9:10 These gifts and sacrifices were meant to be food
Hebrews9:11 But Christ came as a chief priest of the good things that are now here. Christ went through a better

Hebrews9:17 A will is used only after a person is dead because it goes into effect only when a person dies.
Hebrews9:18 That is why even the first promise was made with blood.
